Ensuring a leakage-safe evaluation protocol, the experiments were conducted using subjectwise grouped cross-validation with foldwise feature selection on the noninvasive fetal ECG arrhythmia database available from PhysioNet. The proposed framework achieves robust arrhythmia detection, attaining 91.2% accuracy and 95.0% area under the curve across unseen subjects. To quantify systematic bias and agreement across evaluation stages, Bland–Altman analysis is employed to provide clinical interpretability beyond aggregate scores. Overall, the results demonstrate that convergence-aware Lévy-based optimization improves ANC performance for fECG analysis, while acknowledging dataset scale and offline evaluation limitations.

title: What + IF = IEEE
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Aerospace and Electronic Systems Magazine

title: MG-YOLO: A Lightweight Deep Learning Model for Detecting Blueberry Fruits at Different Maturities
authors: Qin Ling, Cui Kai, Wen Zelong, Zhang Wenyuan
abstract: The yield and quality of blueberry cultivation depend heavily on effective fruit harvesting. Accurate and real-time identification of blueberries at various maturity stages is a prerequisite for precise harvesting. Accordingly, a lightweight network named MG-YOLO is developed for blueberry fruit detection in this study, through multiple enhancements on YOLO11-nano. First, the GBC block integrating gated convolutions and Bottleneck convolutions is adopted to improve adaptive feature discrimination ability, especially for densely occluded blueberries. Second, a novel MG-C3k2 module incorporating multi-scale Ghost convolution is proposed to strengthen feature representation while maintaining model efficiency. Finally, IF-CIoU loss, focusing on inner overlapping areas of bounding boxes and the balance between hard and easy samples, is designed to mitigate the class imbalance issue and accelerate bounding box regression, improving localisation precision. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed MG-YOLO attains 85.7% mAP@0.5 and 60.6% mAP@0.5:0.95, representing remarkable improvements of 4.0% and 5.1% over the baseline, respectively. Furthermore, the parameter count is reduced by 12%, and the model exhibits a detection speed of 51 FPS measured on an NVIDIA RTX 3060 GPU, indicating its potential for for real-time automated harvesting applications.

title: Maximum Entropy Inverse RL for Cyber Attack Detection in IoT Networks
authors: Mohamed Massaoudi, Maymouna Ez Eddin
abstract: The proliferation of Internet of Things (IoT) devices has significantly expanded the attack surface for cyber threats, making effective intrusion detection systems (IDS) critical for network security. Traditional anomaly detection methods often struggle with high-dimensional network traffic data and evolving attack patterns. This paper presents a novel approach for cyber attack detection using Maximum Entropy Inverse Reinforcement Learning (MaxEnt IRL). The proposed method learns reward functions from normal network traffic patterns, enabling the identification of anomalous behaviors that deviate from expected normal operations. By leveraging the maximum entropy principle, the approach captures the underlying distribution of normal traffic states without requiring labeled attack samples during training. The study evaluates the performance with multiple attack types, including Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS), Address Resolution Protocol (ARP) poisoning, and Message Queue Telemetry Transport (MQTT) based attacks. Comprehensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed method achieves 91.18% accuracy, outperforming traditional machine learning approaches, deep learning methods, and other RL attack detection techniques. The results highlight the potential of MaxEnt IRL for robust and adaptive cyber attack detection in IoT environments. All the related codes and datasets publicly available at: https://github.com/Mmassaoudi-cell/maxent-irl-iot-ids.

title: Energy-saving Optimization Control of PEMFC systems for Plateau Railway
authors: Shuqi Xie, Qi Li, Liangzhen Yin, Leshan He, Weirong Chen
abstract: The harsh environment and high-power and high dynamic traction load along the plateau railway led to severe energy loss, limited output capacity and even stack halt of proton exchange membrane fuel cell (PEMFC) systems. To this end, an energy-saving optimization control (ESOC) method is proposed to simultaneously enhance energy recovery and output capacity of PEMFC systems. Based on the close energy interaction between the system and environment, a plateau PEMFC system model is established, and an expander is integrated to achieve the conversion of gas waste heat energy. The contradictory characteristics between the energy recovery capacity and output capacity of the system in the plateau environment are analyzed. Because maximum net power and maximum energy recovery cannot be achieved at the same oxygen excess ratio (OER), a dual-objective optimization method is proposed to determine the optimal OER while strictly adhering to safety constraints. To realize rapid response under complex traction loads, the OER is dynamically adjusted through an active disturbance rejection controller. The comparative experiments on the hardware-in-the loop platform show that the proposed ESOC method can effectively ensure the stable operation of PEMFC systems in plateau areas, and achieve energy recovery and output capacity improvement.

title: Core Values for Ethical AI: A Systematic Review of Frameworks, Contexts, and Applications
authors: Hannah Louise Mulvihill, Taís Fernanda Blauth, Oskar Josef Gstrein, Andrej Zwitter
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) systems increasingly support decisions with significant ethical and societal implications, necessitating systematic approaches to value-aware design and governance. However, it remains unclear which normative values are most consistently emphasized in AI design research and how these priorities vary across domains and research objectives. This study presents a systematic literature review of 46 peer-reviewed publications identified through six major scholarly databases. The analysis distinguishes theoretical and applied contributions and examines Value Sensitive Design (VSD) alongside related value-protection frameworks to identify recurrent value patterns. Results reveal a convergent core set of values including privacy, fairness/equality, transparency/explicability, accountability/responsibility, and autonomy, that dominate the discourse. In contrast, interpretability, safety, and reliability receive comparatively limited attention, while collective goods and ecological sustainability are rarely addressed. Domain-specific differences are evident: safety-critical and embodied systems prioritize safety and interpretability; data-intensive public sector applications emphasize fairness and privacy; and industrial contexts foreground security, control, and responsibility. Furthermore, framework-oriented studies tend to emphasize procedural values, whereas empirical case studies focus on domain-specific risks such as safety and reliability. Across the corpus, a persistent gap is identified between articulated value commitments and mechanisms for post-deployment verification of value realization. Based on the synthesis of 21 recurrent values, this review derives nine illustrative system-level requirements intended to support the design, evaluation, and governance of value-aware AI systems.

title: Performance, Adaptability, and Security in AI-Driven IIoT: A Survey
authors: Uddin Md. Borhan, Arif Raza, Taki Uddin, Mahbubul Islam, A.K.M. Muzahidul Islam, Jie Chen
abstract: The Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) integrates cyber-physical systems, edge-cloud computing, Artificial Intelligence (AI), and advanced connectivity to enable intelligent, data-driven operations across manufacturing, energy, transportation, and healthcare. The convergence of Operational Technology (OT) and Information Technology (IT), together with pervasive AI-driven automation, complicates meeting the joint requirements of performance, adaptability, and security (PAS). This survey adopts an explicitly AI-centric view of PAS and reviews IIoT research spanning deep reinforcement learning (DRL), multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL), digital twins (DTs), federated and collaborative learning, graph neural networks (GNNs), blockchain-assisted analytics, and lightweight and post-quantum cryptography. The reviewed approaches are analyzed across device, edge, fog, cloud, and application layers under industrial constraints such as latency, reliability, resource limits, safety requirements, and regulatory compliance. A PAS-centric taxonomy is introduced that links technique families to deployment layers and application domains, and recurring trade-offs among PAS dimensions are summarized. Open challenges are identified in multi-objective optimization, semantic interoperability, lifecycle validation, and governance, with directions outlined toward secure and sustainable Industry 5.0 IIoT ecosystems.

title: Comprehensive Analysis of Induction Motor Harmonics for Sensorless Speed Estimation in its Application to MCSA
authors: Jorge Bonet-Jara, Joan Pons-Llinares
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: In recent years, companies have been implementing smart sensors for autonomous asset monitoring. In this context, Motor Current Signature Analysis (MCSA) is ideal to monitor induction motors due to its non-invasive and remote measurement capability. An autonomous MCSA algorithm requires precise, automatic and non-invasive knowledge of the motor speed to accurately locate the speed-dependent fault harmonics. Regarding this, harmonic-based Sensorless Speed Estimation (SSE) methods are the most compatible with MCSA, since they are based on the same principles. Among them, Mixed Eccentricity Harmonics (MEHs) are the most commonly used, due to their non-invasive implementation. However, recent studies have also shown the non-invasiveness of Rotor Slot Harmonics (RSHs) techniques. Therefore, the potential industrial use of both types of harmonics makes it necessary to determine which are more reliable for an autonomous MCSA algorithm. Hence, this paper aims to definitely answer this question by conducting an in-depth analysis of RSHs- and MEHs-based techniques attending to the most critical features of these methods: the detectability and accuracy of the harmonic used. For that, several related aspects are studied using three databases containing respectively: construction data of 3643 motors, 100 industrial measurements of 100 different motors, and 135 signals generated in a test-bench. The variety and extension of the databases allows for solid and general conclusions to be drawn that are applicable to industry.

title: F-POI: A Lightweight, Noise-Resilient Parallel Side-Channel Attack on Kyber
authors: Peng Chen, Laifan Pei, Shaoqing Guo, Chi Cheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: Plaintext-checking (PC) oracle-based side-channel attacks represent a well-established category of methods, recognized for their broad applicability and operational efficiency. At TCHES 2023, Rajendran et al. and Tanaka et al. independently introduced the concept of a parallel PC oracle, which significantly lowers the number of traces required for successful key recovery. However, Rajendran et al.’s approach, though lightweight, is highly sensitive to noise, while the method proposed by Tanaka et al. depends on a resource-intensive TensorFlow framework. In this work, we present an F-score based points of interest (F-POI) selection method and a plug-in P-parallel distinguisher architecture. This architecture, which combines a feature extractor and a classifier, resolves the trade-off between noise robustness and computational overhead. The feature extractor replaces recursive T-tests with a single 2P -class F-test, eliminating cumulative errors. The classifier provides flexible backend options, including Euclidean distance and various machine learning models, to optimize the balance among speed, accuracy, and deployment overhead. Experiments on an STM32F407G running pqm4-Kyber-512 show that our approach enables full key recovery with an unprecedentedly low error rate. Our Top-accuracy variant (using scikit-learn, ≈300 MB) achieves this with only 576 traces, resulting in just 0.08 incorrect coefficients (out of 512). Our Lightweight variant (Numpy-only, ≈50 MB) also significantly outperforms prior recursive methods across all tested parallelism levels. Crucially, this performance is achieved without the TensorFlow stack (≈2.1 GB), offering a practical and scalable alternative for security evaluation.

title: A Neuromorphic Image Sensor with In-Sensor Spiking Neural Network Computing
authors: Jung-Hye Hwang, Jong-Beom Kim, Jubin Kang, Kieop Hong, Heein Yoon, Seong-Jin Kim
publish: IEEE Journal on Emerging and Selected Topics in Circuits and Systems
abstract: This paper presents a neuromorphic image sensor (NIS) that integrates in-sensor spiking neural network (SNN) computation combined with a single-photon avalanche diode (SPAD) array. The proposed NIS directly performs spike-domain convolution on integrate-and-fire (IF) neurons integrated into the sensor, eliminating the need for conventional multi-bit analog-to-digital conversion (ADC). A two-step IF computing scheme hierarchically partitions the processing into pixel-level temporal integration and column-level spatial accumulation, enabling efficient spike-based feature extraction with minimal data movement. The pixel circuit employs analog-counter-based convolution and a switched-capacitor IF neuron, achieving low power consumption and a compact implementation while maintaining a high signal-to-noise ratio (SNR). Fabricated in a 110-nm BSI process, the prototype sensor with a 64×64 SPAD pixel array conducts first-layer SNN processing in an ADC-less hardware platform. The proposed NIS achieves a frame rate of 1,525 fps with five time steps and six convolution filters, achieving 95.9% classification accuracy on the MNIST dataset. The measured power consumption is 1.43 mW, corresponding to a figure of merit (FoM) of 7.63 pJ/pix·frame·filter and computing efficiency of up to 1.27 TOPS/W. These results verify the feasibility of energy-efficient, high-speed, and ADC-free spike-domain vision processing for future edge and event-driven AI applications.

title: Motion analysis of a multi-stiffness concentric tube magnetic continuum robot and its remote constraint application for minimally invasive surgical operations_supp2-3699226.mp4
authors: Wenguang Yang
abstract: Conventional magnetic continuum robots (MCRs) show great potential for minimally invasive surgery due to their simple structure and ease of manipulation. However, their monolithic continuous structure and single-stiffness design limit kinematic performance and manipulation accuracy. To address these limitations, we developed a concentric tube magnetic continuum robot (CT-MCR) system with replaceable concentric tubes and adjustable magnetic particle positions. Motion analysis and modeling were performed using the Cosserat rod model to optimize structural characteristics and enhance manipulation accuracy. Within a 270° driving range, the operating end-effector achieves stable trajectory following with a fit rate exceeding 90%. The distal constraint effect minimizes end-effector hopping, improving stability by over 50% compared to conventional MCRs. Navigation and foreign body retrieval experiments in a complex branching vascular model within a 3D Helmholtz coil workspace demonstrate the CT-MCR’s potential for targeted drug delivery and minimally invasive cardiovascular surgery.

title: Trans-Intestinal Port for Periodic Drug Refilling: Bridging Ingestible Capsules and Implantable Devices_supp7-3699210.mp4
authors: Leonardo Ricotti
abstract: Implantable devices for controlled drug release are extremely promising for the treatment of chronic diseases, such as type 1 diabetes, chronic pain, and others. However, these systems still require periodic invasive refills, which can result in clinical complications and patient discomfort. Previous works introduced the opportunity for a non-invasive refilling of these devices through ingestible capsules. However, concerns remain regarding repeated tissue perforation of the intestinal wall and the corresponding risk of fistula formation. This study presents a novel trans-intestinal port system enabling non-invasive refilling of implanted devices designed to create a stable and controlled interface between the device and the intestinal lumen. The system aims to avoid tissue damage due to repeated needle insertions, ensure proper alignment between the device and capsule, and prevent leakage of digestive fluids into the peritoneal cavity. The design and fabrication of this trans-intestinal port are presented, together with the results of bench tests and a preliminary short-term in vivo test. These findings highlight the potential of the proposed trans-intestinal port as an innovative solution to enable a non-invasive periodic refilling of implantable drug delivery systems, paving the way to a full validation of this technology in the future, which will require long-term in vivo experiments.

title: Petra: Enhancing Efficiency in Privacy-Preserving Three-Party Relational Analytics for Private Data
authors: Ziwei Peng, Kaiping Xue, Jingcheng Zhao, Jinjiang Yang, Yingjie Xue
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: As organizations increasingly rely on collaborative relational analytics over private databases, secure multi-party computation (MPC) has emerged as a key solution to process private data. While numerous schemes optimize many MPC-based algorithms for complex relational queries, the basic MPC operations underlying relational computation, such as comparisons, still cost high communication overhead and latency. This imposes a significant bottleneck. To further enhance practicality and efficiency, we aim to reduce the overhead of using MPC, enabling more efficient computation of relational operators and query execution. We propose Petra, a three-party model solution for privacy-preserving relational analytics. Petra introduces new primitives of efficient equality and inequality operations designed for databases. These primitives leverage a pseudorandom transformation and a “shuffle-and-compare” working pattern. Then, by composing these primitives and designing relational operator protocols including SELECT, aggregation, Join, and Group-by, Petra enables efficient execution of SQL operators. Importantly, Petra provides different security guarantees compared to typical MPC protocols, as it allows a limited form of leakage to one of the computing parties. This trade-off is central to Petra's design. Experimental results demonstrate that Petra significantly improves query performance compared to existing approaches and scales to complex analytics over large databases.

title: Cross-Behavior Learning with Object Flow Prediction for Robotic Manipulation
authors: Longrui Chen, David Russell, Yulei Qiu, Mehmet Dogar
abstract: Cross-embodiment learning enables robots to acquire manipulation skills by learning from demonstrations provided by different embodiments. However, most existing research on cross-embodiment learning focuses on transferring similar manipulation behaviors. For the same task, different embodiments may need different behaviors; e.g., it may be easier for a human to push an object to a goal position, while a robot may use a pick-and-place to perform the same task. Making use of such cross-embodiment and cross-behavior demonstrations becomes essential for large-scale imitation learning. In this work, we propose a novel framework for cross-behavior learning based on object flow. Object flow represents the task in a manner that is independent from the embodiment and the particular behavior used during the demonstration. By shifting the focus from the manipulator to objects, our framework enables learning from cross-behavior manipulation data rather than merely imitating the behavior of a specific embodiment. Our results on task representations show that, with only 20 robot demonstrations, integrating object flow prediction improves success rates by up to 91% in-domain and 87% out-of-domain. Adding 40 human demonstrations in addition to the 20 robot demonstrations further boosts out-of-domain performance by 143%.

title: A Soft Worm Robot for Sub-Centimeter Scale Pipeline Inspection_supp8-3699247.mp4
authors: Yitong Zhou
abstract: Inspection of sub-centimeter pipelines with continuous spatial curvatures remains challenging due to extreme geo metric confinement. This paper presents an ultralight, trunkless worm-inspired robot actuated by a single twisted and coiled actuator (TCA) specifically optimized for short-range inspection within localized extreme spatial bottlenecks. By integrating actuation and structural support into a single TCA and eliminating rigid trunks and auxiliary recovery mechanisms, the robot achieves a compact and lightweight design, with a mass of 0.5–1.1 g, a length of 32 mm, and a diameter of 4–10 mm. Directional peristaltic locomotion is enabled by a wedge-based micro silicone frustum foot that provides passive frictional anisotropy without complex control. A simplified mechanics-based anchoring model captures the dominant directional friction asymmetry induced by foot geometry and guides parameter optimization. Experiments demonstrate a maximum friction anisotropy ratio of 4.75, enabling stable crawling at 0.40 mm/s horizontally and 0.13 mm/s vertically in 4–9 mm pipelines, including complex three dimensional curved configurations. Blind inspection experiments with an onboard micro-camera further validate the robot's potential for inspection in highly confined pipeline environments.

title: HMFM: A Robust Hierarchical Multimodal Fusion Module to Enhance Map Learning With Additional Information
authors: Wenjie Gao, Entao Chang, Jiawei Fu, Shitao Chen, Nanning Zheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: High-definition (HD) maps, rich in geometric and semantic information, are essential for autonomous driving systems. Recent online map learning methods, predicting maps directly from onboard sensor data, offer promising alternatives to traditional manual approaches. However, they are limited in long-range detection and complex environments due to onboard sensors’ inherent constraints. While some works incorporate external priors, they often lack generality due to tight coupling with specific architectures and poor robustness to imperfect data. To address these gaps, we propose the Hierarchical Multimodal Fusion Module (HMFM), a robust, generalizable plug-and-play module that fuses onboard data with external information at two levels. At the feature level, the Masked Attention Module (MAM) selectively enhances features to boost fusion efficiency and minimize interference. At the Bird’s Eye View (BEV) level, the Alignment Module (AM) resolves spatial discrepancies; then, the Unified Attention Module (UAM) adaptively weighs external priors to effectively mitigate inaccuracies from imperfect data. We also introduce a distillation framework to distill benefits from additional information into the model without affecting runtime efficiency. Extensive experiments validate HMFM’s generality, leveraging satellite and SD maps for significant gains across baselines (e.g., +22.0 mIoU on HDMapNet, +12.3 mAP on MapTR). Furthermore, the distillation framework boosts the original HDMapNet by 2.8 mIoU without affecting runtime efficiency. The code and data processing pipeline for this work are publicly available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/xjtu-cs-gao/HMFM</uri>

title: Temporal Irregularity-Aware Attention Mechanism for Industry 5.0 Intrusion Detection
authors: Danish Javeed, Muhammad Adil, Prabhat Kumar, Hongbo Liu
abstract: The evolution of Industry 5.0 introduces complex, temporally irregular network behaviors, challenging intrusion detection in heterogeneous industrial environments. Existing Intrusion Detection Systems (IDSs) often struggle with asynchronous traffic and lack interpretability. To overcome these limitations, we propose AegisNet, a novel sequence-based IDS that reformulates intrusion detection as a sequence classification task on fixed-length windows of T consecutive observations (sequence length), augmented with explicit time-delta features to capture irregular traffic intervals. AegisNet introduces a novel Temporal-Aware and Self-Adaptive Multi-Head Attention (TA-SAMHA) mechanism. It introduces learnable temporal bias projections to directly encode irregular time intervals into the attention process, enabling the system to capture fine-grained temporal dependencies and evolving behaviors. It also employs a Dual-Gate Residual Encoder (DGRE) for bidirectional sequence modeling with dual gating, enabling enriched contextual representations and stable learning across deep temporal layers. Additionally, we introduce the IDS-specific adaptation of Temporal SHAP (T-SHAP), enabling time-resolved feature attributions that expose how influential factors shift during sequential intrusion detection, offering a new dimension of temporally aware explainability for evolving attack patterns. Experimental results demonstrate that AegisNet achieves a detection accuracy of 99.63% on the CICIoV2024 dataset and 99.13% on the CICIoT2023 dataset, demonstrating its effectiveness for securing Industrial environments.

title: IoT-Enabled SnO₂-Based Humidity Sensor for Real-Time Monitoring in Neonatal Incubators
authors: Poundoss Chellamuthu, Kirubaveni Savarimuthu, Gulam Nabi Alsath Mohammed, Biancaa Ramesh
abstract: This research presents the development and optimization of a Tin Oxide (SnO₂)-based resistive humidity sensor for accurate monitoring in incubator systems. SnO₂ nanomaterials were synthesized using hydrothermal synthesis technique, followed by comprehensive characterization through X-ray diffraction (XRD), field emission scanning electron microscopy (FESEM), current-voltage (J-V) analysis, ultravioletvisible (UV-Vis) spectroscopy, and electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS). The best-performing sensor exhibited a flower-like nanostructure with a response time of 18 s, a recovery time of 14 s, and a sensitivity of 72.4%. The sensor functioned effectively within the required incubator humidity range of 40–60% relative humidity (RH) and demonstrated superior performance compared to the commercial DHT11 sensor. To ensure accuracy and reliability, machine learning algorithms were applied to estimate error and validate the correctness of the sensed humidity data. The optimized SnO₂ sensor was integrated into an incubator system, with real-time data transmission enabled through an Internet of Things (IoT) platform. These findings highlight the potential of SnO₂-based sensors for high-precision humidity monitoring in biomedical applications, particularly for neonatal care where stable humidity levels are critical.

title: A Continuous Finite-Time Tracking Controller for Nonholonomic-Unicycle Vehicles
authors: Angel I. Paredes, Emmanuel Cruz-Zavala, Antonio Loría, Emmanuel Nuño
abstract: This letter proposes a tracking controller for first-order nonholonomic-unicycle vehicles that guarantees stabilization in finite time. Besides the perks of finite-time stabilization (such as robustness), a significant advantage of our controller is its mathematical simplicity; while existing finite-time controllers often rely on discontinuous switching terms ours is continuous. This is important since the robot is velocity-controlled. Furthermore, in contrast to other controllers achieving finite-time stabilization ours relies only on the knowledge of the desired reference velocities and the system maintains stable in the presence of unknown, non-zero reference accelerations. We establish uniform global finite-time stability (UGFTS) via Lyapunov theory and illustrate our theoretical findings with numerical simulations.

title: Wavelength Dependence and Transient Photoresponse Mechanisms in Aerosol-Jet Printed ZnO Nanowire Ultraviolet Photodetectors
authors: Chenkun Lyu, Haoyi Lin, Joseph Andrews
abstract: Ultraviolet (UV) radiation poses significant health risks, motivating the development of reliable UV sensing systems. This paper presents an aerosol-jet-printed ZnO nanowire UV photodetector fabricated on a Si/SiO₂ substrate using printed interdigitated Ag electrodes and a ZnO nanowire sensing layer. The device exhibits strong wavelength- and intensity-dependent photoresponse under 340 nm and 375 nm UV illumination at a 1 V bias. At the same irradiance of 60 μW·cm⁻², the photocurrent reached ∼0.9 mA at 340 nm and ∼0.015 mA at 375 nm, corresponding to responsivities of 31.71 A/W and 3.67 A/W, respectively. Transient measurements reveal pronounced persistent photoconductivity (PPC) and strong dependence of recovery behavior on illumination conditions. In addition, thermal cycling experiments and a 10-nm Al₂O₃ atomic layer deposition (ALD) encapsulation layer were used to investigate the role of oxygen-related surface processes in carrier transport and recovery dynamics. These results provide insight into the transient photoresponse mechanisms of ZnO nanowire UV photodetectors and the influence of surface interactions on device behavior.

title: AI-Augmented Healthcare Communication: A Review of Large Language Model Healthcare Agents and Generative AI in EHR Integration
authors: Shivam Tyagi, Amit Kumar Goyal, Deepti Sharma
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Generative AI (GenAI) and large language models (LLMs) are among artificial intelligence (AI) innovations that have reshaped the concept of healthcare communication. Such technologies are being used to facilitate both synchronous and asynchronous communications: LLCM-based healthcare agents making structured consultations with patients, and GenAI-generated drafts helping clinicians respond to electronic health records (EHR) messages. The current review brings together recent research literature on these two paradigms that are complementary with regard to their effectiveness in clinical work, implications on workflow, and dilemmas related to them. An experimental and randomized research shows that healthcare agents significantly improve the inquiry proactivity, diagnostic relevance, and communication safety over baseline LLMs, whereas GenAI drafts augment reply empathy and length, but less frequently at the expense of increased review time. A comparative study shows that neither of the two paradigms can fully address issues of physician workload, personalization and medico-legal accountability. Rather, an intermediate system between patient-facing actors and physician-facing EHR draft can provide a moderate solution. Other challenges that are open to review in the paper are the personalization of physician style, integration of multi-modal data, and adherence to privacy rules including HIPAA, and GDPR. We conclude by a research roadmap highlighting the aspects of hybrid integration, personalization, and stringent assessment schemes of AI-assisted healthcare communication.

title: Cpp2Rust: Automatic Translation of C++ to Safe Rust
authors: Lucian Popescu, Francisco Gouveia, Henrique Preto, João Silveira, Dmytro Hrybenko, José Fragoso Santos, Nuno P. Lopes
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Programming Languages
abstract: None

title: Contextual Embeddings: Implementing Bound Variables through Instance Resolution
authors: Samantha Frohlich, Jessica Foster, G. A. Kavvos, Meng Wang
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Programming Languages
abstract: None

title: Online Coreset Selection for Learning Dynamic Systems
authors: Jingyuan Li, Dawei Shi, Ling Shi
abstract: With the increasing availability of streaming data in dynamic systems, a critical challenge in data-driven modeling for control is how to efficiently select informative data to characterize system dynamics. In this work, we develop an online coreset selection method for set-membership identification in the presence of process disturbances, improving data efficiency while preserving convergence guarantees. Specifically, we derive a stacked polyhedral representation that over-approximates the feasible parameter set. Based on this representation, we propose a geometric selection criterion that retains a data point only if it induces a sufficient contraction of the feasible set. Theoretically, the feasible-set volume is shown to converge to zero almost surely under persistently exciting data and a tight disturbance bound. When the disturbance bound is mismatched, an explicit Hausdorff-distance bound is derived to quantify the resulting identification error. In addition, an upper bound on the expected coreset size is established and extensions to bounded measurement noise and nonlinear systems are discussed. The effectiveness of the proposed method is demonstrated through comprehensive simulation studies.

title: A Critical Comparative Survey of ML/DL Approaches for Late Blight Detection and Prediction for Guinean Agriculture
authors: Mohamed Diallo, Demba Faye, Idy Diop, Alie Ndiaye, Salifou Touré
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Late blight (Phytophthora infestans) remains one of the most devastating diseases for staple crops. This survey benchmarks CNN (AlexNet, VGG-16, ResNet-50, DenseNet) and RNN architectures (LSTM, GRU) and hybrid multimodal frameworks. Our analysis reveals that ResNet-50 offers the best accuracy-to-parameter trade-off for image detection (90-99 %), while LSTM outperforms GRU for long-term temporal dependencies in disease risk prediction. We identify four structural limitations: over-reliance on the PlantVillage benchmark, near-total absence of West African training data (the rare African studies (Tanzania, Cameroon) confirm a $\mathbf{3 2 \%} \mathbf{F 1}$-score drop in real-world conditions), neglect of deployment constraints for resource-limited environments, and insufficient integration of image and climate modalities. Based on this analysis, we propose a hybrid CNN-LSTM reference architecture and formulate concrete recommendations for its adaptation to the Guinean agricultural context.

title: Enhancing Small and Medium Sized Enterprises Sustainability: Material Flow Cost Accounting as an Enabler for Circular Economy Thinking
authors: Rahajeng Simpony Santoso, Ang Swat Lin Lindawati, Agustinus Winoto
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This study investigates the influence of Material Flow Cost Accounting (MFCA) and Green Accounting on circular economy practices among small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) in West Jakarta, Indonesia. Data were collected from 410 SME owners and managers through a structured questionnaire and analyzed using the Smart- PLS technique. The results reveal that both MFCA and Green Accounting exert positive and significant effects on SMEs’ adoption of circular economy principles. The integration of these two accounting approaches enhances operational efficiency, reduces production waste, and strengthens environmental accountability. The study contributes to sustainability accounting literature by simultaneously testing MFCA and Green Accounting within the SME context-an area that remains underexplored in developing economies. Practically, the findings provide insights for SMEs, policymakers, and academics in promoting resource-efficient and environmentally responsible business practices

title: Revisiting Deepfake Image Detection: An Analysis of Cross-Dataset Generalization Limits
authors: Charri Meriem, Zaydi Hayat, Assoul Saliha
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The rapid development of Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) has enabled very realistic photorealistic faces to easily be generated in the manipulations, which are widely known as deepfakes, thereby creating significant obstacles for digital forensics to confirm media authenticity. Although high accuracy has been reported by the latest deep learning solutions on standardized evaluation datasets, their robustness to diverse data distributions remains to be challenging tasks for now. The study considers an analysis on the crossdataset evaluation performance on models designed for general image detection, especially focusing on deepfake images, and two models are evaluated in this study because they are very representative in deepfake detection tasks, one is the generic Convolutional Neural Network with ResNet structure, and the other one is designed to detect the artifacts in the images generated by GANs in digital manipulations, which is specifically referred to as the GAN-CNN model. The two models are evaluated in this work based on two publicly accessible deepfake imagery datasets based on intra- and crossdataset testing protocols, according to which significant performance loss has been found in models when they are evaluated in datasets that were not yet seen in the testing tasks, varying to some degrees according to the datasets in question regarding the deepfake detection performance loss due to domain gap, whereas the performance loss can be lessened with the use of manipulation-focused representations by observing the cross-dataset performance improvement based on detection accuracy in the study on the two models.

title: The effects of climate change on the spread of the common bean diseases
authors: Juvénal Havyarimana, Demba Faye, Messikely Anoman, Doudou Dione, Idy Diop
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Common bean (Phaseolus vulgaris) is extensively cultivated across Africa and Latin America, particularly in regions of high and medium altitudes. It represents a major staple food crop in developing countries, owing to its substantial nutritional and agronomic value. Despite its importance, the crop is highly susceptible to both biotic and abiotic stresses, which adversely affect plant growth and overall productivity. Climate change has further exacerbated these constraints through rising temperatures, increased rainfall variability, and a higher frequency of extreme weather events. Such environmental pressures significantly compromise plant health and reduce bean yields. In the context of rapid global population growth, it is imperative to enhance bean production to ensure food security. This study presents a literature review encompassing research conducted between 2014 and 2025, with particular focus on the impact of climate variability on the prevalence and spread of bean diseases. The review also synthesizes proposed strategies for mitigating the effects of climate-related stresses. The findings underscore persistent challenges, notably the insufficiently explored influence of climate change on the dynamics of plant diseases.

title: Digital Transformation Readiness and SME Performance: An Information Systems Model Using TOE and Social Media Adoption
authors: Gromyko Bongso, Nicholas Salim, Imelda Citra Anggraini
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Following the COVID-19 pandemic, studies shown the barriers issues related to the adoption of new technology. In particular, this research studies the social media effect. The authors apply a strict quantitative approach, utilizing structural equation modeling to analyze survey data from SME owners and managers in Indonesia. The research findings illustrate that, within the context of SMEs, social media adoption affect positively the SME performance and is positively influenced by the SMEs’ technological and organizational readiness. The authors state that social media adoption is a mediator between the TOE related factors and performance outcomes. This research shows that social media adoption in SMEs has a positive impact and, therefore, contributes to the literature pertaining to digital transformation.

title: AI-Driven Learning Analytics for STEM Education: Insights from Primary School Admission
authors: Dina Fitria Murad, Shahnorbanun Sahran, Bambang Dwi Wijanarko, Erick Fernando, Siti Maryam, Silvia Ayunda Murad
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This paper explores the integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM) education in the context of elementary school admissions. Through the development of an AI-based learning analytics framework named AISA-P (AI-Based STEM Admission Framework for Elementary Education), this study investigates the technical and pedagogical implications of applying AI to evaluate student readiness for STEM learning. The students involved were in Grade 11, Level 2, at a high school in Pariaman City, totaling 200 students (4 classes). Using a mixed-methods empirical design, this study combined quantitative machine learning techniques-such as Random Forest and Decision Tree algorithms-with qualitative interviews involving teachers and parents. The findings indicate that AI can predict STEM readiness with $\mathbf{8 7. 4 \%}$ accuracy while maintaining interpretability and fairness. However, this study also highlights ethical and pedagogical concerns regarding algorithm transparency, teacher autonomy, and data privacy. By combining analytical rigor with human-centered educational values, this study provides new conceptual and empirical understanding of how AI can support equitable, data-driven, and inclusive elementary education. This study provides a framework that combines empirical evidence and human-centered design principles to inform equitable, transparent, and personalized admissions practices in elementary STEM education.

title: Foundational Study of Topological Indicators and Neural Vision Models for Enhanced UAV Navigation
authors: Kush Saraf, Rudra Patel, Lithika Saravanakumar
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Traditional SLAM based pipelines have high computational costs, while purely learning based methods have limited generalization capabilities. Neither approach is suitable for autonomous UAV navigation in conditions where GPS is unavailable and where resources are limited. Therefore, lightweight navigation methods that combine robustness and efficiency will be very important for small UAV platforms. This research will evaluate the implementation of a dual layer visual navigation system that combines topological place recognition with a learning based vision based local correction model, in a lightweight form that provides robust navigation with reduced processing overhead by separating the local and global aspects of motion refinement. Visual motion information and cues from the current viewpoint and destination node are integrated with the neural layer for geometric adjustments, whereas the topology layer creates a compact graph like structure of visual input keyframes for rough localization and route, destination selection. Preliminary results show that the proposed dual layer configuration enables models to remain smaller, which is particularly beneficial for resource constrained platforms, and that the proposed technique provides greater overall stability and path following than the existing single topology navigation approach. This effort serves to validate the proposed design as well as establish a foundation for additional comprehensive testing and practical development.

title: Optimizing Efficiency and Performance of Axial Flux Machines for Dual-Drive Systems with a variable Air gap
authors: Niklas Kolb, Thomas Albrecht, Stefan Gretzinger, Jan-Niklas Molan, Lennart Kopp, Markus Kley
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Production Technologies and Systems for E-Mobility (EPTS)
abstract: Battery electric vehicles are gaining increasing importance, particularly in terms of sustainability and global competitiveness. Beyond the widely used radial flux machines (RFMs), axial flux machines (AFMs) offer new possibilities for drive topologies due to their compact design. Their small axial package enables compact dual drives, making them the subject of this research. This paper focuses on enhancing the efficiency and performance of AFMs through the implementation of a variable air gap.

title: Subcellular Microdosimetry in a Virtual 3D HaCaT Cell Exposed to 26.5 GHz Millimeter Waves
authors: Sara Fontana, Noemi Dolciotti, Giulia Renna, Anna Sannino, Gianmarco Ronza, Stefania Romeo, Olga Zeni, Maria Rosaria Scarfî, Francesca Apollonio, Micaela Liberti
publish: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)
abstract: The deployment of fifth-generation (5G) has renewed interest in the assessment of human exposure to radiofrequency electromagnetic fields (RF-EMF). This study presents the development of an advanced virtual 3D HaCaT cell, reconstructed from confocal microscopy images. These models are employed for microdosimetric simulations at 26.5 GHz to evaluate electric field distributions and volumetric power dissipation density at cellular and subcellular levels.

title: Battery Management Systems for Lithium-Ion Batteries in Electric Vehicles: Architectures, Modeling, and SOC Estimation Methods - A State of the Art
authors: A. Fhail, H. El Fadil, A. Lassioui, H. Abbade, A. Hasni, S. El Meslouhih
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Electric vehicles (EVs) rely on lithium-ion batteries as their main energy source, making battery safety, efficiency, and reliability critical design issues. To ensure proper battery operation under varying load and environmental conditions, battery management systems (BMS) are integrated into EV battery packs. This paper provides a concise state-of-the-art review of BMS technologies for lithium-ion batteries in electric vehicle applications. The review covers common BMS architectures and implementation topologies, as well as equivalent circuit models used for battery behavior representation. Key BMS functions, including state-of-charge (SOC), state-of-health (SOH), and state-of-power (SOP) estimation methods, are also discussed. In addition, recent trends such as artificial intelligence-based techniques and wireless battery management systems (wBMS) are briefly highlighted. The paper aims to offer an application-oriented overview that supports the selection of suitable BMS solutions for electric vehicle systems.

title: Deep Learning Framework Integrating Self-Supervised Learning and Attention for Landslide Susceptibility Mapping
authors: Yanwei Zhang, Lina Yu, Qiwen Dong
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: The field of landslide susceptibility mapping has long faced a lack of high-precision labeled data. This paper proposes a deep learning framework that combines self-supervised learning with an attention mechanism. The framework utilizes the classic DeepLabv3+ model as its backbone and pre-trains it using a self-supervised learning strategy to enhance feature representation. At the same time, it introduces the CBAM module to enhance the model's sensitivity to spatial and channel information in the input features, thereby strengthening its ability to extract information from remote sensing data. This paper conducts standardized comparative experiments between the proposed deep learning framework and several classical models commonly used in landslide susceptibility studies. The results show that the proposed method achieves an F1 score of 0.820 and a mIoU of 0.846 on the test set, and it outperforms the comparison models overall. In addition, ablation studies are carried out to demonstrate that both self-supervised pre-training and the introduction of the attention mechanism contribute positively to improving model accuracy. Finally, the paper extracts and analyzes the channel attention scores from CBAM, which reveal the relative importance of different influencing factors in the proposed model. The findings of this study provide a new framework for applying deep learning models to regional landslide susceptibility analysis. Furthermore, the proposed framework shows the potential to improve performance by making effective use of limited high-precision labeled data.

title: Oral-Mamba vs. Swin-UNet: A Comparative Deep Learning Analysis for Gingivitis, Dental Caries, and Dental Calculus Segmentation
authors: Remya R Prabhu, Neeraja K, Vimina E R
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Gingivitis, dental caries, and dental calculus are some common oral health issues that many people face. Identifying these problems early through intraoral photographic images is crucial for early diagnosis and computer-assisted dental checks. This study compares two deep learning (DL) models, Oral-Mamba and Swin-UNet, for analyzing oral images to identify gum disease, tooth decay, and plaque. The focus is on training the models to recognize only disease-affected areas effectively. The models are trained and tested using a dataset of 3,365 annotated oral images in a 3-fold cross-validation setup. Evaluation metrics include Intersection over Union (IoU), Precision, Recall, and Accuracy. Results show that the Swin-UNet model achieves an average IoU of 0.8909, an average precision of 0.8989, an average recall of 0.8860, and an average accuracy of 0.9633, while the Oral-Mamba model boasts an average IoU of 0.8477, an average precision of 0.9112, an average recall of 0.9197, and an average accuracy of 0.9743. The experimental results show the comparative performance of the Swin-UNet and the Oral-Mamba model for the segmentation of oral diseases. The Oral-Mamba model shows higher precision, recall, and accuracy compared to the Swin-UNet model, which indicates the improved performance of the proposed model for accurately identifying the disease region in the intraoral image. The results clearly show the effectiveness of the architecture and the increasing importance of deep learning models for dental diagnosis.

title: Generative AI for Structural Design of Earthquake-Resistant Civil–Mechanical Systems
authors: Qasim Aljamal, Mohammad Q. Al-Jamal, Jamal Al Sadi, Mohammad Aljaidi, Mahmoud Al-Jamal, Ayoub Alsarhan, Maruf Farhan, Wael Hadi, Ahmad Abdullah Alshammari
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: The article discusses a machine learning-based earthquake-resistant building generative architecture, more than the traditional deterministic architectures and finite element analysis. This model is a system composed of physics-based generative models and structural dynamics to maximize the stiffness, flexibility,, and energy dissipation designs. It applies a mixture of variational autoencoders and generative adversarial networks as the input, and a series of seismic load histories and material properties. The structure revealed a significant increment: the 32.8% growth in the energy intake, the 24.5% drop in the peak movement and the incredible 99.92% achievement in declaring the designs as either seismically secure or unsafe. The results point to the opportunities of generative AI to create strong and effective structural solutions.

title: Energy Management and DC Bus regulation of an isolated DC Microgrid using Backstepping Control and ANN-Based MPPT
authors: Hajar Akli, Abdelowahed Hajjaji, Hassan Abouobaida, Amina El Aaoud, Safeer Ullah, Ghulam Hafeez, Khadija Oualifi, Abdelmoghit Fathelkhair
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This paper presents a backstepping-based control and energy management strategy for an isolated DC microgrid integrating photovoltaic, wind, and battery energy storage systems. Due to the intermittent nature of renewable energy sources, advanced maximum power point tracking (MPPT) techniques are required to ensure efficient energy extraction under variable environmental conditions. In this work, an artificial neural network (ANN)-based MPPT algorithm is employed to generate optimal voltage references for the $\mathbf{P V}$ and wind subsystems. An energy management system (EMS) is developed to coordinate power sharing and generate the reference battery current, ensuring DC bus voltage stability and balanced operation. The proposed backstepping control scheme is designed to regulate the PV and wind boost converters as well as the bidirectional battery converter, enabling accurate reference tracking and robust performance. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach in maintaining DC bus voltage stability and improving energy utilization under renewable and load variations.

title: Ensemble Strategies for LLM-based Text Summarization: A Timing-Based Framework
authors: Rooz Munjal, Sonali Goyal
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: One of the biggest problems with big language models (LLMs) is finding the right balance between accuracy, cost, and latency while also cutting down on hallucinations. This research presents a temporal categorization of ensemble techniques (EMs) into Ensemble Before Inference (EBI), Ensemble During Inference (EDI), and Ensemble After Inference (EAI). A systematic review of 11 ensemble studies (2022–2025) employing transformers in multimodal domains reveals distinct performance–cost trade-offs. Ensemble approaches significantly enhance hallucination detection, medical and legal summarization, multi-domain tasks, and low-resource language processing, yielding performance improvements between 2% to 33% compared to single models. The temporal taxonomy shows that each strategy has its own strengths: EBI reduces runtime overhead while keeping quality, EDI improves output quality at a higher computational cost, and EAI is a balanced and versatile option. This framework gives academics and practitioners a structured way to choose the best ensemble techniques depending on the needs of the application and the resources available.

title: Heterophily-Robust Patient-Similarity Graphs for Parkinson’s Screening from Voice
authors: Ekta Srivastava, Siddhant Ujjain, Tapan Kumar Gandhi, Sandeep Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: Voice-based Parkinson’s disease (PD) screening is a data-analytics task requiring robust and well-calibrated risk estimates from small, correlated tabular features, and aligns with SDG 3 (Good Health and Well-Being). We propose a lightweight patient-similarity pipeline that builds a k-nearest-neighbor graph on standardized acoustic descriptors and applies Personalized PageRank (PPR) with restart to aggregate multi-hop evidence under heterophily (label-discordant neighborhoods). The method is fully specified for reproducibility (graph construction and propagation settings) and supports decision support via calibrated probabilities and transparent explanations. We provide dual interpretability using SHAP feature attributions and influential-neighbor inspection, and apply post-hoc temperature scaling to improve calibration. On the UCI Parkinsons dataset (n=195; PD= 147, HC= 48), PPR achieves 0.974 AUROC, 0.953 F1, and Brier 0.084, reducing ECE by 0.047. The most significant improvements occur in low to intermediate homophily settings, and ablation studies over k ∈{3,5,10}, cosine and Euclidean distance metrics, and the restart weight α show robustness. Running a full 5-fold pipeline takes approximately ~45 s on a laptop while giving a clear probability score that can be used for screening in clinics.

title: Assessing the Efficacy of Pre-trained Deep Learning Models for Facial Expression Recognition
authors: Maryam Alkandari, Alaa Eleyan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Facial Expression Recognition (FER) is a fundamental problem in affective computing and human– computer interaction, with applications in healthcare, surveillance, and intelligent user interfaces. Recent advances in deep learning, particularly convolutional neural networks (CNNs), have significantly improved FER performance by leveraging transfer learning from large-scale image datasets. This paper presents a comparative performance analysis of four state-of-the-art pre-trained CNN architectures, namely, ResNet152, EfficientNetV2-L, Xception, and DenseNet201 which are applied to facial expression recognition using the Extended Cohn–Kanade (CK+) dataset. The experiments were conducted on eight emotion classes with data augmentation applied to enhance generalization. All models were fine-tuned using the Adam optimizer under identical experimental conditions. Performance evaluation was carried out using confusion matrices and class-wise, micro-averaged, and weighted-averaged metrics. Among the evaluated models, DenseNet201 achieved the highest accuracy of 89.25%, outperforming EffiecientNetV2-L by 1.08% under identical training conditions. This indicates that dense feature reuse is particularly effective for capturing subtle facial expression patterns, while EfficientNetV2-L provides a balanced trade-off between accuracy and model efficiency

title: Robust Speed Regulation of BLDC Motors through a Buck Boost Assisted Super Twisting SMC
authors: A. Hasni, H. El Fadil, A. Lassioui, T. Bouanou, M. El Ancary, Y. El Asri, M. Chiheb
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel control strategy for Brushless DC (BLDC) motor drives based on a Buck-Boost converter, aiming to enhance dynamic performance, reduce speed ripples, and suppress chattering effects. The proposed approach combines an Enhanced Super-Twisting Sliding Mode Controller (STSMC) for precise motor speed regulation with a Proportional-Integral-Derivative (PID) controller dedicated to stabilizing the converter voltage. While the STSMC provides strong robustness against disturbances and parameter variations, it inherently produces chattering phenomena that can deteriorate system smoothness. To mitigate this issue, the PID controller regulates the Buck-Boost converter voltage, ensuring smoother torque and minimizing current oscillations. The hybrid STSMC-PID dual control structure improves both transient and steady-state performance, achieving faster dynamic response and higher control precision. MATLAB/Simulink simulations confirm the efficiency of the proposed method, showing a remarkable reduction of the speed ripple rate to $0.12 \%$ and a fast recovery time of 0.05 s after load perturbations. These results demonstrate that integrating a Buck-Boost converter with the dual STSMC-PID control significantly enhances the stability and responsiveness of BLDC motor drives, providing a promising solution for electric vehicle propulsion and advanced industrial motion systems.

title: Utilizing Academic Analytic Dashboards in Improving Student Graduation Rates
authors: Walid Ibrahim
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The growing complexity of higher education institutions has elevated the need for academic analytics dashboards as a critical tool for enhancing institutional effectiveness, facilitating evidence-based decision-making, and supporting continuous quality improvement. This paper discusses the importance of academic analytic dashboards and presents the United Arab Emirates University (UAEU) as a case study of effective institutional implementation. The UAEU dashboard provides a comprehensive suite of interactive pages designed to support data-informed decision-making across multiple dimensions of academic performance. Among these, this paper focuses on three core pages that are particularly critical for academic planning and enhancing student graduation rate. These pages deliver real-time, insights into course offerings, student registration, as well as graduation, and retention patterns. The dashboard provides academic administrators with the needed insights to adjust course capacities, add or consolidate sections, and allocate instructional resources more efficiently based on emerging trends. Furthermore, the timely detection of registration anomalies or at-risk patterns allows for targeted interventions that help students stay on track with their academic plans. Collectively, these features enhance institutional responsiveness, promote better resource allocation, and ultimately contribute to improving student progression and graduation outcomes.

title: Intelligent Diagnosis of Transformer Partial Discharge Based on ALEF-SSG Signal Enhancement and Multi-Domain Feature Fusion
authors: Jun Deng, Dui Liu, Haibin Zhou, Jinquan Tang, Zhicheng Xie, Xingzi Liu, Heng Wu, Zhicheng Pan
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: Partial discharge serves as the primary early indicator of insulation degradation in power transformers, and its accurate diagnosis is critical to ensuring the safe and stable operation of power grids. Aiming at the engineering bottlenecks of existing methods in weak feature identification, sample imbalance, and model generalization, this paper presents a complete intelligent diagnosis framework. First, an ALEF-SSG signal enhancement scheme and an ACM channel hybrid augmentation strategy are proposed to effectively suppress noise, enrich sample diversity, and mitigate overfitting under small-sample conditions. Second, a time-frequency-entropy coupled multi-domain feature set is constructed, followed by improved Relief-F selection and feature enhancement to achieve dimensionality reduction and discriminability reinforcement of high-dimensional features. Finally, an AMR-MSA-Net network is devised together with a class-balanced training strategy to realize accurate classification of four typical fault types. Validation on related dataset demonstrates that the core evaluation metrics of the proposed model exceed 0.94. Compared with conventional models, the proposed method exhibits significant improvements in diagnostic accuracy, generalization ability, and anti-interference performance, with strong engineering applicability.

title: Ontology-Enriched Abstract Topic Modelling for Biomedical Trend Discovery: A MeSH-based LDA Framework with BI Visual Analytics
authors: Ahmad Altarawneh, Stephen Swift, Allan Tucker, Mahir Arzoky
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: The explosive growth of biomedical literature increasingly makes it difficult to identify incipient topics and long-term developments. Citation-based indicators are slow to capture developments and often fail to represent influence. Whilst Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) supports content-based trend discovery, its bag-of-words assumption can overlook synonymy, polysemy, and hierarchical relations that are central to biomedical terminology. Moreover, recent work provides limited direct evidence on how far MeSH-enriched abstract representations improve LDA-based trend analysis when compared against the original abstract text. To fill this gap, an ontology-driven methodology for the pre-topic modelling of article abstracts by enriching them with hierarchical Medical Subject Headings (MeSH) terms is introduced in this study. We evaluate four representations, namely original abstracts, MeSH parent categories, MeSH-matched terms with parents, and a fully enriched representation, using a decade-long Scopus corpus of 624,486 biomedical journal articles (2015–2024). Model performance is assessed using coherence, distinctiveness, perplexity, and stability. The fully enriched representation achieves the strongest overall results (coherence 0.606, distinctiveness 0.996, stability 0.725, perplexity 1782.040) relative to the original abstract baseline. Temporal topic analysis further reveals interpretable patterns, including the COVID-19 surge and longer-term structural shifts such as the rise of machine-learning diagnostics. These findings indicate that MeSH-guided abstract enrichment can strengthen topic quality and support more effective biomedical trend discovery through business-intelligence visual analytics.

title: A Method for Elevation Calculation of Offshore Islands and Reefs Far from the Mainland Based on Water Level Derivation Technology
authors: Caixia Yu, Yiman Yang, Yan Lu, Haifeng Lin
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: Spaceborne or aerial photogrammetry enables rapid acquisition of image data of uninhabited or special islands and reefs far from the mainland. However, providing a consistent absolute vertical datum for such image data remains a challenging issue. Based on the principle of water level derivation, this paper proposes a method for calculating the instantaneous sea surface elevation by using tidal models, stationary sea surface topography models, and residual water levels transferred from coastal tide gauges. In the China seas, the derivation accuracy of the instantaneous sea surface elevation is assessed using current regional tidal models, stationary sea surface topography models, and residual water level data. Under the worst-case condition without support from coastal tide gauges, the root-mean-square error of the derivation is approximately 18.77 cm. Taking Qianliyan Island in the North Yellow Sea and Weizhou Island in the Beibu Gulf, both 50 km offshore, as an example, the evaluation results show that the root-mean-square error of the estimation under the most unfavorable conditions are 15.22 cm and 14.02 cm, respectively. With the support of coastal tide gauges, the root-mean-square errors are reduced to 8.26 cm and 7.29 cm, respectively.

title: EchoGuard: Biometric Voice-based Audio Watermarking with Blockchain-Enabled Ownership Verification
authors: Padmini Devi B, Vishnu M S, Saaidarun S, Vinith M
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: The rapid growth of digital audio content has made ownership verification and tamper detection increasingly challenging. Existing approaches, including conventional watermarking and blockchain-based timestamping, fail to establish a reliable and identity-linked association between audio content and its creator. To address this limitation, this paper proposes EchoGuard, a hybrid AI–blockchain framework for secure audio ownership verification. The proposed system integrates biometric speaker embeddings, neural audio watermarking, and decentralized blockchain registration into a unified pipeline. A creator-specific voiceprint is first extracted using a deep speaker representation model and converted into a compact watermark payload. This payload is then embedded into the host audio using a deep learning-based steganographic model, ensuring imperceptibility while maintaining robustness against common signal processing attacks. The system further stores cryptographic hashes of the watermarked audio on a blockchain to provide immutable and verifiable ownership records. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that the proposed approach achieves high watermark extraction accuracy and strong robustness under compression, noise, and resampling attacks, while preserving audio quality. The results highlight the effectiveness of combining biometric watermarking with blockchain to enable reliable, tamper-resistant audio authentication.

title: Optimizing Linux Kernel Scheduler Parameters Using Reinforcement Learning
authors: Hitesh Makka, Kartikey, Preeti Kaur
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: The Linux kernel’s Completely Fair Scheduler (CFS) is a cornerstone of modern operating systems, but its static, heuristic-based design often fails to deliver optimal performance for specialized, high-demand workloads. This paper investigates the application of a modern reinforcement learning algorithm, Asynchronous Advantage Actor-Critic (A3C), to dynamically tune nine critical CFS parameters. An agent was trained in a live kernel environment on a PostgreSQL OLTP workload to learn an optimal scheduling policy. The evaluation reveals a significant performance trade-off. The A3C-optimized parameters significantly improved throughput for CPU-intensive tasks, achieving a 47.4% faster completion time on the Hackbench scheduler stress test and a 14.8% increase in requests-per-second for an Apache high-concurrency test. However, this gain came at a significant cost to latency-sensitive applications, with the same policy causing a 7.6% performance regression and a seven-fold increase in instability on the Redis benchmark. The principal contribution of this work is the empirical evaluation of the A3C algorithm for live kernel parameter tuning, characterizing its effects and revealing trade-offs between throughput gains and low-latency performance.

title: Decision letter for "End-to-End Depositional Microfacies Identification from Variable-Log Inputs Using CNN and Attention-CRF"
authors: N/A

title: Secure and Optimized Cyber-Physical Architecture for Smart Grid and IoT-Enabled Energy Systems
authors: Anjali Goswami, Anjali Krushna Kadao
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: One of the strong aspects of smart grids is increasing the use of Internet of things (IoT) technologies to enhance monitoring and control of energy, but also poses serious cybersecurity and operational risks. The proposed study is a safe and efficient cyber-physical architecture of IoT-based smart grid systems aimed at increasing data security, operation efficiency, and scalability. The framework suggested incorporates secure communication standards such as AES-256 encryption, TLS communication and MQTT messaging in a manner that promotes a stable and secure transmission of data between grid devices and control systems. Moreover, predictive machine learning algorithms will be implemented that will help predict loads and optimize the grid in real-time. The architecture would also enable the combination of renewable energy sources and energy storage systems that will enhance the use of energy in a sustainable manner. Evaluation conducted with the help of simulating proves that the proposed method would enhance energy efficiency by approximately 15%, communication latency is reduced to 30 ms, and the rate of breaches of security is reduced to 0.2% in comparison to traditional grid systems. The findings are important in how cybersecurity mechanisms and energy optimization strategies can be combined in cyber-physical smart grid infrastructures. The suggested architecture offers a secure and scalable platform of the upcoming smart grid installation and industrial IoT deployments.

title: A Multi-Stage XAI-Enhanced Transformer Model for Bias-Aware Decision Making in Financial Fraud Detection
authors: Yuvaraj Duraisamy, Rabins Porwal, Kshamta Chauhan, P. Ramya, Kunal, Sribidhya Mohanty
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Loss of transparency and bias in the detection of financial fraud presents two significant problems, namely, poor adherence to the standards of fraud detection, and lost user confidence. In order to address these issues, we have come up with a Multi-Stage XAI-Enhanced Transformer Model that is used to deal with bias-conscious decision making in fraud detection. We use a combination of three aspects Transformers to identify complex pattern of temporal frauds, adversarial debiasing to make fair predictions across the various demographic groups and explainability by visualization of attention and SHAP analysis. We achieve 97.3% accuracy, 98.1% recall, and 99.2% AUC-ROC, better than current algorithms, such as XGBoost (95.1%) and Random Forests (93.8%). Better still, we maintained demographic parity disparity at less than 0.02 which reduced bias by 89.8 per cent relative to conventional systems. The SHAP analysis revealed that the most decisions are made basing on the Transaction Amount, Time of Transaction, and Merchant Category which provides the stakeholders with a clear understanding of the operation of the model. Such a balance between explainability and bias minimization forms a fraud protection system that is both non-discriminatory and credible enough, as required by the regulations and ethical principles of the fintech sector.

title: Integrating Literature and Learning: A Graph Neural Network Framework for Herb-Drug Interaction Prediction Toward Sustainable and Clinically Informed Pharmaceutical Safety
authors: Eyad Mallah, Ahmad Altarawneh, Khaled Omari, Stephen Swift, Mahir Arzoky
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: The increasing combined use of herbal medicines and conventional drugs has increased the need for the reliable prediction and mitigation of herb-drug interactions globally. Prediction is difficult because herbal products are chemically complex mixtures, and their constituents can act across multiple biological pathways simultaneously. Conventional computational approaches, including quantitative structure activity relationship models and standard machine learning (ML) classifiers, often underperform in this setting because they represent the problem as isolated entities rather than as an interaction network. This work proposes a more appropriate herb-drug interaction prediction model, called Heterogeneous Graph Attention Network (H-GAN), which is a Graph Neural Network (GNN) framework. By building a heterogeneous graph for herbal components, drugs co-administered, and significant biological targets (e.g., metabolic enzymes, transporters), the proposed model can consider the possible synergistic or antagonistic effects of multiple constituents that are responsible for the interaction outcomes. This approach provides a mechanism for enhancing patient safety and facilitates the inclusion of safety in the sustainable development of pharmaceuticals following the principles of green chemistry. Accordingly, this paper offers a conceptual, literature-grounded framework for herb–drug interaction prediction using heterogeneous graph attention networks, outlining the proposed graph schema, biologically motivated meta-paths, and interpretability considerations to guide future implementation and validation. This contribution is conceptual and framework-orientated; it does not include implementation, dataset construction, or quantitative validation.

title: A Vector-Free Global Terrain Analysis Algorithm and its Application: Extraction of Constructible Areas for Navigation in Wild and Extreme Disaster-Stricken Zones
authors: Boyang Guo, Mianqing Zhong
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: Aiming at the inefficiency and heavy multi-source data reliance of traditional methods for constructible area extraction in vector-free extreme scenarios (e.g., war, disaster zones), a DEM-only vector-free global terrain analysis algorithm is proposed. The algorithm constructs a directed weighted slope topological graph integrating slope, azimuth and terrain flow direction via DEM preprocessing and regular sampling, then realizes candidate node preliminary screening, terrain-constrained 8-neighborhood DFS connected domain retrieval, and secondary screening by azimuth consistency and area threshold to automatically extract qualified contiguous constructible areas. Lightweight, globally adaptable and reproducible, it abandons auxiliary data and large GIS tools, and can be rapidly applied to vector-free scenarios to provide efficient technical support for emergency engineering terrain decision-making.

title: Effect IoT based-RFID on Stock-Recording Efficiency in Indonesian Retail Industry: T-O-E Model with Workflow Optimization
authors: Bryan Lewis, Mahaning Indrawaty Wijaya, Hendry Hartono
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This study analyzes effect IoT integration based on RFID technology, system integration, and employee digital competence on stock recording efficiency in the Indonesian retail industry. Internet of Things (IoT) technology has encouraged the adoption of Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) technology in inventory management to improve stock recording efficiency. However, stock recording efficiency is not only influenced by technology implementation, but also by human, organizational, process, and system factors. The results aim to analyze effect of IoT-based RFID adoption, system integration, and employee digital competence on stock recording efficiency in the Indonesian retail industry, with workflow optimization as a mediating variable and organizational change readiness as a moderating variable. This study uses a quantitative research approach using the SmartPLS application with the Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM) model, following the methodological guidelines of Hair et al. (2019). The results indicate that the adoption of IoT-based RFID, system integration, and employee digital competence have a positive and significant impact on workflow optimization. In addition, workflow optimization has a significant positive impact on stock recording efficiency and mediates the relationship between independent variables and stock recording efficiency. Organizational change readiness significantly moderates the relationship between workflow optimization and stock recording efficiency. These results indicate that improving stock recording efficiency requires not only technology adoption, but also integrated systems, employee digital competence, optimal workflows, and organizational readiness to manage change.

title:
Numerical Investigation of MASnBr
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    /CsSnGeI
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    /SnO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Solar Cell: Understanding the Impact of Absorber Layer Parameters
authors: Abhijit Kumar Kar, Mnisha, Chandni Tiwari
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
