abstract: The study of solar cell based on perovskite materials have been reported numerously in past few years. However, the most reported paper has used lead as one of the constituent elements of perovskite which pose toxicity and environmental challenges. To address this issue, this study is focused on investigating a lead-free substitute cesium tin– germanium triiodide (CsSnGeI<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>) which offers better performance. Tin dioxide (SnO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>) and MASnBr<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> were selected as the electron transport layer (ETL) and hole transport layer (HTL), respectively, because of their suitable energy band alignment and superior carrier mobility. We have investigated the performance of solar cell by varying the absorber thickness and electron affinity. The device has also been investigated for variation in bulk defect and interface defects with ETL and HTL. The software used here is SCAPS 1D for the numerical investigations. The studied solar cell depicted a power conversion efficiency (PCE) of 30.61%, with an open-circuit voltage (Voc) of 1.2 V, a fill factor (FF) of 89.8%, and a short-circuit current density (Jsc) of 28.31 mA/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>.

title: Clustering of Equipment for Maintenance in BIM 7D Using the K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) Approach Applied to Data Reduced by t-SNE (t-Distributed Stochastic Neighbor Embedding)
authors: Adnane Abdellaoui Alaoui, Mouna El Mkhalet, Nouzha Lamdouar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Building Information Modeling (BIM) in its 7D dimension incorporates maintenance management and life cycle cost of technical equipment. Optimizing this maintenance requires intelligent methods capable of grouping equipment according to their operational behaviors and characteristics. This article proposes a supervised clustering approach using the K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) algorithm applied to dimensionally reduced data using the t-Distributed Stochastic Neighbor Embedding (t-SNE) technique. The objective is to visualize and classify equipment based on aging criteria, failure frequency, temperature, duration, and intensity of use. The results show that this $t-S N E+K N N$ combination allows for distinguishing three main groups of equipment with similar maintenance behaviors, paving the way for predictive models integrated into 7D BIM.)

title: Beyond information: how smart systems manage consumer knowledge
authors: Yasmine Lebbadi, Nisrine El Ayat
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Smart systems, including artificial intelligence driven platforms, recommender systems, conversational agents, and algorithmic decision-support tools, are increasingly embedded in consumer decision-making processes. While prior research has primarily conceptualized these systems as advanced information providers (Shankar, 2018; Huang & Rust, 2021), this article argues that their role extends far beyond information delivery. Drawing on knowledge management theory (Nonaka & Takeuchi, 1995; Davenport & Prusak, 1998), consumer learning (Alba & Hutchinson, 1987; Bettman, Luce, & Payne, 1998), and algorithmic governance (Gillespie, 2014; Pasquale, 2015), we propose that smart systems function as active consumer knowledge managers. We develop a conceptual framework explaining how smart systems manage consumer knowledge through three interdependent processes: knowledge acquisition, via algorithmic curation, personalization, and selective exposure (Thorson & Wells, 2016; Wedel & Kannan, 2016); knowledge transformation, through sensemaking support, cognitive scaffolding, and experiential learning facilitation (Weick, 1995; Hoffman & Novak, 2018); and knowledge governance, whereby algorithmic authority, trust calibration, and opacity shape epistemic dependence and perceived expertise (Luhmann, 1979; Logg, Minson, & Moore, 2019). We argue that smart systems progressively reconfigure consumer knowledge from an individually constructed cognitive asset toward an algorithmically mediated form of knowing, altering autonomy, decision confidence, and perceived control. While these systems enhance cognitive efficiency and reduce information overload (Eppler & Mengis, 2004), they simultaneously raise concerns related to knowledge asymmetry, cognitive passivity, and ethical responsibility. By reframing smart systems as knowledge management actors rather than neutral informational tools, this research advances marketing information systems literature and provides strategic guidance for designing smart systems that support sustainable consumer knowledge development rather than mere behavioral optimization.

title: Study on Cutting of Rectangular Copper Hollow Wire for the Use in Hairpin Stator Electric Traction Motors
authors: Till Backes, Achim Kampker, Henrik Born, Michael Nankemann, Simon Krusenbaum, Matthias Hörder
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Production Technologies and Systems for E-Mobility (EPTS)
abstract: The increasing demand for efficient electric traction motors requires innovative approaches in design and production. Hairpin stators have established themselves as a promising technology for increasing the power density and efficiency of electric motors. The use of rectangular hollow copper wires offers additional advantages in performance through significantly improved heat dissipation and weight reduction. However, the precise cutting of these hollow wires poses a technical challenge, as conventional cutting methods can lead to deformation or damage. In this study, the cutting process for rectangular hollow copper wires is analyzed and optimized. Various cutting methods, including mechanical sawing and laser cutting were analyzed in terms of their cutting quality, process speed and effects on material properties. The results show that sawing offers the highest precision with minimal thermal impact on the material. Optimized cutting processes enable improved integration of the hollow copper wires in hairpin stators. The findings of this study contribute to the further development of production technologies for future-oriented electric motors and thus support the transition to sustainable mobility.

title: The Influence of AI-Powered Reviews on Consumer Purchase Intention and Decision in E-Commerce: An Adapted UTAUT2 Approach
authors: Amanda Saphira, Cadelina Cassandra, Danang Prihandoko
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The increasing adoption of AI-powered reviews in e-commerce platforms has changed how consumers evaluate products and make purchase decisions. This study aims to examine the influence of AI-powered reviews on purchase intention and purchase decision by adapting the Unified Theory of Acceptance and Use of Technology 2 (UTAUT2) framework. This research investigates the effect of effort expectancy, performance expectancy, hedonic motivation, personal innovativeness and trust on consumers' purchase intention as well as the effect of purchase intention on purchase decision. The data were collected using an online survey method and 382 Indonesian e-commerce consumers participated in the study. Data were analyzed using Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM) with SmartPLS version 4.1.1.6. The result shows that all variables significantly influence purchase intention toward AI-powered reviews. Furthermore, purchase intention is found to significantly affect purchase decisions. This study, however is restricted to Indonesian consumers and uses self-reported data, suggesting that future research might examine cross-cultural contexts and long-term behavioral impacts.

title: Quantum-Resilient Cryptography and Safe Guarding Post Quantum Cryptographic Protocols Against Quantum Threats
authors: S Hemajothi, Keerthana B, Janani R, Farzana E
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: The Hybrid approach in Quantum Cryptography creates adaptable security model which provides the safest Communication in the emerging quantum era. The development of quantum computing raise questions about the long-term security. Traditional algorithms, such as RSA, ECC, and Diffie-Hellman, which is seemed computationally secure but it can be effectively solved by quantum algorithms such as Shor's and Grover's. This problem leads to the origination of two key trends in current studies, namely Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC), designing mathematical approaches to encryption resistant to quantum attacks; Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) using the principles of quantum mechanics to assure security in communications. Accordingly, this research proposes a hybrid security framework including reliable data encryption and authentication using PQC, combined with the safe key- exchange method of QKD. The model is implemented in Python, Qiskit, and PQClean's Kyber and Dilithium algorithms to show how these two paradigms can cooperate toward quantum- resilient communication. To present the system performance, key exchange efficiency, computational overhead, and security strength have been considered. According to the results, this hybrid approach gives superior defense against quantum threats while maintaining practical effectiveness for real-world applications such as cloud security, 6G networks, and the Internet of Things.

title: Affective Intelligence-Enhanced Visual Content Summarization for Context-Rich Video Understanding
authors: Kumar P, Hari Sainath C
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Combining emotion recognition technology with video to text summarization brings about a totally novel innovative avenue for automated content analysis. Here we present the conceptualization and implementation of the Emotion Infused Visual Content Summarizer (EIVCS) which is emotionally intelligent regarding the summarization methods used in the textual domain. EIVCS takes advantage of the emini API to produce brief video to text summaries and incorporates emotion recognition to determine the emotional states shown by visual content. EIVCS text interpretation and emotional derivation come from the same video frame to generate texts that are not only the summarization of the recorded videos but also the representations of the emotions experienced, the full and contextualized representation of video content. This paper demonstrates the set up the plan, and the components of the EIVCS besides the combination of emotion detection algorithms with novel summarization models. The scheme reveals how the system's ability to interpret linguistic and affective signals may result in improved user content understanding feedback which is the strength of automatic systems that claim to analyze the users' content understanding. Moreover, the potential implementation areas related to this line of inquiry are media analysis, user experience improvement, content recommendation systems, and other areas at the interface where emotional context could be critical to user engagement. By integrating video-based content summarization with emotion recognition, the EIVCS is a new and more human-centered perspective on videos that can not only enhance user experience but also make video content analysis more nuanced and meaningful.

title: Towards Robust Closed-Loop Control for Aerosol Jet Printing Using Laser Transmission Feedback
authors: Sagni Majumdar, Florian Risch, Jorg Franke, Sebastian Reitelshofer
publish: 2025 16th International MID Congress Mechatronic Integration Discourse (MID Congress)
abstract: Aerosol Jet Printing (AJP) is an emerging direct-write technology for fabricating high-resolution functional materials. Despite its advantages, the absence of robust process control remains a major barrier to widespread industrial adoption. This paper addresses the challenge of implementing closed-loop control in AJP by presenting an approach based on laser transmission measurements. Building on previous research employing camera and optical scattering-based feedback mechanisms, this work presents a control architecture that enables real-time, accurate, and non-intrusive monitoring of the aerosol stream. Expanding upon foundational studies, this method facilitates localized process control by employing a single beam splitter with dual photodetectors for measurement and reference signals. This work reviews the current state of AJP process monitoring, identifies limitations of existing control solutions, and details a scalable architecture designed to enhance precision and adaptability in future implementations.

title: Big Data-Driven Monitoring and Risk Early Warning for Mountainous River Waterways
authors: Zhihao Yao
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: Mountainous river waterways are characterized by complex terrain, abrupt hydrological variation, and coupled navigation-geology hazards, while conventional monitoring and alarm systems remain constrained by fragmented data, low precision, and delayed response. This study proposes a big-datadriven monitoring and risk early warning framework for mountainous river waterways. A heterogeneous data fusion architecture is first established to integrate hydrological, meteorological, navigation, geological, and dispatch-related streams. A dynamic warning model is then developed using an optimized long short-term memory (LSTM) network to encode temporal dependency, cross-source interaction, and hazard escalation patterns. On this basis, an intelligent platform integrating acquisition, analysis, diagnosis, and warning push is designed for closed-loop operation. A representative Wujiang mountainous waterway reach is selected for validation using three years of measured data. Results show that the optimized model achieves an overall warning accuracy of 92.7%, improving by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 1. 3 {\%}}$</tex> over a conventional threshold-based method. The false alarm rate decreases to 4.1%, and warning latency under extreme hydrological conditions is controlled within 8 s. The framework accurately identifies high-risk shoals, rapid-flow reaches, and landslide-prone banks, demonstrating a practical route toward safer inland navigation governance and smart water transport upgrading.

title: AI-Based Predictive Energy Management of a Hybrid Solar-Wind System for Green Hydrogen Production: Case Study of Tarfaya, Morocco
authors: Fatima Zahra El-Yazidi, Hamid Mounir
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Combining renewable energy systems and the production of green hydrogen presents a major opportunity to enable significant decarbonisation of both the energy and industrial sectors. However, due to the intermittent nature of both solar (PV) and wind resources, achieving and maintaining the necessary conditions for electrolyzers to operate continuously and reliably remains a major hurdle. To address this challenge, this paper proposes an artificial intelligence (AI)based predictive energy management system to manage a hybrid solar-wind-battery system that produces hydrogen. The target location for this application is the coastal city of Tarfaya (Morocco) where the authors conducted an earlier data-driven analysis that determined the location had a very high level of suitability for deploying hybrid renewable systems. Along with the system’s renewable energy harvesting component, on-site climatic data are employed for renewable energy production simulation and in AI-based prediction & intelligent control technique development. A forecasting machine learning model is developed to predict the solar and wind generation on a dayahead basis, and an RL-based energy management strategy is proposed in order to coordinate renewable generation, battery storage as well as grid interaction. The objective of the proposed approach is to reduce RE intermittency and energy loss, as well as guaranteeing the electrolyzer continuous operation.

title: From Data to Value: Assessing the Role of Digital Approaches in Enhancing Battery Cell Manufacturing through Expert Perspectives
authors: Marcel Drescher, Rui Yan Li, Clemens Scherer, Paul Lingohr, Heiner Heimes, Achim Kampker
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Production Technologies and Systems for E-Mobility (EPTS)
abstract: Battery cell manufacturing represents a critical technology for the future of energy storage and electrified mobility, yet continues to face substantial challenges in process stability, yield optimization, and industrial scalability. Data-driven optimization approaches offer significant potential to address these challenges by leveraging advanced analytics, machine learning, and adaptive control systems. However, their practical implementation remains limited due to systemic barriers. This study investigates the current state, technological readiness, and key obstacles associated with data-driven optimization in battery cell production through a structured expert survey involving 46 professionals from industry and academia. The analysis is structured along three core dimensions: (i) general digitalization challenges, (ii) traceability as a prerequisite for adaptive control, and (iii) cross-process optimization. The results confirm that deficits in process data availability, traceability, and cross-process knowledge are the primary obstacles to industrial adoption of data-driven manufacturing. Despite these challenges, experts unanimously recognize the high potential of advanced traceability and integrated control systems to significantly improve production efficiency and flexibility. The findings underline the need for coordinated research and development efforts addressing technical traceability solutions, data infrastructure harmonization, and the systematic generation of high-quality process data to advance data-driven battery manufacturing towards industrial maturity.

title: Deep Semantic Feature Learning and Efficient Indexing for Content-Based Image Retrieval
authors: Khushbu Devi Tiwari, Swati Khanve, Nitya Khare, Vinay Lowanshi
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: A lot of organizations and people have huge amounts of images. These pictures can be used for training data, visualization, marketing, etc. Searching through picture archives can become an overwhelming task because of the amount of data. Deep features require efficient indexing and storage methods for image retrieval that is scalable and efficient. Content-based image retrieval (CBIR) is the system that allows access to and search for images based on their contents. CBIR makes it easier for people to look for information or products by efficiently searching and browsing the world of images on the internet. Manually extracting images of interest as per query is a tedious process. CBIR plays an important role a better alternative to retrieval of images on a large scale. It also helps for quicker and easier detection than manual detection. The system generates feature vectors for every pixel in the image for further analysis of the image and the object. The system does not rely on any content (text or metadata) surrounding the image but on the actual content in the image.Systems based on handcrafted features of CBIR do not have sufficient semantic representation and scalability. We need to find a better way to extract content from images. Additionally, efficient and scalable algorithms for indexing are also needed.

title: Design and Optimization of a Waveguide Applicator for Cell Cultures Exposure in the FR1 Band of 5G Signals
authors: Stefania Romeo, Olga Zeni, Anna Sannino, Micaela Liberti, Francesca Apollonio, Alessandra Paffi, Maria Rosaria Scarfî
publish: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)
abstract: This paper presents the design and optimization of a waveguide applicator for exposure of cell cultures to RF-EMF at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3. 5 ~ G H z}$</tex>, an operative frequency of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 G}$</tex> technologies. In accordance with the requirements for an RF applicator, numerical dosimetry analysis was carried out to maximize uniformity of SAR distribution in the cell monolayer and the number of samples to be exposed simultaneously.

title: Hybrid Essay Assessment Framework: Integrating Neural Content Analysis with Reference-Based Similarity Evaluation
authors: Eman Alduweib, Essa Hezzam, Mohammed Noman, Abdel Khalik Alhammouri
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Essay grading is one of the most important applications of artificial intelligence in education. As digital learning platforms grow and are expanded, automated grading is said to offer students consistent immediate feedback and reduce teacher's assessment workload. This research provides a theoretical foundation for an automated grading model based on both reference-based similarity evaluation and content-based neural analysis of essays. The two methods are currently being used separately by most automated grading systems. The proposed framework uses three key components: dual pathway representations to maintain semantic depth and structural similarities, dynamic integration of evaluation signals through adaptive integration mechanisms, and plagiarism aware evaluation (to distinguish quality from plagiarism). We draw upon transformer-based language models, Information Retrieval Theory, and Educational Measurement to provide a basis for addressing long standing issues of fairness, consistency and interpretability. this framework provides a comprehensive assessment of a student's writing quality through its dual pathways. In addition, the adaptive integration mechanism in the framework allows for adjustments to be made to the assessment process based on the context in which the assessment is being conducted.

title: ALLMS-Modified Jaya Optimized Unified Power Quality Conditioner to Improve Power Quality
authors: B. Pavankumar, Saroj Pradhan
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: In this paper, a PV fed UPQC is constructed to address the Power Quality (PQ) problem in a distribution system. The suggested UPQC combines a series filter and a shunt filter, and an appropriate controller is developed in order to operate both filters effectively. At first, the controller is made to produce the reference injection signal for both filters. The reference for the shunt part is provided by combining an indirect current controller technique with adaptive leaky least mean square (ALLMS) and a modified Jaya algorithm based proportional integral (PI) technique with the active component of PV. The same method is proposed for the series but instead of current signal in shunt, voltage signal is chosen for series. The actual injection and reference injection are compared, and the error is fed to the hysteresis for the UPQC's switching operation. THD analysis is carried out for both current harmonics and voltage sag problem. The acceptable THD value as per IEEE 519 of source current after filtration is 4.31 % for current harmonics and 3.98 % for voltage sag is obtained, which shows the effectiveness of the technique. System performance is analyzed with MATLAB /Simulink platform

title: Exploration of Engineering Educational Models based on CDIO and TPACK
authors: Meriem El Batoul Eddaif, Mohamed Tabaa, Souad Ahriz, Es-Saadia Aoula
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The 21st century has witnessed a profound digital transformation in higher education, particularly within engineering education institutions. While the CDIO (Conceive–Design–Implement–Operate) framework promotes experiential and project-based learning grounded in real engineering practice, there is no clear approach for operationalizing technology, pedagogy, and content knowledge to enhance these experiential learning opportunities in a way that aligns seamlessly with CDIO standards. Conversely, the TPACK (technological, pedagogical and content knowledge) framework offers a theoretical foundation for teaching but lacks contextual anchoring in authentic engineering workflows. In order to facilitate the methodical creation of successful engineering learning experiences, this paper proposes an integrated CDIO–TPACK pedagogical model that integrates TPACK competencies with CDIO standards. The model provides educators with an organised approach to curriculum design, instructional planning, and assessment by clearly mapping CDIO standards to content knowledge, pedagogical knowledge and technological knowledge. A structured workflow for a Python programming course given to first-year engineering students demonstrates the applicability of the proposed framework into instructional designs that promote active learning and close the gap between theory and practice.

title: PSAR: Progressive Structure-Aware Refinement for Salient Object Detection via Spectral-Biased Dynamic Curriculum
authors: Aarsh Muley, Manjusha Behera, Rukmabha Mukherjee, Tapas Kumar Patra
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: State-of-the-art SOD models like F3Net often face high computational demands due to complex feature aggregation modules. Here, we introduce PSAR, a streamlined Feature Pyramid Network that replaces these complex modules with a Dy namic Resolution Curriculum (DRC). Inspired by the Frequency Principle, DRC scales inputs (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$224^{2} \rightarrow 352^{2}$</tex>) to stabilize global patterns before refining high-frequency details. On the DUTSTE benchmark, against a reproduced F3Net (32 epochs), PSAR achieves a superior MAE of 0.0354 (vs. 0.0421), indicating better global consistency. While we observe a trade-off in boundary precision (lower Max- <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F_{\beta}$</tex>) due to smoothing, PSAR operates with 46% fewer Inference FLOPs and significantly reduced training costs. This confirms that curriculum learning enables lighter models to achieve faster convergence and lower global error rates under constrained budgets.

title: Parameter Estimation of Intermittent Sampling Repeater Jamming Based on Orthogonal Block-Concatenated Waveform Design
authors: Chang Zhou, Wenfeng Chen, Zhu Tang, ALei Chen
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: Intermittent Sampling Repeater Jamming (ISRJ) achieves both deception and suppression effects by flexibly setting jamming parameters. Therefore, fast and high-precision estimation of ISRJ parameters is the premise for radar to implement effective anti-jamming. Existing estimation methods based on time-frequency analysis and ambiguity function transformation have problems such as high computational complexity and poor robustness at low jamming-to-noise ratios. To address these issues, this paper proposes a lightweight jamming parameter estimation method based on radar transmitter waveform optimization and receiver-side multi-block energy center calculation. This method uses deep learning to design a set of orthogonal phase-coded block sub-blocks with low autocorrelation sidelobes and low cross-correlation peaks, and splices them into a radar transmit waveform. When affected by ISRJ, based on the peak position offset and amplitude distribution of the matched filtering output of each sub-block, the Multi-block Energy Center Calculation (MEC) algorithm is used to calculate the jamming sampling period and duty cycle, avoiding complex transform domain operations. Simulation results show that Monte Carlo experiments verify the effectiveness of the proposed parameter estimation algorithm, which can provide an engineering implementation path for radar to sense ISRJ.

title: A Deep Learning Approach to Improving Multilingual Neural Machine Translation using Semantic and Linguistic Features
authors: Yaxi Su, Mohamad Yusof Darus, Norizan Mat Diah, Yuxia Du, Ling Li, Yang Xiao
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: This study presents a novel semantic-linguistic feature decoupling framework designed to significantly enhance the performance and generalization capabilities of multilingual neural machine translation (MNMT). By employing a specialized dual-channel feedforward network, the proposed approach explicitly separates language-agnostic semantic representations from language-specific linguistic features. This structural decoupling facilitates more robust cross-lingual knowledge transfer and effectively mitigates interference between diverse languages. Furthermore, the framework is integrated with a lightweight Transformer architecture that leverages dynamic sparse attention mechanisms and Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA). This combination ensures both exceptional computational efficiency and high-quality translation outputs. Comprehensive experimental evaluations on standard benchmarks such as IWSLT2017 demonstrate remarkable gains: the model achieves a substantial BLEU score improvement of +3.74 in zero-shot translation directions. Simultaneously, it optimizes operational efficiency by reducing memory usage by 62.5% and increasing inference speed threefold. Ultimately, this framework successfully addresses the critical issue of off-target generation, offering a scalable solution suitable for deployment in diverse resource-constrained environments.

title: Cognitive Networking using Deep Reinforcement Learning for Real-Time Optimization
authors: Jobin Thomas, Riya Sanjesh, Devi Priya V.S., Galiveeti Poornima
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Modern communication networks are becoming more complex due to factors such as the rapid proliferation of varied devices, changing traffic patterns, and tight quality-of-service standards. It is challenging to respond in real time with rule-based or model-driven network management in such dynamic contexts. Cognitive networking holds great promise as it enables networks to see, understand, and adjust autonomously. This study presents a cognitive networking framework for optimising networks in real-time that is based on deep reinforcement learning (DRL). Intelligent and adaptive optimisation of routing, resource allocation, and congestion control is possible when network control is viewed as a sequential decision-making issue. By interacting with the network environment and learning the necessary rules from experience, the framework adapts to new scenarios without network models. The experimental results demonstrate that cognitive networking based on DRL outperforms typical optimisation methods in terms of latency, throughput, and packet loss, all while being scalable and stable.

title: Research on Gridded Reconstruction and Generalization Capability of Sunshine Duration in Hubei Province
authors: Fei He, Chuandong Zhu, Peihua Xu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: Obtaining high-resolution gridded sunshine duration data in complex terrain is challenging due to the sparse distribution of ground stations. To overcome the extrapolation errors of traditional models, this paper proposes a two-step gridded reconstruction framework integrating astronomical physical constraints with machine learning. Driven by CLDAS high-resolution radiation products, the framework first establishes theoretical sunshine limits and truncation thresholds based on celestial mechanics. Subsequently, Random Forest (RF) and Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) are utilized to construct non-linear mappings of multi-dimensional spatiotemporal features. We innovatively evaluated the models using both temporal and rigorous spatial hold-out validations. Results indicate that XGBoost excels in temporal historical imputation at known coordinates <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{R}=0.857)$</tex>. However, in ungauged spatial estimation, multiple linear regression suffers catastrophic extrapolation collapse, whereas RF demonstrates superior spatial generalization robustness without topographical overfitting (RMSE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=1.842$</tex> hours). Ultimately, a high-precision 0.0625° gridded sunshine duration product for Hubei Province was successfully generated using the RF algorithm, providing a robust scientific paradigm for large-scale meteorological grid reconstruction.

title: A Multi-LLM Ensemble Approach for Evaluating Software Development Technologies
authors: Billal El Maalmi, Mohammed Lahmer
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Choosing a framework for a project is not obvious. Although surveys, expert panels, and targeted benchmarks provide guidance, those approaches are costly and difficult to implement. Moreover, they often fail to synchronise with evolving trends. To overcome this limitation, the MultiEval approach automates the evaluation protocol by leveraging six large language models (LLMs): GPT-5, Claude 4.5 Sonnet, Gemini 2.5 Pro, DeepSeek-V3.2, Qwen3-Max, and Grok 3. The approach uses a shared set of standardised prompts to generate ratings on a scale of 1 to 5 across six key dimensions: adoption, community support, learning curve, performance, security, and maturity. We then correlate those scores against GitHub and Stack Overflow signals to assess how well they mirror real-world ecosystem data. For backend frameworks, the six-model ensemble aligns with expert judgments 92% of the time ($\kappa=0.85$ [95% BCI: 0.81-0.88]) and outperforms every single model. Frontend scores exhibit higher variance because developer experience is inherently subjective, yet they remain strongly correlated with public ecosystem data ($r=0.98$ [95% BCI: 0.95-0.99]). A retrospective backtest that withheld all post-2020 information demonstrates the ensemble’s ability to identify long-term winners early (78% top-3 accuracy [95% BCI: 68-86 %]). Taken together, a heterogeneous model ensemble combined with a fixed scoring protocol offers a fast, repeatable method for rating software frameworks at scale.

title: Optimization potential in the production and end-of-life-management of electric drive units to increase resource efficiency
authors: Thorsten Ihne, Alexander Vogel, Manuela Ockel, Mario Sprenger, Roman Hahn, Marcel Baader, Nils Thielen, Jörg Franke, Florian Risch
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Production Technologies and Systems for E-Mobility (EPTS)
abstract: The electrification of road traffic enables significant reductions in greenhouse gas emissions and is therefore a key aspect of the industrial transformation strategy of many industrialized countries. A central component of electric vehicles is the electric traction drive, which is often designed as a functional unit consisting of the motor, a gearbox, and power electronics. The focus of product development for electric drives is particularly motivated by the maximization of energy efficiency during utilization, as this aspect has a major ecological and economic impact over the entire service life of the electric vehicle. However, to further improve ecological and economic sustainability, the entire product life cycle must be considered holistically. This applies to the resource and energy-efficient production of the drive unit and the processing of the end-of-life product by circular economy approaches. In this context, the present paper identifies and discusses optimization potentials regarding the production and the end-of-life-management of electric traction units. The focus here is on the electric motor and the inverter. Both subsystems have a complex manufacturing process chain and may contain critical raw materials. One main aspect that is often neglected in industrial practice is design for recycling or remanufacturing, where there are close interactions between product and process aspects. In this context, the conducted study aims to systematically identify and evaluate complications for component and material recovery using a reference series drive. Based on this, product and process innovations are discussed that allow sustainable and circular production of the drives without affecting the quality-relevant product properties.

title: A Sustainable Approach to Climate Change Mitigation and Energy Security Enhancement Through Biofuels Integrated with IoT
authors: Mohammad Maroof Siddiqui, Piyush Charan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: Biofuels consumes approached to be referred to as an substitution and renewable source of energy that has led to a decrease in the production of carbon dioxide besides the improvement in energy security in the world. The given paper presents the details on biofuels and the potential of applying IoT in the role of a smart technology to enhance the production of biofuels, distribution, and environmental impact analysis thereof. IoT sensors, cloud analytics, and real-time data processing can be used to enhance the efficiency of the biofuel systems and make them more transparent and adaptable. It has an accounting of different types of biofuels, state of art production routes, environmental effects, trends in technology and a case study on biofuel development in India. The paper has also proposed a smart biofuel systems methodology in IoT environment, which demonstrates the challenges, opportunities and future perspective towards research and implementation. The interrelation of IoT and biofuels is a game changer towards a more green, intelligent and secure energy infrastructure in the world.

title: Comparative Analysis of Machine Learning Models for Large-Scale Water Quality Assessment
authors: Anuj Kumar Jain, Manu Midha, Narinder Kaur, Geetanjali Sharma, Arushi Vaish, Shaveta Jain
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: Water quality monitoring is crucial for environmental sustainability as well as public health and traditional laboratory-based water quality assessment methods are time-consuming and costly and difficult to scale. This study introduces a machine learning based framework for efficient classification of water quality using a large scale environmental data. A dataset with more than 2.5 million data records after cleaning containing 8 most important physicochemical parameters, namely: pH, temperature, turbidity, nitrate, phosphate, hardness, chlorides and dissolved oxygen is used. Comprehensive preprocessing steps such as Cleaning of the data, Normalizing of data, Feature selection and Handling Imbalance in classes are taken up to make the data set rely-able data. Multiple machine learning models namely Logistic Regression, Support Vector Machine, Random Forest and Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LightGBM) are evaluated. Experimental results show that the LightGBM model is better than other methods and its accuracy is 98.6% and other evaluation indexes (precision, recall and F1-score) are better. Comparative analysis is a method to prove the robustness and scalability principle of the proposed mechanism in the case of big data. Furthermore, the trained model deployment is done using a Streamlit-based web interface for performing the water quality prediction in real-time. The proposed framework is a timely and cost-effective way of tackling the issue of water quality assessment with possible applications in environmental monitoring, policy making and public health management.

title: Early Detection of Hypokinetic Rigid Syndrome Through Vocal Biomarker Analysis Using Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Rohan Dhumane, Rahul Bhandekar, Prashant Tarone, Nilesh Nagrale, Jayesh Bisen, Jiwan N. Dehankar, Nutan Dhande
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Hypokinetic Rigid Syndrome leaves vocal traces before visible symptoms appear. This study demonstrates how artificial intelligence detects these changes, enabling earlier diagnosis. We analyzed 400 voice recordings examining 23 acoustic characteristics revealing hidden disease patterns. Using multiple machine learning approaches, Convolutional Neural Networks achieved 97% accuracy distinguishing healthy from Parkinson's-affected voices, outperforming K- Nearest Neighbors and Support Vector Machines. CNNs overcome classical limitations by automatically learning complex patterns without handcrafted features. Voice recordings are non-invasive, inexpensive, and easily collected, ideal for widespread screening and telemedicine. While data standardization and validation challenges remain, this research advances accessible early detection tools deployable via smartphones, giving patients crucial intervention time.

title: Differentiated Warning Method for Wildfires in Transmission Corridors Based on Multi-Dimensional Visual Features
authors: Aihui Wen, Jun Wu, Enze Zhou, Siyan Lin, You Zhou
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: To overcome the limitations of single-dimensional warning, this paper proposes a vision-based differentiated warning method for wildfire in transmission corridors. First, an improved lightweight YOLOv8, incorporating DySConv and DyHead, is developed to enhance the detection of small smoke and fire targets in complex backgrounds. Second, a multi-feature extraction algorithm, fusing monocular spatial ranging with RGB+YCbCr/HSV color spaces, is designed to accurately quantify flame distance, height, and intensity. Finally, by integrating these visual features into the Byram combustion and three-interval insulation tripping models, a comprehensive warning matrix based on "arrival time margin" and "dynamic tripping probability" is established. This approach provides proactive decision support for differentiated emergency response in power dispatch centers.

title: Real-Time MPPT Optimization for PV-Battery Hybrid Systems in EV Fast Charging Infrastructure
authors: Madhumathi R M, Bharath M, Mahadevan V, Thanagan K, Udayan K
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: The development of electric vehicle (EV) fast charging infrastructure is required that uses renewable energy sources as the source of power." This paper addresses the research work carried out with the objective of developing a real-time maximum power point tracking (MPPT) optimization technique for solar photovoltaic (PV) – battery storage systems for electric vehicle fast charging applications. A single DC–DC converter is used as part of the proposed system for MPPT tracking as well as battery charging control, making the complete system more efficient as well as cost-effective for developing electric vehicle fast charging infrastructure.

title: Effect of Dipole Length on the Design and Performance of High Permittivity Materials in 7 T MRI
authors: Vincenzo Miranda, Giuseppe Carluccio, Giuseppe Ruello, Riccardo Lattanzi, Daniele Riccio
publish: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)
abstract: At 7T MRI, radiofrequency field inhomogeneity and limited penetration arise from wavelength shortening in biological tissues. High permittivity materials (HPMs) have been proposed as a passive solution to improve transmit efficiency and signal-to-noise ratio by reshaping the electromagnetic field distribution. However, their design remains largely simulation-driven, with limited physical insight into their interaction with realistic RF sources. An analytical scattering framework based on canonical geometries has recently been introduced to provide a physics-based interpretation of HPM behavior and to identify optimal material parameters. Here, we investigate the robustness of this framework under different RF excitations by analyzing the effect of dipole length on the performance of a spherical HPM at 7T. Analytical predictions obtained with an elementary electric dipole are compared with full-wave simulations using a realistic half-wavelength dipole. Results show that, at the sphere center, the optimal permittivity remains essentially invariant to substantial variations in dipole length, despite differences in the transmit magnetic field distribution. This behavior is attributed to spherical symmetry and to the modal nature of the scattering process governing field enhancement. These findings support the proposed analytical framework as a robust and physically grounded tool for HPM design in ultra-high-field MRI.

title: Learning-Driven Job Recommendation Systems: A Comprehensive Review of Deep Semantic, Transformer-Enhanced, Ontology-based and NLP-Bidirectional Approaches
authors: Velusamy A, Aishwarya S, Chellam N, Devipriya R, Prabhu R, Vasudevan I
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Deep learning, semantic matching, natural language processing (NLP), and transformer-based systems have significantly improved job recommendation systems. Such developments respond to the increased complexity of the job market, the large data about high dimensional skills, dynamically changing employed requirements, and the need of increasingly individualized employment support. The review is based on the contributions of recent literature in the area of matched representation learning, semantic structure modeling, attention mechanisms, transformer-based framework, reciprocal recommendation model, ontology-enhanced system, and resume fusion system. Ten advanced methodologies are compared in terms of data modeling strategy, interpretability, bidirectionality, scalability, adaptability to labor-market development, and relevance to different groups of people such as college graduates, migrants, and vocational learners. The paper also outlines some of the major constraints of classical job-matching pipelines and suggests a hybrid architecture, which combines semantic skill extraction, contextual modeling through transformers, ontology-based filtering, and the two-way resume-job matching. Comparative findings demonstrate higher accuracy, relevance ranking, explainability, and robustness. Future directions include multimodal-resumes-understanding, adaptivity through reinforcement-learning, cross-regional-labor-market-intelligence-engine-based.

title: A Coefficient Feature Matching–Based Method for Single-Phase-to-Ground Fault Location in Active Distribution Networks
authors: Shoudong Xu, Zixin Li, Jinxin Ouyang, Xiaofu Xiong, Bofei Yang
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: The large-scale integration of distributed generation alters the fault current distribution characteristics of distribution networks, which significantly increases the difficulty of locating single-phase-to-ground faults. This paper proposes a coefficient feature matching–based method for single-phase-to-ground fault location in active distribution networks. By constructing the positive-sequence augmented network of an active distribution system, the intrinsic relationship among the fault location, the feeder outlet, and the positive-sequence current injected by distributed generation is analytically derived, and a generalized fault location model is established. The proposed method formulates location criteria by exploiting the distinctive features of current vector proportion coefficients under different faulted sections, thereby enabling rapid identification of the faulted section and accurate distance estimation. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method is insensitive to fault location, transition resistance, and the number of distributed generation units, exhibiting superior location performance and providing effective technical support for the rapid handling of faults in active distribution networks.

title: Data-driven Prediction of California Bearing Ratio Using Fuzzy C-Means Clustering
authors: Yogesh Iyer Murthy, Nikhat Raza Khan, Syed Rameez Ali, Rajesh Tiwari, Shalini Dubey, Priya Shrivastava
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: The current research presents the application of Fuzzy Clustering Method (FCM) with Sugeno Type I to develop the prediction model for the soaked California Bearing Ratio (CBR) of black cotton soil. In this research, 211 samples of soil were investigated experimentally. The prediction models were developed using proportion of fractions below and above 0.75 mm in size, optimum moisture content (OMC), maximum dry density (MDD), plasticity index (PI), shrinkage limit (SL), and swell pressure as input parameters. Soaked CBR values were considered as output. The defuzzification of rules was carried out using weighted average approach. The results thus obtained were further tuned using genetic algorithm with 1050 configurable parameters. The results obtained by this hybrid algorithm presented excellent predictability with an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.945.

title: Toward Portable and Energy-Efficient Home Hemodialysis: Clinical, Economic, and Technical Review with Engineering Perspectives
authors: Ilyasse Boujraf, Yassine Zahidi, Mohamed El Moufid, Hassan Gziri, Wafaa Dachry, Hicham Medromi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Home hemodialysis (HHD) offers a promising alternative to in-center hemodialysis (ICHD), providing improved quality of life and treatment flexibility. This review presents a comparative analysis of HHD versus ICHD with respect to cost-effectiveness and quality of life, based on a structured literature search in the Scopus database. In addition, a comprehensive technical evaluation of four commercial HHD systems (Quanta SC+, NxStage System One, Physidia $\mathrm{S}^{3}$, and Tablo) is conducted, focusing on key design parameters including weight and dimensions, dialysate delivery strategies, circuit configurations, consumables requirements, and portability characteristics. The analysis highlights three major, interconnected technological challenges in current HHD systems: high dialysate consumption, significant energy demand, and extensive use of disposable consumables, collectively impacting system cost and portability. Proposed directions are intended to serve as a structured engineering perspective to guide future research. Experimental validation, system modeling, and prototype implementation are identified as necessary steps toward developing cost-effective, energyefficient, and portable HHD technologies.

title: Optimal Control for Managing the Dynamics of Two Computer Virus Co-Infection
authors: Bashir Abdullahi Baba
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This study presents a mathematical model describing the simultaneous spread of two distinct computer viruses within a networked system, taking into account vertical transmission for one virus as well as treatment strategies for both infections. The model is used to derive the basic reproduction number of the combined system, which helps determine the conditions under which the infections either die out or persist. We analyze the disease-free and endemic equilibrium states to better understand the longterm dynamics of the system. To identify effective mitigation strategies, optimal control theory is incorporated into the model, and Pontryagin’s Maximum Principle is applied to establish the necessary conditions for minimizing infection levels and associated intervention costs. Numerical simulations are performed to demonstrate how the proposed control measures influence the spread of infection. The results indicate that combining equilibrium analysis with carefully designed control strategies can substantially reduce infection prevalence while keeping costs at a reasonable level, providing useful insights for managing co-infections in computer networks.

title: Influence of Electric Field Strength on the Dielectric Response of Epoxy Resin foe Electrical Insulation
authors: Qian Wang, Ting Lei, Qiulin Chen, Jiahe Yu, Haohan Zhou, Chao Wu
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: Epoxy resin is one of the most widely-used polymer dielectrics for electrical insulation. Polarization properties are vital to the electric field distribution and the heat management of equipment. Polarization of dielectric is usually investigated by dielectric response, while the voltage applied in previous research is much lower to that in practical operation, which affects the universality of conclusions. Therefore, in this research, the dielectric response of typical epoxy resin under different field strengths is measured, which reaches the magnitude of actual operation. The influence of field strength on different micro polarization processes is revealed, which suggests a linear polarization trend. The charges involved in the polarization processes are found to be mainly thermal-activated, which means the predominant factor of polarization performance is temperature. The findings herein would prominently benefit the design and the maintenance of epoxy-based high voltage equipment.

title: Comparative Analysis of Enhanced Weighted A* and Theta* Search Algorithms on Pacman Mazes
authors: Shlok Burmi, Sujal Kishore, Shweta Malwe, Prachi Mehta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: Pathfinding is a fundamental element in a number of AI applications in the form of games and navigation. The algorithms that we tested in this study are DFS, BFS, UCS, A, as well as Weighted A* and Theta* with the help of the Berkeley Pac-Man Environment. Our results justify the claim that the effect of heuristic is used in Weighted A* to perform better in terms of speed, since with a negligible decrease in the quality of the path, we were able to achieve speed improvement by almost 30 percent. Theta* services acquire smoothing paths at the expense of increased computation by path line-of-sight checks. The tests used varied in the sizes of the mazes to use. The comparison was done according to the path cost, time, memory and the nodes explored. All approaches had a trade off between speed and accuracy. By looking at the results in general the average run times with small margin of difference (+-2% ) indicate that both the algorithms operate in a consistent manner. The process of determining the usage of memory in all the sizes of the maze assists us in comparing the cost as well as the real comparison of the most feasible method.

title: A Unified Multi-Dynamics Framework for Perception-Oriented Modeling in Tendon-Driven Continuum Robots
authors: Ibrahim Alsarraj, Yuhao Wang, Abdalla Swikir, Cesare Stefanini, Dezhen Song, Zhanchi Wang, Ke Wu
abstract: Tendon-driven continuum robots offer intrinsically safe and contact-rich interactions owing to their kinematic redundancy and structural compliance. However, their perception often depends on external sensors, which increase hardware complexity and limit scalability. This work introduces a unified deterministic multi-dynamics modeling framework for tendon-driven continuum robotic systems, exemplified by a spiral-inspired robot named Spirob. The framework integrates motor electrical dynamics, motor–winch dynamics, and continuum robot dynamics into a coherent system model. Within this framework, motor signals such as current and angular displacement are modeled to expose the electromechanical signatures of external interactions, enabling perception grounded in intrinsic dynamics. The model captures and validates key physical behaviors of the real system, including actuation hysteresis and self-contact at motion limits. Building on this foundation, the framework is applied to environmental interaction: first for passive contact detection, verified experimentally against simulation data; then for active contact sensing, where control and perception strategies from simulation are successfully applied to the real robot; and finally for object size estimation, where a policy learned in simulation is directly deployed on hardware. The results demonstrate that the proposed framework provides a physically grounded way to interpret interaction signatures from intrinsic motor signals in tendon-driven continuum robots.

title: Toward a Soft Robotic Gripper Integrating High Force Grasping and Dexterous Manipulation: The SOFRo Gripper_supp2-3693142.mp4
authors: Dongjun Shin
abstract: Developing a robotic hand that integrates high fingertip force, rapid response, and multi-degree-of-freedom (DoF) motion to achieve human hand-like capabilities remains a challenge in the field of robotic hands. This study presents the SOFRo Gripper, a soft, high-force robotic gripper designed to combine these capabilities within a compact pneumatic architecture. The finger module of the SOFRo gripper incorporates synergistically arranged chambers together with a multinode tendon routing strategy that distributes actuation forces, enabling both flexion and abduction/adduction motions while enhancing fingertip force. Furthermore, a Force-Enhanced Pleated (FEP) mechanism was applied to the chambers to increase fingertip force, resulting in a 32.41% improvement over conventional chamber designs. The proposed SOFRo gripper achieves a high fingertip force of 68.76 N and dexterous motion capabilities, enabling a maximum lifting force of 400.0 N and in-hand manipulation. To validate its versatility, extensive experiments were conducted across a wide range of tasks, including object grasping, high-force manipulation (e.g., lifting heavy objects, closing a valve), delicate handling (e.g., grasping tofu, inserting a light bulb), and high-speed manipulation (e.g., spinning a top, catching a ball). These results demonstrate the SOFRo Gripper’s ability to combine high force with versatile soft robotic manipulation.

title: Enhancement of Tumor-Treating Field Efficacy by Time-Varying Frequency Sweeping
authors: Yushi Kawamura, Kosaku Kurata
abstract: Objective: This study aimed to enhance the therapeutic efficacy of tumor-treating fields (TTFs) by replacing a single fixed frequency with a time-varying, frequency-swept electric field. Methods: Human lung carcinoma A549 cells were exposed for 72 h to alternating electric fields (3 V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">p–p</sub>/cm) generated between parallel electrodes. Fixed frequencies of 150 and 300 kHz were compared with continuously swept fields ranging from 100 to 400 kHz at modulation rates of 0.6–100 kHz/s. Cell proliferation was quantified using a tetrazolium-based viability assay, and intracellular electric-field nonuniformity around a dividing-cell geometry was analyzed by finite-element modeling. Results: Fixed-frequency exposure caused only minor, non-significant reductions in cell number, whereas frequency sweeping at 0.6–2.5 kHz/s reduced proliferation by more than 50%. Numerical analyses showed that the effective frequency range associated with pronounced electric-field redistribution near the cleavage furrow depends on cell diameter, ranging from 200 to 450 kHz for the observed size distribution. Frequency sweeping effectively encompassed multiple effective bands, suppressing heterogeneous cell populations without bias toward particular sizes. The most effective modulation rates coincided with the timescales of cell retraction and mitosis, suggesting that temporal synchronization between frequency variation and cellular events maximizes efficacy. Conclusions: Frequency-swept TTFs provide a robust and practical strategy to overcome tumor heterogeneity, significantly improving treatment performance compared with fixed-frequency application. Significance: Adjusting the time-dependent sweep of the applied electric-field frequency to match tumor-specific cellular characteristics may improve reproducibility and therapeutic outcomes in clinical TTF protocols.

title: Optimizing Server Placement for Vertical Federated Learning in Dynamic Edge/Fog Networks_supp1-3700898.pdf
authors: Su Wang
abstract: We investigate the control and optimization of vertical federated learning (VFL), a class of distributed machine learning (ML) methods in which edge/fog devices contain separate data features, in dynamic edge/fog networks. Owing to heterogeneous data features and hardware across edge/fog networks, devices’ contributions to VFL vary substantially, and, moreover, dynamic edge/fog networks can lead to the permanent exit or entry of select data features. In this setting, our proposed methodology, server controlled VFL in dynamic networks (SC-DN), first establishes the existence of a global first-order stationary point for every global round, and then leverages this result to jointly optimize ML model training and resource consumption based on four key control variables: (i) server placement, (ii) device-to-server transmit power, (iii) local device processor frequency, and (iv) local training iterations per global round. The resulting optimization formulation contains coupled variables as well as numerous forms of logarithmic constraints which we show is a mixed-integer signomial program, an NP-hard problem, and for which we develop a general solver. Finally, via experiments on both image and multi-modal datasets, we show that our methodology demonstrates superior classification/regression performance and resource consumption savings than even greedy methodologies.

title: Federated Clustering: An Overview of Algorithm Evolution and Research Prospects
authors: Shifei Ding, Chao Li, Lili Guo, Qiang Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: As a new paradigm that integrates clustering with federated learning, federated clustering (FC) has recently attracted increasing attention, as it addresses the practical issue of privacy protection in distributed data. In this paper, we provide a comprehensive survey of recent advances in FC. This survey is organized into four parts. First, since FC is often developed by extending existing clustering methods, we review several classical clustering paradigms. Meanwhile, the inherent challenges of FC are summarized, and common improvement strategies are categorized. Second, we summarize experimental setups and evaluation protocols used in FC studies. Third, from the perspectives of data partitioning schemes and whether deep representation learning is incorporated, FC methods are divided into four categories, and representative algorithms in each category are reviewed. Finally, we discuss the limitations of current FC approaches and highlight potential directions for future research.

title: Data Visualization for Teenage Teaching: A Review of Current Applications, Pedagogical Strategies, and Impact on Learning_supp1-3700620.xlsx
authors: Xingyu Lan
abstract: Data visualization has long been recognized and applied as a powerful tool for education. However, systematic reviews within the visualization community focusing on this specific area remain limited, and existing studies have focused more on higher education or early childhood education. In this work, we focus on a particular stage of education: teenage education, where students are undergoing significant changes in cognitive abilities and facing increased curriculum demands, making visualization a crucial support tool. We are particularly interested in two research questions: (i) What is the existing method space of implementing visualization in teenage teaching, and (ii) How are the effects of the implemented methods? Driven by these questions, we reviewed 112 papers that provide valuable empirical evidence on how data visualization has been applied in teenage education and analyze three main aspects: (i) visualization applications (e.g., visualization types, forms, tools, educational goals); (ii) its pedagogical integration with curricula and teaching activities; and (iii) how it affects learning performance and user experience. Based on our analysis, we discuss a set of key trends, such as a predominant focus on STEM disciplines and the increasing integration of AR/VR and AI technologies with visualization. We also highlight various implementation challenges, such as the gaps between professional visualization tools and the actual needs of educational settings, as well as the complexity of student characteristics. Through this review, we hope to provide visualization researchers, developers, and educators with in-depth insights to better align the use of visualization with the developmental needs of teenage learners.

title: SFMamba: Social-Intention-Aware Human Trajectory Prediction with Social Force State Space Model
authors: Ziyang Ren, Ping Wei, Jin Yang, Haowen Tang, Huan Li
abstract: Human trajectory prediction is crucial for understanding human social behavior and motion intentions. Existing studies have often integrated social interaction and intent-driving features to predict changes in motion states, but overlook their varying impacts on specific motion states at different time nodes. In this paper, we propose a novel framework called Social Force Mamba (SFMamba) to tackle these issues for trajectory prediction. This framework constructs a social force state space model by integrating the motion differential equations and the social force model. It represents motion trajectories as transitions from hidden motion states to surface motion states and introduces the Social Mamaba Encoder to extract hidden motion states driven by social interaction and motion intentions. It projects surface motion states into coarse-grained future motion states and combines the Goal Mamba Decoder to refine motion transitions guided by goal intentions. By integrating the selection mechanism to adaptively regulate the injection of physical attributes at different time nodes, our approach models the social-intentionaware state transition process for motion trajectory prediction. Our approach achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) results on ETHUCY and SportVU NBA datasets while effectively simulating real human movement with physical interpretability.

title: Different-frequency Differential Protection Method of Adjustable Speed Hydro-Generating Unit Based on Fixed Characteristic Frequency Band
authors: Jian Qiao, Haoyuan Yu, Yikai Wang, Yifan Zhao, Xin Yin, Zhihui Dai, Xianggen Yin
abstract: During the operation of Adjustable speed hydro-generating unit (ASHU), due to high speed and large centrifugal force, the rotor winding is easily squeezed and causes short-circuit fault. The development of a reliable protection scheme is imperative. As a new and potential method, the universality of different-frequency differential protection is still limited under wide speed operation conditions. Therefore, a rotor winding short-circuit protection method based on fixed characteristic frequency band is proposed in this paper. Firstly, the internal relationship between the harmonic characteristics of rotor current and slip rate in the case of winding short-circuit fault of ASHU is analyzed. On this basis, the construction method of different-frequency differential current protection criterion based on fixed characteristic frequency band is proposed. Furthermore, combined with the waveform of the dynamic simulation experiment, the Generalized S Transform (GST) is used to carry out time-frequency analysis. The feasibility of the construction approach of the protection criterion is verified. Finally, the experimental results show that the proposed protection method is suitable for the wide speed operation condition of ASHU. It can achieve fast action under rotor winding short-circuit fault (RWSF), and has high selectivity under stator winding short-circuit fault (SWSF), which can effectively avoid protection misoperation.

title: Image Fusion with Quantum Processing for Scene Classification using the BigEarthNet Dataset
authors: Leslie Miller, Glen S Uehara, Niraj Anil Babar, Andreas Spanias
abstract: This paper presents algorithms, simulations, and results on the use of optical and Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) quantum image fusion and machine learning algorithms for radar and remote sensing applications. We propose novel quantum image fusion techniques that extract meaningful features for identifying and classifying scenes obtained from the BigEarthNet dataset. Specifically, this work focuses on multi-label scene classification using quantum image fusion of C-band SAR and optical imagery from the BigEarthNet dataset. The proposed fusion techniques enhance and sharpen structural and textural details that are not readily observable in raw SAR imagery, thereby improving downstream classification performance. To enable quantum image fusion, we design new quantum circuits that employ unitary and Pauli gates for phase encoding of pixel intensity values. These circuits allow optical and SAR image information to be encoded and fused within a quantum processing framework. Three fusion strategies are investigated, resulting in three distinct quantum image fusion models. Comparative evaluation of these models demonstrates that pixel-averaging–based quantum fusion yields improved classification accuracy relative to alternative fusion approaches. The fused images are subsequently processed using deep classical neural networks to perform scene classification. Our neural network study begins by customizing the Visual Geometry Group 16 (VGG16) architecture for multi-label remote sensing classification tasks. In addition, we examine the use of a Residual Network 50 (ResNet-50) architecture and provide comparative performance analysis between the two models. The effects of quantum noise on the fusion and classification pipeline are also studied to assess robustness under realistic quantum processing conditions. To evaluate classification performance, we employ standard metrics such as F1 and F2 scores, and additionally introduce false discovery rate and false negative rate metrics to provide a more comprehensive assessment of classification accuracy, particularly in multi-label settings. Overall, the results demonstrate the effectiveness of optical and SAR quantum image fusion in enhancing scene classification performance and highlight the potential of integrating quantum image processing with deep learning for advanced remote sensing applications.

title: A Cooperative Scheme for Voltage Ripple Extraction and Suppression in DC Distribution Networks Based on TTAO-VMD and Improved DC-APF
authors: Yi Zhang, Menghan Wang, Baojiang Wu, Sian Chen
abstract: Voltage ripple is a significant power-quality concern in DC distribution networks, and effective suppression requires precise extraction of the ripple components. Traditional methods, such as low-pass filtering, often suffer from inaccurate band division and limited dynamic adaptability, which constrain the performance of DC active power filters. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a collaborative governance scheme with two main innovations. First, a triangular topology aggregation optimizer (TTAO) is introduced to adaptively adjust the penalty factor and modal number in variational mode decomposition (VMD), minimizing envelope entropy for high-precision, real-time ripple extraction. Second, an improved parallel active DC filter (DC-APF) based on a buck-boost topology is developed, utilizing a dual-loop control strategy that integrates feedforward and feedback for rapid, accurate compensation current tracking. Simulation results show that the TTAO-VMD algorithm effectively prevents modal aliasing and achieves a maximum absolute ripple extraction error of less than 0.02 V. The DC-APF, using this accurate signal, reduces the DC bus voltage ripple peak-to-peak value from 32 V to 0.3 V (99.06% suppression) and lowers total harmonic distortion from 1.85% to 0.17%. These results demonstrate the scheme’s effectiveness for ripple control in DC distribution networks.

title: Why Large Language Models Alone Fall Short for Responsible Learner Modelling in K–12 Tutoring: A Case Study
authors: Danial Hooshyar, Yeongwook Yang, Gustav Šír, Tommi Kärkkäinen, Raija Hämäläinen, Mutlu Cukurova, Roger Azevedo
abstract: The rapid rise of large language model (LLM)-based tutors in K–12 education has led to the misconception that generative models can replace traditional learner modelling and act as general-purpose engines for adaptive instruction. This is especially problematic in K–12 settings, which the EU AI Act classifies as a high-risk domain requiring responsible design. Motivated by concerns surrounding the role of learner modelling in responsible AI-powered tutoring, this study synthesises existing research evidence on key limitations of LLM-based tutoring systems and then presents an empirical case study investigating one critical aspect of these concerns: the accuracy, reliability, and temporal coherence of assessing learners’ evolving knowledge over time. To this end, we compare a deep knowledge tracing (DKT) model with a widely used LLM (with and without fine-tuning) that has demonstrated competitive performance in tutoring-related tasks, using a large-scale open-access dataset. Our findings show that DKT achieves the highest discrimination performance (AUC = 0.83) on next-step correctness prediction and consistently outperforms the LLM across evaluation settings. Although fine-tuning improves the LLM’s AUC by about 8% over the zero-shot baseline, it still remains 6% below DKT and produces higher early-sequence errors, precisely where incorrect predictions would be most harmful for adaptive learner support. Temporal-coherence analyses further reveal that while DKT maintains stable, directionally correct mastery updates, LLM variants display substantial temporal weaknesses, including smooth but wrong-direction updates, and, even after fine-tuning, remain inconsistent and unable to match DKT’s temporal stability. We also illustrate that these issues persist even though fine-tuned LLMs required nearly 198 hours of continuous high-compute training, far exceeding the computational demands of the lightweight DKT model. Our qualitative analysis of multi-skill mastery estimation further shows that, even after fine-tuning, the LLM produced unstable and inconsistent mastery trajectories, whereas DKT maintained smooth and coherent multi-skill updates. Collectively, these findings suggest that LLMs alone are unlikely to achieve the same positive effect sizes observed in decades long intelligent tutoring systems literature. Rather than replacing learner modelling, LLMs may be more appropriately deployed as pedagogical interfaces or content generators paired with dedicated learner modelling components to ensure responsible, accurate, reliable, and pedagogically sound support.

title: Effects of Magnetic Powder Filling Ratio on the Characteristics of a Magnetic Composite Core Motor
authors: Takashi Nakamura, Ren Washioka, Kenichiro Ochiai, Tsutomu Mizuno, Mitsuhide Sato
abstract: Magnetic-composite materials, comprising soft magnetic powder and a resin binder, are characterized by excellent machinability and low iron loss. Motors with stator cores made of magnetic-composite materials (magnetic composite core (MCC) motors) can reduce iron loss and permanent magnet eddy current loss, which are critical under high-speed operation. This study investigates the effects of magnetic powder filling ratio on the characteristics of MCC motors. First, the torque characteristics of prototype motors were experimentally evaluated. The analytical results indicate that high-filling MCC motors can improve efficiency in low-speed, high-torque regions, whereas low-filling MCC motors can improve efficiency in high-speed, low-torque regions due to reductions in iron loss and spatial harmonics. Furthermore, the improvement in the temperature characteristics resulting from high-filling MCCs was clarified through thermal analysis.
