title: Beyond Representation: Mechanisms of Culturally Affirming Mentorship for Black and Latinx Students in STEM
authors: Isabella Lopez, Maria Sanchez, Sanorita Dey
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 Conference on Research on Equity and Sustained Participation in Engineering, Computing, and Technology
abstract: None

title: Autonomous Blanket Covering for Service Robots
authors: Song Jiang, Bin Zou
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: Elderly individuals often unconsciously move their limbs during sleep, leaving parts of their body exposed. To maintain their body temperature, it is necessary to promptly recover the exposed areas with a blanket. However, manual caregiving is difficult to sustain in terms of continuous and timely response. To address this, we propose an autonomous blanket recovering system for a service robot designed to assist bedridden elderly patients. A multi-objective scoring mechanism based on body part coverage rates is constructed in a physics-based simulation environment, upon which a blanket re-covering policy is trained via reinforcement learning. An attachment block mechanism is introduced to mitigate mesh penetration issues arising from deformable object manipulation in simulation. Three categories of covering tasks are defined targeting the upper body and both arms, the lower limbs, and the full body respectively, with quantitative evaluation conducted based on surface coverage results and generalization validated through randomized testing of body shape parameters and initial blanket poses.

title: Household Vulnerability Clustering Analysis Using K-Means Algorithm
authors: Yousra Harb, Mohammad Rashdan, Zaid Alkhear Aburumman, Mohamed Abuseini
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: This study underscores the importance of utilizing data science in socioeconomic assessment. Particularly, in poverty and vulnerability profiles in developed economies. This research evaluates the effectiveness of using K-means clustering algorithm combined with Kruskal-Wallis Test for household poverty and vulnerability assessment in USA. Using data from 9152 households including 27 socioeconomic indicators, K-means clustering algorithm identified four distinct vulnerability clusters: critical, high, moderate, and low. K-means clustering algorithm results accuracy was evaluated using Silhouette Score (0.434) and Davies-Bouldin Index (1.086), demonstrating reasonably defined and separated clusters. The Kruskal-Wallis Test results confirmed that the clusters represent distinct household vulnerability profiles. The clustering results revealed that households in USA reside in positions that range from poverty to vulnerability to resilience. The clustering result theoretically matters because it classifies households in different welfare states and rejects the binary classification of poverty. The findings also provide insights into data-driven poverty and vulnerability reduction strategies.

title: Comprehensive Review on Satellite-Based Crop Monitoring and Weed Detection Techniques, Challenges and Future Trends for Smart Agriculture
authors: S. Girinath, V. Anantha Natarajan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Increased demand of a sustainable food production combined with pressure of limited agricultural production has compelled the agricultural sector to a technological advancement. The most powerful remedies of enhancing crop management decision making have been identified to be remote sensing and machine learning (ML). The review of the terrain looks at the approaches that are aimed at resolving two of the key issues in smart agriculture, which are the prediction of crop yields and weeding. The predictors are usually vegetation indices such as the NDVI, EVI by reference to the multispectral satellite image. Random Forest Regression, time-series analysis and integration of simulation models have been identified to be applicable in the early season crop production prediction. Meanwhile, deep learning architectures have helped in the detection and segmentation of images associated with weeds to aid in the spraying of fields and ensure that the field is well maintained, e.g. CNNs and U-Net. Further, the paper points out that interpretable models and scaled deployment schemes are needed, particularly where the resources are low. The current models are analyzed in-depth with the assistance of the following metrics of RMSE, R 2, Precision, Recall and IoU, which assist in identifying the predictive performance and the classification performance. The review concludes by formulating the new trends in the future of smart agriculture which are multi-sensor data fusion, edge computing and explainable AI. The proposed paper will assist policy makers, developers and researchers to develop more resilient, scalable and farmer friendly systems of agricultural intelligence by synthesizing and mapping gaps of this area.

title: Selecting Educational Robots for Morocco’s STM: Curriculum Alignment and One-Year FLL Validation
authors: Zahrou Younes, Zidani Younes, Nissabouri Salah, Mansouri Khalifa
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Educational robotics (ER) is widely promoted to support STEM learning through hands-on experimentation. However, platform selection is often driven by datasheets, popularity, or ecosystem visibility rather than curriculum requirements and classroom constraints. In Morocco’s Sciences et Technologies Mécaniques (STM) program-structured around information and energy chains and supported by project-based practice-decision-makers lack both curriculumspecific evaluation instruments and ecologically valid evidence on implementation feasibility. This paper addresses these two gaps through a three-phase methodology. First, STM requirements are operationalized into a reproducible curriculum-alignment rubric with six dimensions: Information Chain (CI), Energy Chain (CE), Pedagogical Appropriateness (PED), Project Support (PCE), Economic Accessibility (ECO), and Institutional Support (INST). Second, the rubric is applied to ten representative platforms selected from a structured techno-functional corpus of 51 robots, producing interpretable alignment profiles and weighted rankings. Third, ecological validation is provided through a one-year longitudinal singlecase field study of LEGO SPIKE Prime in the First LEGO League (FLL) Morocco context. Field observations reveal adoption-critical constraints that are not captured by datasheets: ecosystem lock-in limiting customization, sensor unreliability (35% color-detection failure), higher-thanexpected scaffolding needs ($8 \mathrm{~h} /$ week vs. 4 h anticipated), and strong underestimation of task development time ($\mathbf{1 2 - 1 5 ~ h}$ vs. 24 h). We conclude with actionable “classroom readiness” indicators and a phased procurement strategy for STM programs operating under resource constraints.

title: Optimal User-Driven Performance Governance for Server-Side Rendered Web Applications
authors: Harshendra Gite
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: To enhance the first load time, search engine visibility, and user experience, server-side rendered (SSR) web applications are commonly used but uniform behavior with variable user behavior and server workloads are difficult to maintain. The current performance assessment methods are very dependent on fixed benchmarks or synthetic testing that do not reflect the actual user interactions and are not adaptively optimized. In order to address these shortcomings, this paper proposes the best user-based performance governance to server-side rendered web applications which combine real-user performance measures with intelligent learning and optimization. The systematic feature identification, expert-based screening, and correlation analysis is used to produce a compact and meaningful set of performance measures based on real-user monitoring data to create a refined dataset. Deep linear perceptron (DLP) model is employed in learning relationships between the performance indicators and the overall quality of the page in order to predict the performance accurately. The feature selection and model parameter tuning are performed by a modified pelican optimization (MPO) algorithm that ensures a higher accuracy of prediction as well as less redundancy. These findings demonstrate that the EPO-optimized DLP model has a high accuracy, stability and computational efficiency as compared to non-optimized models. The results show that the use of user-driven analytics and metaheuristic optimization allows the proactive performance governance and facilitates adaptive decision-making in SSR environments.

title: Hybrid Ensemble-based AI Intrusion Detection Framework Leveraging Sequential Deep Learning and Feature-Optimized Machine Learning for Real-Time Multi-Tier Network Threat Classification
authors: A Ajeeth, A Sivanesan, Boddu Prabhas, N V Sagar Varaprasad, Avaneesh R, R Sri Sabarish
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Rising frequency and sophistication of cyber threats are highly challenging to the security of the current network infrastructure. The signature based intrusion detection systems are quite conventional and are not capable of detecting new or emerging attacks since they are based on fixed patterns. The Artificial Intelligence (AI)-based network Intrusion Detection System that is proposed in the given paper aims at the reactive detection of malicious actions in real-time on the basis of the machine learning and deep learning algorithms. The recommended framework has an organized workflow based on gathering, preprocessing of data, feature engineering, training of the model, classifying threats, and automatic alerting. The analysis of the network traffic features through random forest classifiers and their selection of the significant features are used whereas Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks help to identify the patterns in the sequences of the network traffic flows and the action of specific attacks. An ensemble learning approach integrates the prediction of two or more models to enhance finding accuracy, lower a false positive and increase the overall stability of the system. There is also a multi-level threat classification system which allows quick automated response but also make security-critical incidents be verified by humans. This is confirmed by experimental assessment revealing the system is able to offer high detector accuracy, low latency, and high scalability, thus, becoming a realistic and affordable application of the approach to offer real-time protection against cybersecurity in contemporary networked settings.

title: Graph Learning-Enhanced U-Net for Early Gastric Cancer Detection and Segmentation
authors: K.Menaga, Chamundeeswari R M, P. Pandiyarajan, C. Preethibha, Machiraju Sivakumarraju, B.Beaula Pinky
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Early Gastric Cancer (EGC) is not a disease that is easily identified when it happens since it is not visible and has no distinct appearance in case a patient undergoes the gastroscopy process, and therefore it is not easily diagnosed with less than 10 percent. Diagnostic process, we propose an improved U-Net architecture modified to identify and separate gastroscopic EGC. The model integrates recently developed U-Net-based loss function to enhance the representation of the multi-scale features and outline the lesion boundary. The proposed research evaluates a U-Net architecture with Graph Learning (GLIU-NET) which can be trained to detect and localize Early Gastric Cancer (EGC) inside gastroscopic images automatically. Comparative analysis of its performance and the four baseline models revealed that it was better on all the measures. It performed a Dice Score of 0.89, IoU of 0.83, F1-Score of 0.88 and Edge Accuracy of 0.87 that was better than the best baseline (MedFormer, +0.02 Dice, +0.02 IoU, and +0.03 Edge Accuracy). There was also a graphical ablation study that determined the significance of the model graphical reasoning and bi-directional fusion that indicated that performance losses were up to 4 percent when either of the two was removed. Clinical validation by clinical experts indicated that, the level of clinical acceptability was 93 percent and GLIU-NET had the best boundary clarity (4.5/5) and the least degree of over-segmentation (1.6) among all the models tested. Such results suggest that GLIU-NET has the great potential to enhance the accuracy of diagnosis and clinical decision-making during the screening of early gastric cancer.

title: Performance degradation method of rolling bearing based on refined composite multiscale entropy weighted by sensitive features
authors: Di Xu, Fangyi Li, Xinyu Xu, Fenyu Gu, Yaping Wang, Lei Teng
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: Early fault signals in rolling bearings exhibit weak detectability and are susceptible to various interference factors, posing challenges in accurately assessing performance degradation. This study proposes a performance degradation evaluation method based on weighted composite multiscale entropy incorporating sensitive features. The approach constructs a multidimensional original fault feature set by integrating timefrequency domain characteristics, evaluates feature sensitivity using four category assessment criteria, ranks feature by importance, and selects optimal sensitive features. Through Hadamard product operations, the composite multiscale entropy is combined with weighted sensitive features to establish a performance degradation index. Experimental validation demonstrates that this method accurately estimates signal complexity across all scales, generates enhanced feature vectors capable of effectively distinguishing between normal and abnormal states, and effectively differentiates fault degradation severity levels.

title: An Approach for Real-Time Distribution Transformer Health Monitoring and Predictive Maintenance System
authors: Mihir Narayan Mohanty, Saumendra Kumar Mohapatra, Amar Bijay Nanda
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: Power distribution transformers are critical assets in different electrical infrastructure. Sometimes the unexpected failures lead to prolonged outages, economic losses, and safety risks than can't be resolved on use of traditional monitoring due to mechanical relays and manual inspections. Also, it fails in real-time fault prediction. This paper presents a modern, low-cost IoT-based system for real-time monitoring of key transformer parameters including voltage, current, oil temperature, winding temperature, oil level, humidity, vibration, and dissolved gases. The system uses an ESP32 micro-controller for real time data acquisition and prepossessing. For long-range, low-power wireless communication with adaptive data rates Zigbee platform is utilized. It can satisfy the IoT based communication, visualization and predictive analysis. With protective actions. Similarly, the data communication can be performed using SMS, email, and push notification. Integration of a hybrid machine learning model as Dynamic Multi scale Attention CNN-LSTM, DMSA-CNN-LSTM) enables predictive maintenance with multi-modal data fusion, achieving 99.17% classification accuracy, 97.14% F1-score, 97.81% precision, and 96.47% recall on fused datasets. The proposed design outperforms in terms of range, power efficiency, scalability, cost, and aligning with recent advancements in smart grid applications, including AI-driven anomaly detection and digital twins.

title: Evaluating YOLOv11-OBB on Critical Defects for the Optical Quality Inspection of PEM Electrolyzer Bipolar Plates
authors: Johannes Buchholz, Edwin Blum, Richard Börgardts, Florian Stamer, Gisela Lanza
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Production Technologies and Systems for E-Mobility (EPTS)
abstract: The large-scale deployment of proton exchange membrane (PEM) electrolysis is currently hindered by high production costs and insufficient manufacturing capacities. A critical bottleneck lies in the quality assurance of complex components like bipolar plates (BPPs). These parts are highly functionally integrated and prone to defects that can severely impact electrolyzer efficiency and safety. One way to address this is by using an automated optical inspection system suitable for high-volume production. This paper presents the development and evaluation of an automated optical inspection system for the inline quality control of metallic BPPs. Leveraging machine learning, the YOLO-OBB (You Only Look Once - Oriented Bounding Box) algorithm is utilized for the precise identification, classification, and geometric evaluation of defects. In response to the challenge posed by the scarcity of authentic defective components, a new methodology is devised applying realistic defect types, including scratches, dents and sealing defects, to new BPPs. This approach enables the variation of defect shape, position and severity for model training and validation. The evaluation of the trained YOLO-OBB model reveals very good performance for the 'scratch' class, indicating robust detection capabilities for a critical and common defect. While detection performance for 'dent' and 'sealing defect' classes remains limited, primarily due to the smaller proportion of these defects in the training dataset, findings underscore the significant potential of automated inspection systems for industrial application.

title: Analysis and Arduino-based Low-cost Experimental Implementation of Single-phase Half-bridge Series Resonant Inverter
authors: Alla Eddine Toubal Maamar, Oumaymah Elamri, Mourad Naidji, Touhami Abdelouahed, Radu Porumb, Saad Mekhilef
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Resonant converters are increasingly adopted in modern high-frequency applications due to their superior efficiency and reduced switching stress. Among them, the series resonant inverter (SRI) offers attractive features such as soft switching, and low harmonic distortion, making it well suited for induction heating systems, power supplies, and ozone generators. Despite these advantages, most experimental implementations rely on expensive digital signal processors or complex control platforms. This paper presents a low-cost and practical experimental implementation of a half-bridge series resonant inverter using an Arduino Uno board as the main control unit. The proposed system is composed of three main stages: a command unit based on Arduino, an isolation and adaptation interface, and a power stage built around a half-bridge configuration supplying a series RLC resonant load. Simulation under MATLAB/Simulink is first conducted to predict system behavior near resonance and to determine the appropriate operating conditions. A complete hardware prototype is then designed, fabricated, and tested over a wide range of switching frequencies from 4 kHz to 6 kHz. Experimental results confirm the resonance phenomenon when the inductive and capacitive reactances are equal, resulting in a significant rise in capacitor voltage. A comparison between theoretical, simulated, and measured values demonstrates good agreement, with minor deviations attributed to parasitic elements and measurement noise. The results verify that an Arduino-based control platform is capable of generating stable high-frequency signals suitable for resonant inverter applications. The proposed solution offers an accessible and economical alternative for laboratory experiments and educational as well as industrial prototyping in power electronics.

title: Toward Quantum-Enhanced Privacy in Medical Data Analytics: A Framework for QOTP-based Secure Computation
authors: Gohin B, M Ramabhadra Rao, K Lakshman Rao, Suresh Chanamala, R Sunitha, Y S Swarupa
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: As quantum computing advances toward practical deployment, healthcare faces a dual challenge: enabling powerful computational analytics while guaranteeing rigorous patient data privacy. Existing classical approaches, particularly Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE), suffer from prohibitive performance costs and computational security assumptions vulnerable to quantum adversaries. This paper presents QPrivMed, a novel privacy-preserving framework for medical data computation to Noisy Intermediate-Scale Quantum (NISQ) devices. QPrivMed couples the Quantum One-Time Pad (QOTP) with selective homomorphic evaluation over Clifford circuits, achieving information-theoretic security for targeted clinical tasks without fault-tolerant hardware. Validated on 8-qubit IBM Quantum processors, the framework achieves 94.7% average circuit fidelity across binary medical classification scenarios, while outperforming classical FHE by a factor of 4.4× in execution time for equivalent Clifford workloads. Security analysis confirms zero mutual information leakage and tamper detection with probability ≥ 1 − 2<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−λ</sup>. This work bridges quantum cryptographic theory and practical healthcare data processing, establishing a scalable foundation for quantum-secured medical analytics while providing an honest assessment of current limitations and a realistic deployment timeline extending to the 2030.

title: Decentralized biometric verification of patients: a Blockchain-IPFS-Oracle hybrid architecture with evaluation performance and GDPR compliance
authors: Yama Sow, Keba Gueye, Samuel Ouya
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Biometrics plays a key role in digital identity management, particularly in healthcare environments where secure authentication, data integrity, and regulatory compliance are critical. However, conventional centralized biometric systems suffer from major limitations, including irreversible data compromise, insider threats, and poor alignment with data protection regulations such as the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). This paper proposes a decentralized biometric patient identity verification architecture based on a hybrid Blockchain-IPFS-Oracle model. The proposed system separates on-chain identity management from off-chain encrypted biometric storage and verification, using modular smart contracts for traceability and governance, IPFS for distributed encrypted storage, and a trusted oracle for confidential biometric comparison. This design enhances data sovereignty, mitigates single points of failure, and improves resistance to internal manipulation. An experimental proof of concept was implemented and evaluated in terms of enrollment and verification latency, blockchain execution costs, and duplicate detection accuracy. The results demonstrate the technical feasibility of the proposed architecture in healthcare-oriented scenarios, while offering improved GDPR compliance and scalability compared to traditional centralized approaches.

title: Health Care Blockchain: A Prototype for Secure Medical Records Using Blockchain
authors: Adnan Shaout, Ritesh Revansiddappa Honnalli, Krina Trivedi, Vivek Reddy Pulakannti
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: This paper presents the design of a blockchain-based healthcare records management system. The proposed system attempts to fix the security and privacy gaps in electronic health records (EHR) solutions. The proposed system is hybrid. It stores medical records encrypted off-chain in a database. Also it stores hashes and access logs on a blockchain. It has an audit that cannot be changed so anyone can trust the history of who looked at a record. It also proves that no one changed the data without permission. The proposed system has three user roles. The first role is patients. Patients control the patients’ records. The second role is healthcare providers, which need consent to view the records. The third role is administrators. Administrators manage the whole system. The proposed system keeps the data safe by encrypting it with AES-256 and verifying the data on a blockchain so any change to the data can be seen quickly, and uses blockchain to check that the data is correct. It manages consent. Adds expiration dates to each consent. Also records audit trails that capture every action. The proposed system was implemented using Python with FastAPI for the backend, React with Vite for the frontend, PostgreSQL for data storage, and a custom blockchain implementation for transaction logging. It successfully demonstrates how blockchain can enhance healthcare data security while maintaining patient privacy and control.

title: Weighted Ensemble Learning for Defensive Intrusion Detection
authors: Mohammad Adnan Aladaileh, Abdullah Ahmed Bahashwan, Ahmed J. Hintaw
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Network intrusion detection systems (IDSs) complement preventive controls by monitoring traffic and raising alerts when malicious activity is suspected. While signature-based IDSs remain effective for known threats, they struggle with novel or obfuscated attacks and typically require continuous manual rule engineering. Machine-learning-based IDSs can learn complex traffic patterns, but published results often suffer from limited transparency, inconsistent experimental protocols, and weak reproducibility—especially on legacy benchmarks. In this paper, we present a reproducible, defensive pipeline for multi-class intrusion detection using the NSL–KDD benchmark derived from KDD’99. We evaluate four classical supervised learners (logistic regression, random forest, gradient boosting, and a shallow multi-layer perceptron) and propose a weighted soft-voting ensemble where weights are proportional to validation macro-F<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf> scores to emphasize minority classes. On the official NSL–KDD test split, the ensemble attains accuracy 0.774, macro-F<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf> 0.588, and weighted-F<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf> 0.738, offering competitive performance relative to individual models. We further include an Isolation Forest baseline for binary anomaly detection and provide interpretable analyses including confusion matrices, ROC/precision– recall curves, feature importance, and learning curves. Overall, our study provides a transparent baseline and a defensively oriented reference implementation for future IDS research.

title: Nonlinear Control of a Three Phase AC/DC Converter for EV Chargers
authors: Khadija Oualifi, Hassan Abouobaida, Younes Abouelmahjoub, Abdelmoghit Fathelkhair, Hajar Akli
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: this paper presents the modeling and control of a three-phase bidirectional AC/DC converter used as the grid-side stage of an electric vehicle (EV) on-board charger. The converter interfaces the electrical grid with the battery pack through a DC-link and is required to ensure unity power factor operation, accurate DC-bus voltage regulation, controlled active and reactive power exchange, and reliable bidirectional power flow for safe charging and discharging processes. To achieve these objectives, three control strategies are investigated and compared: conventional field-oriented control (FOC), nonlinear backstepping control (BSC), and integral backstepping control (IBSC). All controllers are implemented within a cascaded dqframe control architecture with decoupled current dynamics. Their performance is evaluated through MATLAB/Simulink simulations, focusing on dynamic response, robustness, and power quality under grid-connected operating conditions. The results demonstrate the superior performance of the IBSC strategy in terms of tracking accuracy, robustness against disturbances, and overall control effectiveness compared to FOC and conventional BSC.

title: Energy Aware Traffic Engineering: Optimizing Data Center Efficiency Through AI Driven Flow Steering
authors: Krishna Suman Dara
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Traffic engineering that is energy conscious has been necessary to enhance the efficiency of data centers in the face of increased workloads and the need to meet sustainability goals. In this paper, an AI-based flow steering paradigm is introduced, which dynamically optimizes the distribution of the network traffic and minimizes the utilization of the energy without affecting performance guarantees. The suggested concept takes advantage of incessant telemetry of switches, servers, and links to study spatiotemporal traffic models and anticipate congestion-energy advantages in real-time. According to these predictions, the network flow paths are reconfigured dynamically to force the traffic onto energy-efficient network segments, to turn off and selectively activate links and switching devices without breaking the latency or throughput demand. In contrast to rule-based or fixed, the framework underlines the context-dependent decision-making in such a way that it interactively addresses the workload intensity, link utilization, and work power states. The architecture will be model-agnostic which means that it can be easily integrated with software-defined data centers fabric and heterogeneous hardware. The technique correlates traffic engineering with energy optimization and, thereby, provides the scalable basis of the intelligent, self-optimizing data center networks. This paper identifies flow steering using AI as one of the enablers of sustainable and performance-conscious operations in data centers.

title: An AIoT-Enabled Framework for Resilient Healthcare Supply Chains: Bridging Technology Gaps through Intelligent Integration
authors: Hajar Sassi, Atman Jbari, El Hassan Laaziz
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The healthcare supply chain is crucial for ensuring that medicines, vaccines and medical equipment are readily accessible, of high quality, and delivered on time. However, it faces long-standing issues such as limited visibility, ineffective inventory management, broken cold chains, and risks of product wastage and fraud. The convergence of artificial intelligence (AI) and the Internet of Things (IoT), referred to as AIoT, offers significant potential to transform supply chains into intelligent, data-driven networks. In this paper, we conduct a structured literature review of existing AIoT applications in healthcare logistics and identify nine key research gaps concerning interoperability, scalability, security, and organisational deployment. We propose a conceptual multi-layered AIoT framework comprising four integrated layers, namely IoT sensing, secure data management, AI-driven analytics, and application interfaces, designed to address these gaps. To illustrate the framework’s applicability, we present a scenariobased analysis of vaccine supply chain management, demonstrating how AIoT integration can improve visibility, resilience, and operational efficiency. This work establishes a theoretical foundation for future implementation and empirical validation of AIoT-enabled healthcare supply chains.

title: Intelligent Tutoring Systems from Symbolic AI to LLMs: A Critical Review
authors: Bouchra Salh, Sara Bouskour, Lahcen Oughdir
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Intelligent tutoring systems (ITS) represent for five decades an important deployment of Artificial Intelligence (AI) into the educational field, aiming to adapt and personalize learning. This paper provides a critical analysis of ITS evolution over five successive technological generations: Symbolic AI, cognitive architectures, web and probabilistic approaches, datadriven methods and the current era of Deep Learning (DL) and Large Language Models (LLMs). Our study presents four major paradigmatic transitions as well as persistent scientific challenges such as affective and motivational dimensions insufficiently integrated a trade-off between cognitive sophistication and scalability and limited dynamic modeling of individual trajectories. The integration of LLMs into multiagent architectures presents an opportunity to resolve these historically antagonistic dimensions. However, critical challenges remain concerning pedagogical reliability, the ability to justify decisions, multimodal fusion that improves results by using several data modalities and algorithmic fairness that avoids biases and discriminations.

title: Harnessing Large Language Models for Next-Generation Artificial Intelligence Applications
authors: Navin Garg, Sasikanth Yangalasetty, Tharakesavulu Vangalapat, Mina Kumari, Mahesh Basavaraj, Manish Gupta
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) usage is the innovation capable of introducing the new generation of the artificial intelligence system with adaptive thinking, multimodal understanding, and grounded knowledge synthesis. Transformer architecture can be developed; less supervised scalable cognitive automation can be enabled by combining developed augmented retrieval-based generation and parameter-efficient tuning. This paper presents a technically valid and conceptually coherent hybrid-grounded LLM model that has cross-modal embedding alignment, external-knowledge recall, symbolical equivalent validation, and, the hallucination-controlled decoding. The methodology guarantees the semantic integrity and logic-sensitive decision-support in the different fields. The experimental evaluation of a group of complex reasoning demonstrates a considerable improvement as the number of perplexities was decreased to 7.1 instead of 16.8, the level of overall accuracy was increased by 63.4 to 83.8 percent, and the level of F1-score improved by 65.1 to 85.4. Generative metrics also show a significant improvement as BLEU scores 36.9, ROUGE-L scores 56.4 and exact match scores also goes to 63.2 and expectation calibration error also goes down to 3.6 and hallucination error also drops to 5.1. These findings indicate that grounded LLM design is an experimental catalyst of the future AI ecosystems with central interests on reliability, knowledge validity and high-fidelity reason.

title: MIN-Trust: A Minimum Necessary Information Trust Orchestration Framework for Multi-Agent Collaboration
authors: Jinyu Chen, Feiyang Wang, Tian Guan, Yumeng Ma, Linghao Yang, Yutong Wang
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Education
abstract: None

title: Proceedings of the 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Education

title: Distributed AI Vector Search with Data Sovereignty: Architectural Analysis and Scalability Characterization of Oracle Database Sharding
authors: Chaitanya Kulkarni, Chandrashekhar Medicherla, Bharadwaj Vulugundam, Siva Rama Krishna Varma, Milan Parikh, Amit Kumar Padhy
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Data sovereignty regulations mandate that certain data remain within specific geographic jurisdictions, with violations incurring penalties up to EUR 20 million or 4% of global revenue. This paper presents an architectural analysis of Oracle Globally Distributed Database 26ai, demonstrating how geographic database sharding enables large-scale vector search while ensuring data sovereignty compliance. The analysis examines how Oracle 26ai distributes vector embeddings across geographically separated shards using system-managed and geographic sharding strategies, with Raft-based replication providing high availability. Native Hierarchical Navigable Small World (HNSW) and Inverted File Index (IVF) vector indexes operate independently on each shard, enabling parallel similarity searches with single-shard query latency of 10-15ms. Through scalability characterization, this work shows that sharded vector search achieves near-linear throughput scaling proportional to the ratio of direct-routed queries while maintaining constant per-query latency. Three scenarios are identified where distributed architectures outperform centralized approaches: datasets exceeding 10 billion vectors, strict multi-jurisdictional compliance requirements, and globally distributed user bases. This work provides comprehensive architectural analysis of production-scale distributed vector search for sovereignty-compliant Artificial Intelligence (AI) systems.

title: Bounding Box Discrepancy-Driven Building Height Reconstruction from Optical and SAR Imagery
authors: Fanxun Luo, Pu Gu, Sihao Dong, Tao Lai, Qingsong Wang, Haifeng Huang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: Automatic building height extraction is crucial in remote sensing. The fusion of optical and synthetic aperture radar (SAR) imagery has been shown to improve the accuracy of height reconstruction. However, most existing cross-modal approaches rely on deep neural networks for implicit multimodal feature fusion, without explicitly modeling the geometric discrepancies of buildings between optical and SAR imagery, thereby limiting physical interpretability. This letter proposes a Bounding box Discrepancy Height Reconstruction Network (BDHR-Net), which reconstructs building heights by mapping bounding box offsets between optical and SAR imagery to physical heights. A Radar Geometry-guided Logarithmic Regression (RGLR) mechanism and a Height Consistency Constraint (HCC) are designed to enforce consistency with SAR imaging geometry and the physical constraints of building height. Experiments on the MSAW dataset show that BDHR-Net reduces root mean square error (RMSE) by 4.92 % and improves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\delta_{1}$</tex> by 3.29 % over existing state-of-the-art methods. These results demonstrate that explicit geometric modeling offers clear advantages in both reconstruction accuracy and physical interpretability.

title: An Intelligent IoT-Enabled Women and Child Protection Device with GPS Tracking and SOS Alert Mechanism
authors: R. Asha Mary, Devi P, Kaviya M, Rakesh V S, Ramachandran A, Rathika S
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Ensuring the safety of women and children has become a critical societal concern due to the increasing number of security threats and emergency situations. It was imperative that women and children be protected from the increasing threats and dangers during the previous times. Thus, this report proposes an embedded system for women and child protection through tracking and an SMS warning system to provide effective protection and assistance during emergency situations. The embedded system makes use of the microcontroller together with the GPS and GSM modules, which provide precise position tracking and effective communication features. If there is a distress signal, the system will send an SMS warning to the predetermined emergency contacts depending on its location. The embedded system is very convenient to use since it is portable and compact in size. Besides, it is also designed to consume less power.

title: Detection of Chronic Respiratory Diseases Using a Deep Learning- Based CXR Framework
authors: N Aravindhraj, N Shanthi, M Muthuraja, Y S Shalini, Sharan Prasath, T Vishal
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Chronic respiratory diseases such as COVID- 19, pneumonia, and non-specific lung opacities remain major global health challenges, and rapid screening through chest X-ray (CXR) imaging is essential for early intervention. However, manual interpretation of CXRs is often time-consuming and prone to variability, especially in high-workload clinical environments. This study presents a deep learning–based framework designed to automatically classify CXR images into four clinically relevant categories: Normal, COVID-19, Lung Opacity, and Viral Pneumonia. The system integrates three pretrained convolutional neural networks—InceptionV3, ResNet50, and DenseNet201—and employs an attention- guided fusion mechanism to capture complementary spatial and semantic features. A structured preprocessing pipeline, class-balanced training strategy, and two-phase fine-tuning process further enhance model robustness. Evaluated on a curated multi-source dataset, the proposed model achieves 91% accuracy, with balanced precision, recall, and F1-scores across all classes. Grad-CAM visualizations high-light pathology-relevant regions, supporting interpretability and clinical trust. The results demonstrate that combining multi-architecture feature extraction with attention-based fusion offers a reliable, computationally efficient solution for automated CXR analysis, providing a strong foundation for real-time screening and decision support in healthcare settings.

title: A Survey on Hand-Crafted, Deep, and Hybrid Feature Extraction Methods for Medical Image Mining
authors: Fatima-Ezzahra Assamid, Mohamed Daoud, Youssef Filali, My Abdelouahed Sabri, Abdellah Aarab
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The rapid growth of large-scale medical image repositories, including MRI, CT, dermoscopy, and histopathology data, has increased the demand for advanced image mining techniques capable of extracting meaningful and interpretable knowledge. This paper presents a comprehensive survey and comparative analysis of feature extraction methods for medical image mining, covering hand-crafted, deep learning-based, and hybrid approaches. Traditional hand-crafted features, such as SIFT (Scale-Invariant Feature Transform), HOG (Histogram of Oriented Gradients), LBP (Local Binary Patterns), and GLCM (Gray-Level Co-occurrence Matrix), are analyzed in terms of efficiency and interpretability, while their limitations in modeling complex patterns are highlighted. In contrast, deep convolutional neural networks (CNNs), including VGG, ResNet, DenseNet, and EfficientNet, provide superior performance but suffer from limited explainability and high computational cost. To address these challenges, this survey emphasizes hybrid CNN-ARM frameworks, which combine deep feature representations with interpretable rule-based models. The analysis highlights key strengths, limitations, and research gaps, showing that hybrid approaches offer an effective trade-off between accuracy and interpretability for clinical decision support systems.

title: A Hybrid Intelligent Framework for Secure and Adaptive Task Scheduling in Cloud Systems
authors: Madhusudan S, Muthu Kumar B
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Cloud computing environments face challenges due to dynamic workloads, heterogeneous resources, cost constraints, and security risks, making efficient task scheduling difficult. This study proposes a hybrid intelligent framework integrating GROS-NET for workload forecasting, Dual-STACK for cost-and deadline-aware scheduling, and SAM-Cloud for secure load balancing. The framework is evaluated using the Google Cluster Trace dataset in the CloudSim environment. The results show that GROS-NET achieves high forecasting accuracy with an RMSE of 0.081, outperforming LSTM and GRU models. Dual-STACK reduces make-span and operational cost compared to traditional methods such as FCFS and PSO. SAM-Cloud enhances system security by improving detection accuracy and reducing SLA violations and load imbalance. Overall, the proposed framework improves performance, cost efficiency, and security in cloud task scheduling. The integration of prediction, optimization, and security mechanisms makes the system suitable for dynamic and real-world cloud environments.

title: Determinants of Electric Vehicle Adoption in Jabodetabek: An Extended Decomposed Theory of Planned Behavior Approach
authors: Nathan Ihza Alwaaly, Dewi Tamara
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This study investigates the determinants of electric vehicle (EV) adoption in Jakarta, Bogor, Depok, Tangerang and Bekasi (Jabodetabek) Indonesia, utilizing an extended Theory of Planned Behavior (TPB) framework. Despite rapid growth in New Energy Vehicle sales with electric. This research examines how Infrastructure Readiness, Government Policy, and Range Anxiety influence consumer attitudes and perceived behavioral control, ultimately affecting purchase intentions. Data were collected from 415 respondents in the Jabodetabek region through online surveys and analyzed using Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS- SEM). The results show that infrastructure and policy factors are mediated indirectly by psychological factors, as they do not directly influence purchase intentions. This study contributes to the body of literature on sustainable transportation in emerging markets and provides valuable insights to policymakers and stakeholders on how to fast-track EV adoption in Indonesia to achieve its target of net-zero emissions by 2060.

title: Unsupervised Learning–Based Detection of Traceability Anomalies in Automotive Supply Chains Using Isolation Forest and Reconstruction Error–Based Principal Component Analysis
authors: Rime Guelail, Hamza Ennadafy, Nawal Belouaggadia
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The increasingly digitalized automotive supply chain has generated considerable volumes of traceability data from various sources including logistics operations, environmental monitoring systems and quality control systems. This data represents a significant source of information about component life cycles; however, it introduces new difficulties associated with the timely detection of latent risks and anomalous behaviors due to a general scarcity of labeled data. Regarding the application of machine learning techniques for the early detection of traceability anomalies in automotive supply chains, most studies have relied on labeled data or supervised models which do not suitably reflect industrial reality, i.e., traceability anomalies are relatively rare, diverse and often unknown in advance. Therefore, despite the potential benefits of applying unsupervised learning techniques to identify traceability anomalies from the variety of heterogeneous logistics data available within an automotive supply chain, few robust unsupervised approaches are currently available in literature and industrial practice. This contribution outlines an unsupervised learning methodology utilizing the combination of Isolation Forest and Principal Component Analysis (PCA) based on reconstruction errors for the purpose of identifying traceability anomalies in an automotive supply chain. The methodology was evaluated on a structured dataset of 1,000 automotive components described by 19 traceabilityrelated features reflecting realistic industrial logistics conditions. A percentile-based thresholding strategy was adopted to isolate the most atypical observations in a conservative decision-support setting. The results show that both methods individually identify about 2.5 percent of nonexpected items. Isolation Forest detects localized deviations, whereas PCA reconstruction error reveals broader structural inconsistencies. Their non-overlapping outputs highlight a strong complementarity, supporting the development of a hybrid anomaly screening strategy. The results show that each method individually identifies approximately 2.5% of components as anomalous, while capturing different forms of abnormal behavior. In addition to this, the creation of an anomaly score enables the risk of the components to be ranked in terms of their priority and facilitates the identification of a sub-group of components to be inspected more closely. These results provide evidence that using unsupervised machine learning techniques can contribute positively to traceability of automotive parts, particularly by identifying logistics related risks proactively and providing support for making decisions within the complexity of industrial systems.

title: Exploring Explainable AI for Transformer-Based Deepfake Detection and its Resistance to Adversarial Attacks
authors: Nagendra Singh, Priyanka Gupta, Harsh Pratap Singh, Ajit More, Mohammed Ahtesham Farooqui, Shashi Prabhat Ranjan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: The high rate of development of deepfake generation technologies poses a serious threat to the authenticity of digital media, which requires effective and open detection systems. This research article presents transformer-based models to improve the accuracy and interpretability of deepfake detection models. Our new framework is based on the idea that it does not only use transformers to identify forgeries more accurately in both visual and audio formats but also incorporates explain ability mechanisms to demystify the decisions made by the model to enable forensic analysis and legal validation. Moreover, we also systematically assess the resilience of these explainable transformer models to advanced adversarial attacks, their strengths, and possible weaknesses. The experimental findings on benchmark datasets like DFDC, Celeb-DF, and FakeAVCeleb show better accuracy, robustness, and transparency, which open the way to the reliable application of deepfake detection technologies in the real world.

title: Optical Biosensor Based on a Photonic Crystal for Real-Time Detection of Proteins in Sports Performance Monitoring
authors: Abdelilah Nait Bihi, Younouss Bahou, Mourad Rattal, Hamid El Malali, El Mostafa Oualim
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Enhancing athletic performance increasingly relies on advanced monitoring approaches capable of improving training efficiency while preserving athletes’ health. Real-time detection of blood proteins represents a promising strategy for workload adjustment, injury prevention, and recovery monitoring. In this work, we propose and numerically investigate an optical biosensor based on a one-dimensional $\mathrm{PbS} / \mathrm{SiO}_{2}$ photonic crystal incorporating a defect cavity designed to respond to refractive index variations induced by blood proteins. Numerical simulations performed using the Transfer Matrix Method (TMM) demonstrate that the cavity thickness is a key parameter governing the spectral response of the device. The optimized configuration achieves a maximum sensitivity of $\mathbf{4 1 6 2. 9} \mathbf{n m} /$ RIU for a cavity thickness of $24 \mu \mathrm{~m}$, highlighting the importance of geometrical optimization in enhancing light-matter interaction within the sensing region. The obtained results confirm the potential of the proposed structure for real-time optical detection of proteins in sports-related biomedical applications. This work provides a promising foundation for the development of portable and non-invasive optical biosensing platforms and opens perspectives for future experimental validation and extension to additional biomarkers.

title: Research on Photovoltaic Overvoltage Suppression Based on Voltage Dependent Inductor
authors: Chenglai Tian, Yalan Zhong, Jun Wang, Heng Luo, Jing Wang, Yaoqin Chen, Cuihua Tian
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: Addressing the issues of reactive power imbalance and overvoltage in distribution networks caused by grid-connected distributed photovoltaic systems, this paper proposes a method combining "voltage dependent inductor and series inductor". Firstly, starting from the magnetization and volt-ampere characteristics of voltage dependent inductor, its electromagnetic equation is derived based on the magnetic saturation effect. Secondly, the causes of overvoltage in photovoltaic systems are analyzed, revealing the core principle of voltage dependent inductor in suppressing overvoltage through dynamic inductive reactive power output. Finally, the effectiveness of this method is verified through simulation of a 380/220V low-voltage distribution network. The results show that voltage dependent inductor can suppress the overvoltage generated by reverse power flow from photovoltaic energy, significantly enhancing the voltage stability of photovoltaic grid-connected systems.

title: A Privacy-Respecting Federated Approach for Non- IID ECG Signal Classification using Adaptive Aggregation
authors: Aditya Nath Mishra, Preeti Yadav, Vinay Rishiwal, Kaushal Kumar, Mano Yadav
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: Electrocardiogram (ECG) signals are critical for detecting heart conditions at an early stage, yet their sensitive nature implies data sharing among institutions encounters firm obstacles. Machine learning techniques customarily require centralized datasets, and this conflicts with privacy regulations modern like HIPAA and GDPR. Federated Learning is a collaborative framework for local hospital model training. This framework works to avoid the moving of raw patient data. However, most of the existing FL approaches such as FedAvg do battle real-world non-identically distributed data. The paper proposes an Adaptive Federated Learning (AFL) framework to address client heterogeneity and also improve global model performance. It does combine local fine-tuning along with adaptive aggregation strategies. Using the MIT-BIH Arrhythmia dataset, our method shows superior accuracy and stability over FedAvg across simulated hospital environments, proving its effectiveness in privacy-preserving ECG classification.

title: Integration of Cameras in Industry for Maintenance and Production Optimization
authors: Mohamed Lamine Soudani, Salah Nissabouri, Moulay El Houssine Ech-Chhibat
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Technological advancements and the rise of Industry 4.0 have provided the industrial sector with new tools and methods to enhance equipment performance and optimize their utilization. In this context, industrial vision cameras have been developed and adapted to technological changes, enabling realtime optimization of maintenance and production. In light of this, this article highlights the various types of cameras and their applications in industry, as well as the most commonly used artificial intelligence algorithms. To achieve these objectives, a literature review was conducted to identify the cameras used, followed by a survey of algorithms applied to visual data analysis for proactive maintenance. Finally, the study examines how the integration of these technologies helps improve proactive maintenance and optimize productivity.

title: How will Social Media Usage impact on Online Shopping Purchase Intention
authors: Martin Fo, Santy
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Studies were examining influence on social media usage on online purchase intention by analyze from many studies from different industry and geographical context which result showing social media playing vital role in shaping consumer purchase intention through many mechanisms. Studies were highlighted that consumer trust mediating the relationship between purchase intention and social media marketing emphasizing importance of building trust from credible content. Another studies found that elements such as like social interaction, professionalism and information value on social media platform being significantly increasing consumer purchase intention on E-commerce platforms. E-commerce also shown that informativeness, personalization and entertainment on social media advertising will increasing purchase intention and brand awareness within millennials. This was also revealed that conspicuous consumption on social media influence purchase intention through social aspiration. Another study showed that many online platforms like TikTok, Instagram and Facebook have big vital role in online purchase intention. Much more these studies have confirmed effective marketing strategies on social media which taking on value, entertainment, social information dynamics and account trust being significantly increasing consumer purchase intention on context of online shopping

title: Determinants of Student Engagement in E-Learning: Evidence from Blended and Fully Online Courses
authors: Faisal Aburub, Rami A. Abdel-Rahem, Mayyas Al-Remawi
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: This research focuses on determining the factors that lead to student participation in e-learning environments by comparing blended modalities with almost entirely online options. A survey of 315 university students was distributed to evaluate the perceptions in terms of social isolation, platform efficacy, time investment, digital equity issues, and preferences regarding mandatory versus optional e-learning adoption. Results show that 63.8% of participants experience increased social isolation in fully online environments whereas 58.1% report increased engagement in blended formats. Microsoft Teams was rated positively (63.5%) for its contribution towards communication. Notably, 79.7% of them stated that lack of access to the internet was a major obstacle to participating in e-learning. Participants had a strong preference on e-learning implementation as optional, 65.4% agreed while 33.3% preferred mandatory implementation. These findings have significant implications for educational policy and the design of courses in the higher education institution in order to optimize digital learning experiences.

title: A Unified TSN and Beamforming Framework for Ultra-Low Latency Mobility in 6G mm Wave XL-MIMO Systems
authors: CH. Nagaraju, C. Venkatesh, Shaik Karimullah, Chennuru Shaik Sana, Uppusetty Varshitha, Menta Venkata Naresh, Vallepu Venkata Ganesh Kumar
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: 6G wireless networks are anticipated to offer various missioncritical services with deterministic and ultra-low latency and high reliability for highly dynamic scenarios. However, meeting these conditions is extremely demanding, and such an achievement becomes more complicated in the case of mmWave XLMIMO systems where mobile basestations (BSs) are used -e.g., UAV or vehicle-mounted platforms-, causing fast channel variations and frequent link failures. In response to these issues, this paper presents a unified protocol framework that combines latency-sensitive Time Sensitive Networking (TSN) scheduling with adaptive multi-timescale beamforming for mobile 6G systems. The design includes slow-timescale user association, medium-time scale analog beamforming and fast-timescale digital beamforming with TSN-driven scheduling to provide stable BS–user and BS–BS link relationships. Latency-sensitive traffic is served by deterministic TSN mechanisms, guaranteeing strict implementations of presented approaches 235 latency and jitter bounds.Extensive system level simulations confirm the efficiency of the protocol in reducing TSN deadline violations and jitter while maintaining high throughput in mobile base-station and varying traffic priority scenarios. It is shown that the system has strong scalability in the dense user environment and large robustness for mobility-induced outages. In summary, the proposed framework realizes a realistic path to a reliable and adaptable 6G mmW network that can support industrial automation, autonomous mobility, disaster-response communication with deterministic performance guarantees

title: Thermal Design of a Dry-Type Transformer Using High-Thermal-Conductivity Epoxy and Embedded Ceramic Rods
authors: Jun Deng, Gaojia Zhu, Haibin Zhou, Xingzi Liu, Xiangjiang Yang, Zhicheng Xie, Heng Wu, Dingguo Cai
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: For epoxy-encapsulated dry-type transformers, as the heat dissipation paths of the windings are restricted by the surrounding insulation structure, the accumulated internal heat can cause severe overheating issues. To address this problem, this paper proposes a thermal design for a dry-type transformer using high-thermal-conductivity epoxy and embedded ceramic rods. First, a three-dimensional (3D) thermal model of the transformer with conventional epoxy encapsulation is established, and its thermal behavior is analyzed. Then, the conventional epoxy is replaced with a high-thermal-conductivity epoxy, and high-thermal-conductivity ceramic rods are embedded between the low-voltage (LV) and high-voltage (HV) windings. The proposed design not only improves the thermal conductivity of the encapsulation layer but also introduces additional heat-transfer paths from the inner LV winding to the outer region without completely blocking the air cooling channel between the two windings. Based on numerical simulations, the temperature distributions of the conventional and improved structures are comparatively investigated. The results show that the proposed design can effectively accelerate heat dissipation from the inner winding, reduce the hot-spot temperature of the winding, and improve the overall temperature uniformity of the transformer. Compared with the conventional structure, the proposed design decreases the hot-spot temperature by 20.53 ℃ and reduces the temperature difference between the inner and outer windings by 10.13 ℃. The proposed method provides an effective thermal design approach for improving the heat dissipation capability and thermal reliability of dry-type transformers.

title: Spatial Attention GAN for Cloud Removal in Optical Remote Sensing Images
authors: Jizhe Xu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: Optical remote sensing imagery is widely used due to its high spatial resolution and stable geometric properties, but cloud contamination is unavoidable and greatly reduces data usability. This paper presents a paired cloud-removal framework based on a Spatial Attention Generative Adversarial Network (SpA-GAN). The model introduces a local-to-global spatial attention mechanism to identify cloud-affected regions and to enhance information recovery in these areas, while preserving the geometric structure of ground objects. Experiments are conducted on the public RICE dataset (RICE1 and RICE2). Quantitative evaluation with PSNR and SSIM demonstrates that the attentionguided design improves the quality of cloudless image reconstruction, particularly on challenging scenes with dense cloud coverage.

title: Research on Bi-Level Robust Optimal Scheduling of Virtual Power Plants Based on MPA Algorithm
authors: Ruiyi He
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: To address the impact of photovoltaic output uncertainty on the economic efficiency of virtual power plants (VPP), this paper proposes a two-layer robust optimal scheduling strategy combined with the Marine Predator Algorithm (MPA). Firstly, a mathematical model of the VPP is constructed. Based on the two-stage robust optimization framework, the MPA is used to determine the start-stop schedule of gas turbines, and a mixed-integer linear programming method is integrated to solve the scheduling strategy with the lowest cost. Case studies compared with the benchmark C&CG algorithm show that the MPA enhanced robust optimization method can reduce the total cost from 46,204 yuan to 15,410 yuan, with an economic improvement of 66.65%. It has significant advantages in the cost control of electricity purchase and sale, as well as carbon trading, providing an efficient solution for VPP to cope with uncertainties.
