To address these issues, we suggest the federated learning-based detection framework that will enable IoT gateways to collectively train a global model without sharing raw data. At local, all clients obstruct flow-level statistical properties of traffic and feed them into a simple deep neural network, and only model updates are swapped with the central aggregator without compromising either privateness or generalization tenacity. The framework combines the following two to address the data imbalance and heterogeneity: the class-weighted loss functions and adaptive aggregation strategies. Top IoT dataset CICIoT2023 evaluations are run, which report accuracy of 99.3%. Macro-F1 of 0.989 and ROC-AUC 0.996, which indicates a maximum of 3.7 percentage points over top of centralized baselines in macro f1. The last model (2.1 MB, 0.85 M parameters) spends 12.4 ms on inference latency on average in scenarios where a Raspberry Pi 4B is used and demonstrates that the model is viable to run in real time to offer a privacy-preserving DDoS benevolent detector in a heterogeneous IoT network.

title: Machine Learning-Based Prediction Modelling of EDM Application on Ti6Al4V Alloy Using 3D-Printed SS316L Electrodes
authors: Satyabrata Barik, Basanta Kumar Nanda, B. Surekha, Swayam Bikash Mishra
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: This study explores the application of 3D-printed SS316L electrodes in the Electrical Discharge Machining (EDM) of Ti6Al4V alloy and employs machine learning (ML) models to predict and optimize key performance metrics such as Material Removal Rate (MRR), Tool Wear Rate (TWR), surface roughness, and hardness. EDM experiments were conducted using a BoxBehnken L27 orthogonal array design, and data-driven models were trained using four regression algorithms. Initial experiments with 27 data points showed limited model accuracy (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}} \boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{5 6. 2 \%}$</tex> for MRR using Random Forest). To enhance prediction capability, the dataset was expanded to 200 samples using polynomial interpolation. After expansion, Random Forest achieved <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 5. 6} \boldsymbol{\%} \mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> in predicting MRR and TWR, while XGBoost reached <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$98.6 \% \mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> for surface roughness and hardness. These findings demonstrate that integrating 3D-printed electrodes with AI-driven modelling and improving the data set significantly improves the predictive control.

title: Automated Nursing Care Plan Creation Based on NANDA-I, NIC, and NOC Standards Using Pre-Trained Transformer Models
authors: Allen Bernard M. Tan, Herwayne John H. Cawili, Japeth A. Gomez, Salimar B. Tahil
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: This study developed a domain-specific constrained generation workflow for Nursing Care Plan (NCP) generation grounded in the standardized NANDA-I, NIC, NOC (NNN) frameworks and powered by pre-trained transformer models. The goal was to support nursing education by enhancing the accuracy, consistency, and efficiency of NCP creation while evaluating the capability of modern large language models (LLMs) to generate clinically aligned nursing documentation. The workflow benchmarked three large language models (LLMs): Claude Sonnet 4.5, Gemini 2.5 Pro, and GPT-5 across 20 expert-curated cases (derived from 80 student NCPs) to rank suitability for educational NCP support, prioritizing NNN adherence as the primary criterion. A model selection framework was applied to identify the most suitable for clinical educational use, prioritizing NNN adherence as the primary criterion. Claude Sonnet 4.5 achieved the highest adherence score (77.93%), outperforming Gemini 2.5 Pro (74.62%) and GPT 5 (62.20%), resulting in its selection as the system’s core model establishing it as the optimal pilot model. These pilot-scale metrics provided functional guidance for educational deployment rather than clinical perfection benchmarks. The developed system was subsequently evaluated through expert validation by nursing faculty and usability testing with nursing students. Expert reviewers found the AI generated NCPs to be clinically acceptable and strongly aligned with NNN standards, while student respondents reported high usability, clarity, and perceived educational value of the system. Overall, the study demonstrates the feasibility of integrating transformer based LLMs into nursing education to support standardized NCP development. The findings highlight the potential of AI assisted tools to enhance learning, promote documentation accuracy, and streamline academic workflows in nursing programs.

title: An Intelligent Hybrid Model of CNN-BiLSTM for Fault Classification in AC Microgrid
authors: Rakesh Sahu, Pratap Kumar Panigrahi, Deepak Kumar Lal, Rudranarayan Pradhan
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: The reliable and fast fault classification is paramount for the stability and resilience of your modern power systems as the networks are increasingly dynamic through adopting renewables. This paper proposed a hybrid Deep Learning model employing Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) and Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) networks for the intelligent classification of three main transmission line faults; Line-to-Ground (LG), Line-to-Line (LL), and Double Line-to-Ground (LLG). In the proposed model, CNN layers were used to extract local spatial and frequency-domain features during faults occurring in threewaveforms of current signals, while the bi-directional layer is effective in identifying long-range temporal dependencies during transient fault conditions. An attention mechanism was used to indicate the relevant parts of the temporal signal window during the classification process, providing interpretation of the signal focus. In order to improve the accuracy, especially for LL faults, hyperparameter optimization with Optuna was used to tune important model hyperparameters including the learning rate, LSTM size, and dropout rate. The model was trained using synthetically produced, Z-score normalized datasets with data augmentation to mimic realistic operating conditions. When compared to traditional classifiers including Decision Tree, SVM, Naïve Bayes, and MLP, the CNN-BiLSTM architecture demonstrated superior performance with accuracy reaching up to 99.9 % for each fault type. The model also showed good generalization and robustness, as evidenced by confusion matrices, ROC curves, and visualizations of attention-weighs. The findings reinforce that the suggested CNN-BiLSTM approach provides an effective and interpretable option for performing fault classifications in real-time, paving the way for intelligent protection schemes for future smart grids.

title: Optimizing Campus Security Systems: Leveraging Ontologies and Large Language Models to Enhance Detection Accuracy and System Reliability
authors: Yangyi Xia, Shiyu Liang, Fuhong Jiang, Xian Huang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: Ensuring campus security requires AI-driven anomaly detection systems that are not only accurate, but also interpretable and responsive. This study presents a decisionsupport framework that combines retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) with ontology-driven reasoning and large language models (LLMs) to enhance security monitoring. A controlled experiment involving 30 professional security personnel and 50 simulated scenarios evaluated the system’s effectiveness. The results indicate a significant improvement in anomaly detection accuracy, increasing from 65% (baseline) to $85 {\%}(p=0.027)$, while reducing false alarms. Additionally, response times decreased by $\mathbf{5 2} {\%} \boldsymbol{(} \mathbf{p}=$ 0.001) compared to conventional keyword-based retrieval systems. By integrating structured domain knowledge with adaptive AI reasoning, this approach facilitates precise threat identification and enables faster, well-informed security decisions. These findings demonstrate that ontology-enhanced AI can effectively address key limitations in security monitoring, offering a scalable and interpretable solution for real-time campus safety management.

title: Federated Learning as a Governance-Aligned Intelligence Paradigm for Pharmaceutical Manufacturing and Research
authors: Faisal Aburub, Mayyas Al-Remawi, Rami Abdel-Rahem, Faisal Al-Akayleh, Ahmed S.A. Ali Agha
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: The pharmaceutical sector is experiencing rapid growth in data generation across discovery, development, manufacturing, and post-marketing activities. Despite this expansion, the adoption of advanced machine learning remains constrained by structural data fragmentation, stringent regulatory oversight, and the need to protect intellectual property and sensitive operational knowledge. Centralized learning paradigms are therefore often incompatible with pharmaceutical data governance requirements. This article advances a conceptual reframing of pharmaceutical artificial intelligence (AI) through federated learning, a distributed machine learning paradigm that enables collaborative model development without centralized data aggregation. Federated learning is examined as an enabling infrastructure for cross-site manufacturing intelligence, multi-institutional pharmaceutical research, and lifecycle process optimization. Drawing on documented industrial and healthcare-adjacent case studies from the literature, the manuscript demonstrates how federated learning reconciles advanced analytics with regulatory compliance, validation integrity, and organizational autonomy. The central contribution of this work lies in positioning federated learning not merely as a privacy-preserving technique, but as a governance-aligned intelligence paradigm for regulated pharmaceutical systems.

title: Machine Learning Models for SQL Injection Detection: A Comparative Analysis and Hybrid Framework Proposal
authors: Mahmoud Baklizi, Mohammad Alkhazaleh, Loui Al Sardy
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: SQL Injection (SQLi) has continued to be among the most severe threats to web application security and has always been featured in the OWASP Top Ten. Conventional signature-based and static analysis can not cope with new attack vectors and obfuscation. In this paper, the authors provide an in-depth comparison of machine learning (ML)-based SQLi detectors, comparing the performance of traditional and deep learning architectures and ensemble-based approaches on a curated and modern dataset. We prove that our hybrid models that combine natural language processing (NLP) with ensemble learning outperform better, and the F1-scores are more than 99.2. In addition, we introduce SQLiDetect-Hybrid, a new framework that consists of syntactic features extraction, contextual embedding with language models and a stacking ensemble. The framework has a false positive rate (FPR) of 0.42% and real-time (<15ms) queries. We find empirical support to integrate adaptive ML-based detection in the next generation web application firewalls (WAFs) and intrusion prevention system (IPS).

title: Explainable Artificial Intelligence for Crop Yield Prediction Using Agro-Climatic Data
authors: Gurmeet Kaur, Ashanand, Rupinder Singh, Preet Kamal, Rakhi Dua, Kamlesh Kukreti
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Proper forecasting of crop yield is a requirement of proper agricultural planning and protection of food security. The current work presents a broad machine-learning-driven model of the prediction of crop yield, combining the range of regression models with strict evaluation criteria and elucidable artificial intelligence (XAI) methodologies. Agro-climatic, edaphic and nutrient related predictors were trained in decision tree, random forest, gradient-boosting, k nearest neighbors, support vector regression and deep neural networks. The evaluation of performance was conducted with the help of R2 and Root-Mean-Square-Error (RMSE), which showed that ensemble and deep -learning methods provide the best predictive values. Specifically, the highest accuracy was achieved with the Random Forest and Deep Neural Networks, which underscore their ability to estimate complex non-linear relationships among the dataset. Analysis of feature-importance identified temperature, humidity and soil quality as the most influential predictors of yield. Besides, lack of systematic bias and high generalisation was supported by error residual diagnostics. The explanations provided with SHAP also provided global and local answers to the decisions made by the model, thus increasing transparency and interpretability. Together, the proposed framework provides accurate, consistent and interpretable crop-yield predictions, providing priceless insights into data-driven agronomic decision-making and the promotion of sustainable farming systems.

title: SMARTGRID FORECAST: Machine Learningdriven Electricity Price Prediction for Smart Energy Systems
authors: Ch Armaan, Anuj Panda, Tumulu Rajsekhar, Neelamadhab Padhy, Mahavir Ojha, Chinmayee Mishra
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: Electricity prices are becoming less and less predictable because of changes in demand, the unpredictability of renewable energy sources, and unseen market forces from outside the market. Because both suppliers and consumers are affected by these changes, there is a greater need than ever for precise price forecasting tools. Objective: Our main goal is to create a machine learning model that uses environmental and historical data to predict short-term changes in electricity prices. Energy stakeholders can make better planning and consumption decisions with improved forecasting. Material/Method: This study includes several regression-based machine learning models, including Linear Regression, Ridge, Lasso, Elastic Net, Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, XGBoost, LightGBM, and CatBoost. Each model was evaluated on predictive accuracy and robustness using metrics such as RMSE, MAE, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex>. We used a dataset containing half-hourly records of electricity market variables. It consists of over 35,000 instances with features such as wind production (forecasted and actual), system load, temperature, wind speed, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2}$</tex> intensity, and time-related details. The target variable is SMPEA (System Marginal Price for EA), which represents the electricity price. Result: After evaluating multiple models, we got the maximum accuracy of 83 % only with [35, 000, 22] of data, and then we applied the Advanced Feature Engineering, and we got that The Algorithms like XG Boost, LightGBM, and Cat Boost demonstrated the superior performance compared to other linear Models with a Performance Matrix of 99.08 %.

title: ZIF-8 Deposition on Optical Fibers and Silicon Wafers for Methane Sensing
authors: Artur Samp, Valerius Abb, Alex Wolfgramm, Tobias Reichenstein, Jörg Franke, Martin Kammler
publish: 2025 16th International MID Congress Mechatronic Integration Discourse (MID Congress)
abstract: Metal-organic frameworks (MOFs), such as Zeolitic Imidazolate Framework-8 (ZIF-8), are increasingly explored for sensing applications. In this field, the formation of uniform thin films is often essential. This study details the deposition and stepwise characterization of covalently bonded ZIF-8 thin films for methane sensing on two types of silica-based substrates: silicon wafers with native oxide and optical fibers. We emphasize practical and accessible methods to verify each step of the deposition process. Surface preparation was tailored to each substrate. Silicon wafer substrates were cleaned using Caro’s acid, while optical fibers were activated via oxygen plasma to account for handling constraints. Both substrate types were subsequently functionalized with a monolayer of isopropyltrimethoxysilane (IPTES) to promote covalent bonding of ZIF-8. Particles of ZIF-8 were deposited using a seeded growth approach. Solvothermal synthesis of ZIF-8 was performed, while simultaneously immersing the substrates in the synthesis solution, promoting the formation of a continuous thin film. The deposition process was characterized at each stage. Free ZIF-8 powder obtained from the synthesis was centrifuged, washed with ethanol and analyzed by attenuated total reflection infrared (ATR-IR) spectroscopy. Silicon wafers enabled contact angle measurements to assess IPTES functionalization, as well as spectroscopic ellipsometry for analysis of the formed ZIF-8 thin film. Scanning electron microscopy (SEM) was used to examine film morphology on both substrate types. To evaluate methane sensitivity, optical fibers coated with ZIF-8 were exposed to vacuum and methane atmospheres, while changes in transmitted optical power were monitored to probe refractive index variations.

title: Security and Privacy in Connected and Autonomous Vehicles: Architecture and Challenges
authors: Mohamed Alhyan, Zineb Nadifi, Mariya Ouaissa, Mariyam Ouaissa, Ali Kartit
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The combination of autonomous vehicles and the internet, along with related modern information and communication technologies, has created the concept of the Connected and Autonomous Vehicles (CAVs). CAVs are a promising direction for the development of the intelligent transportation system. However, CAVs are also an open system with a huge number of attack surfaces and vulnerabilities, which could lead to considerable cybersecurity risks. Although considerable research has been dedicated to addressing such risks, there is a lack of an overall assessment framework for CAVs’ cybersecurity. This paper proposes a comprehensive cybersecurity assessment framework for CAVs that combines architecture analysis, attack taxonomy, and a multi-factor risk model.

title: A Multilayer Perceptron Fitted Simulation Model for 2D Sea Surface Scattering and Doppler Velocity Based on M4S
authors: Yingrui Zhang, Rui Qi, Xiaonan Yao, Xiaoqing Wang, Zhibin Wang, Xinzhe Yuan
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: The normalized radar cross-section (NRCS) and Doppler velocity are critical parameters in ocean microwave remote sensing, playing a key role in studying various oceanic phenomena, such as wind, currents, internal waves, and shallow topography. Simulating NRCS and Doppler velocity textures under complex current modulation effects is essential for understanding these phenomena. The widely used M4S simulation model provides essential information but suffers from high computational costs and a limited number of pixels. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a multilayer perceptron based fitted model for 2D sea surface scattering and Doppler velocity, using M4S as the reference. The proposed model establishes a mapping from sea surface current fields, wind fields, and radar parameters to the resulting NRCS and Doppler velocity. Compared to M4S, the prediction time of the proposed model is reduced by four orders of magnitude. Moreover, the correlation coefficients between the model predictions and M4S results are 0.89 for NRCS and 0.99 for Doppler velocity.

title: A Low-EMI Spread-Spectrum Clocking Circuit with Digital Calibration for High-Precision MCU On-Chip Oscillators
authors: Tianyuan Liu, Xu Meng
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: The on-chip clock oscillator is fundamental to the performance and electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) of microcontroller units (MCUs). This paper presents the design and simulation of a high-precision RC oscillator integrating an active spread-spectrum clocking (SSC) circuit for substantial electromagnetic interference (EMI) reduction. The design tackles the co-design challenge of maintaining frequency accuracy under modulation by employing a two-step digital calibration system (current-mirror coarse-tuning and resistor-network fine-tuning) in tandem with a triangular-wave-based SSC modulator. Implemented in a $\mathbf{1 1 0 n m}$ CMOS process, the oscillator supports a configurable center frequency $\boldsymbol{(} \mathbf{~} \mathbf{~} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{1 6 ~ M H z} \boldsymbol{)}$. Post-layout simulation results demonstrate that the proposed design achieves a peak EMI suppression exceeding $\mathbf{1 0 ~ d B}$ while maintaining a calibrated frequency stability of better than $\pm 1.2 \%$ over the industrial temperature range ($-40^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ to $105^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$), supply voltage variations, and all process corners. This work introduces a coordinated system-level approach to balance frequency precision and EMI mitigation for high-reliability MCU environments.

title: Optimization Accuracy of Crime Rate Prediction using K-Mean and Random Forest based Machine Learning
authors: Nikita Namdev, Ritu Prasad, Arjun Rajput, Saurabh Karsoliya
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: In smart cities, proper prediction of crime rates is critical in improving security of the citizens, strategic policing, and efficient management of available resources. Conventional crime analysis tools have difficulties with complex spatial-temporal patterns and latent relationships that occur in large and heterogeneous crime data. The study hypothesizes a hybrid ML model that will combine K-Means clustering with the RF classifier to maximize the accuracy of prediction. The strategy starts with the preprocessing of historical crime data, which includes the extraction of the information as the type of crime, the patterns of location, frequency of incidences, and time aspects. The clustering method used is K-Means, which is an unsupervised data clustering method where similar incidences of crime are grouped into meaningful clusters to reduce noise in the data and achieve better features separation. These clusters are further fed into the Random Forest model as supplementary inputs and thus allow the model to acquire more elegant and organized patterns to learn. Random Forest, which was characterized by its stability and use of the ensemble method to make decisions, predicts more successfully and with high stability the types and frequencies of crimes. Accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score and ROC analysis are the evaluation metrics that show that the hybrid model is much better than the traditional standalone techniques. The findings indicate that feature enhancement through clustering results in a superior way of detecting the existence of crime hotspots and more dependable prediction of possible future crime incidences. It is concluded the paper, the hybrid K-Means-RF system provides a viable, scalable, and efficient way to estimate intelligent crime rates in high-tech urban environments.

title: Learning Analytics for Dual Education: A Privacy-Preserving Framework Bridging Campus and Workplace Competencies
authors: Mohammad H. Abu-Arqoub, Amal Shafiq Abdulla, Ali A. Titinchi, Rashiq Rafiq Marie
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Dual education programs struggle with a fundamental challenge: demonstrating empirically how campus learning activities translate into workplace performance. This paper presents a privacy-preserving analytics framework that integrates learning management system behavioural patterns with supervisor-assessed workplace competencies and an institutional outcomes assessment infrastructure. The architecture introduces three components: a behavioural indicator extraction engine operating on Moodle’s event logs, a cross-context competency mapping protocol validated through expert consensus and aligned with institutional rubrics, and an uncertainty-aware prediction pipeline with automated quality controls. Deployed across 47 students in computer science and software engineering programmes at the University of Petra over 12 weeks, the framework revealed strong associations (Spearman ρ = 0.61-0.76) between LMS engagement patterns and structured workplace evaluation rubrics measuring ten performance criteria. The system requires 15-20 hours of initial setup, operates under institutional review board-approved governance protocols, and integrates with existing program learning outcomes assessment systems to provide comprehensive evidence for accreditation and quality assurance.

title: Method for Automating the Layout of Wireless Charging Infrastructure for Public Transport Vehicles
authors: Patrick Ziesel, Fabian Neff, Konstantin Kuch, Martin Cichon
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Production Technologies and Systems for E-Mobility (EPTS)
abstract: Transforming mobility to a shared, public, and decarbonised system is a key challenge of our time. Battery electric buses are a promising option for cities to reduce local greenhouse gas and noise emissions in public transport. The dominant charging strategy to date is concentrated during breaks but mainly overnight in depots, which leads to several disadvantages.The sustainable energy mix in Germany peaks during the daytime. Demand-oriented charging strategies hold a massive potential in increasing the overall efficiency due to smaller and lighter battery packs and shorter downtimes. Dynamic Wireless Power Transfer (DWPT) is considered an economic solution approach. This paper presents a method for automating the planning of the wireless charging systems for public transport. The output is an organized map indicating the optimized positioning and sizing of the inductive coils.Beginning with a demand-oriented energy estimation, the method uses operational, vehicle and infrastructure data to derive an optimized coil positioning within the serviced road network. Therefore, single route sections are evaluated in terms of their characteristics such as maximum permitted speed, route utilisation, stops, traffic lights and pedestrian crossings. Furthermore, the study addresses the challenges posed by the involvement of multiple stakeholders and the identification of barriers to integration with existing transport infrastructure. The study also considers the fusion of meta data from relevant sources such as the charging infrastructure and vehicle specifications to gain additional information and improve the planning process. The complex set of variable input parameters are sampled within an optimization loop to derive the first data based optimum. A posteriori gained restrictions due to local, urban-structural conditions such as telecommunication, energy and other lines narrow down the scope for a new iteration. The paper contributes to the development of sustainable urban mobility solutions by providing coherent approaches to an efficient design of sustainable public transport.

title: Graph Attention Networks Enhance Fine-Grained Academic Performance Prediction in MOOCs by Modelling Structural Relationships
authors: Huang Shiqin, Zhou Jiaying, Xiao Hongyun, Hai Tao, Yang Xiao
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Predicting student performance in massive open online courses (MOOCs) is critical for personalizing education and preventing dropouts, but existing methods are limited by their coarse granularity and neglect of complex structural relationships within educational data. Here, we introduce a graph-based approach that significantly improves the accuracy of fine-grained, challenge-level score prediction. We first present MOOPer, a large-scale dataset from a major Chinese MOOC platform comprising 2.53 million practice records and a rich knowledge graph of 11 entity types. We model student-challenge interactions as a bipartite graph and employ a graph attention network (GAT) to learn structural embeddings, which encode the importance of relationships without pre-training. Integrating these graph-derived features with standard student and challenge data, we achieve state-of-the-art prediction performance. Our GAT-enhanced gradient boosting model attained an accuracy of 0.89 and an F1-score of 0.92, substantially outperforming baseline models. Feature importance analysis confirms that structural metrics—such as node centrality and interaction frequency—are critical predictors. This work demonstrates that explicitly modelling the educational graph structure is essential for accurate fine-grained prediction, providing a foundation for intelligent intervention systems in online learning.

title: Flexible Vivaldi Antennas for Estimating Breast Skin Parameters
authors: Milad Mokhtari, Milica Popović
publish: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)
abstract: In this work, we present two generations of compact, flexible surface-wave-exciting Vivaldi antennas designed for tissue-adjacent operation and fabricated on Kapton polyimide films. Through accurate modeling of surface-wave propagation along the breast tissue-air interface, we aim to reliably calculate the propagation delay required for confocal microwave breast imaging and to even sense superficial pathologies, such as Paget's disease. The Gen-1 antenna employs a two-layer microstrip feed, while the Gen-2 design adopts a simplified single-layer CPW-fed architecture to improve flexibility. Measured reflection coefficients show acceptable agreement with full-wave simulations, suggesting that the antennas and the surface-wave approach can yield data to help improve the overall performance of the confocal microwave imaging system.

title: A Piecewise Fuzzy SIER Model for Alzheimer Disease Progression
authors: K. Mythili Gnanapriya, T. Chandrapushpam, M. Gowri Manohari, S. Ruth Kethsial, M. Dhanalakshmi, R. Naveenkumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: Alzheimer’s disease is a progressive neurodegenerative disorder characterized by slow, uncertain, and stage-wise deterioration of cognitive function. Classical deterministic models are often inadequate for representing the inherent ambiguity in disease progression rates and therapeutic responses. To address this limitation, this paper developed a piecewise fuzzy Susceptible–Exposed–Infected–Removed (SIER) model in which the transition parameters for susceptibility, exposure, symptomatic progression, and removal are formulated as triangular fuzzy numbers to capture neurological and biological uncertainty. The model further incorporates piecewise-defined time intervals to represent changes in dynamical behavior before and after medical interventions such as drug initiation, cognitive therapy, or lifestyle modification. Analytical expressions for the fuzzy progression index, fuzzy equilibrium states, and piecewise stability conditions are derived using fuzzy differential calculus. Numerical simulations illustrate how parameter fuzziness and regime switching influence the temporal evolution of disease stages. The results demonstrate that the proposed model more accurately represents real-world variability and uncertainty compared to classical deterministic SIER frameworks, making it a promising mathematical tool for studying Alzheimer’s disease dynamics.

title: Optimal Energy Optimization of Renewable-Assisted AC Microgrid with Hybrid Energy Storage via Lagrange Multiplier
authors: Khadija Azakaf, Abdelmounime El Magri, Rachid Lajouad, Ilyass El Myasse
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Microgrids are emerging as a promising solution for sustainable energy by integrating renewable energy sources (RES) like solar and wind power. However, the intermittent nature of RES presents significant challenges to system stability and cost-effective operation. To address these issues, hybrid energy storage systems (HESS) are being used to enhance flexibility, reliability, and environmental performance. This paper proposes an optimal energy management strategy for a renewable-assisted AC microgrid that includes RES, a diesel generator and HESS. The optimization problem is formulated using the Lagrange Multiplier method to minimize operating costs, ensure a reliable power supply, and reduce pollutant emissions. Simulation results confirm that the proposed strategy effectively improves energy flow management, mitigates the impact of RES intermittency, reducing pollutant emissions by $\mathbf{7 5 \%}$, and supports the transition toward a more sustainable and resilient microgrid.

title: Performance Assessment of an Advanced MPPT Technique for Grid-Connected Photovoltaic Systems Using Real Irradiance Data
authors: Oumaima Echab, Noureddine Ech-Cherki, Tourıa Gueddouch, Omaima El Alani, Abdellatif Obbadi, Youssef Errami, Smail Sahnoun
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This paper proposes a Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) based on an integrated Incremental Conductance-Backstepping Control (IC-BC) technique to enhance the robustness and performance of grid-connected photovoltaic systems (GCPVS) under realistic operating conditions. The proposed method is evaluated using two irradiance profiles; the first one presents a typical clear-sky day, and the second displays rapid and uncertain changes. The feasibility of the technique is validated by Arduino Due through Processor-In-the-Loop (PIL). The results demonstrate the high stability of the technique, superior efficiency, and a diminished Total Harmonic Distortion (THD) with an average value of $\mathbf{1. 0 3 \%}$ with fewer fluctuations. The IC-BC outperforms other benchmarked techniques, proving its ability for real-time practical applications.

title: LVRT Control Strategy for a Grid-Forming Wind-Storage System Based on DC-Link Power-Energy Coordination
authors: Qing Dong, Shaoze Tian, Xiangwu Yan, Fan Zhou, Peisheng Shi
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: To address the transient energy imbalance of the DC-link and the sluggish response of traditional energy storage control based on DC-link voltage feedback during the low-voltage ride-through (LVRT) process of grid-forming doubly-fed wind-storage integrated systems under weak grid conditions, this paper investigates a grid-forming doubly-fed wind-storage system equipped with a supercapacitor energy storage unit. The DC-link energy coupling mechanism among the machine-side converter, grid-side converter, and energy storage branch during LVRT is analyzed. On this basis, an LVRT control strategy based on DC-link power-energy coordination is proposed. A simulation model of the grid-forming doubly-fed wind-storage system is established in MATLAB/Simulink, and comparative analysis is carried out against the conventional DC voltage regulation-based energy storage control strategy. Simulation results show that the proposed strategy can significantly reduce the DC-link voltage sag and recovery overshoot during LVRT, shorten the system recovery time, suppress post-fault oscillations, and enable the energy storage unit to participate in DC-link transient energy regulation more rapidly and effectively.

title: Movie Stream
authors: Dominik Bönisch Alpári, Léon Lancelot Repp, Christian Geiger
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 ACM International Conference on Interactive Media Experiences
abstract: None

title: ZoneSep: A Lightweight End-to-End Neural Beamformer with Post-mask Decoder for In-vehicle Multi-zone Speech Separation
authors: Longjie Luo, Junnan Wu, Lichun Fan, Zhenbo Luo, Jian Luan, Qingyang Hong, Lin Li
abstract: Recently, the task of in-vehicle multi-zone speech separation (IMSS) has attracted significant interest. However, a persistent issue in this area is the cross-zone leakage problem. To address this, we present ZoneSep, a lightweight, fully end-to-end neural beamformer equipped with a post-mask decoder to suppress leakage effectively. Additionally, we propose a novel loss function, SC-SI-SDR loss, which enables simultaneous optimization of both non-silent and silent zones. Experiments on the IMSS dataset demonstrate the effectiveness of our approaches, showing that ZoneSep significantly outperforms recent advanced methods while requiring only 0.65M parameters and 4.02 GMACs. The source code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/EeLLJ/ZoneSep/</uri>.

title: MobileROS: A Wireless-Native Robot Operating System for Mobile Robotics
authors: Boyi Liu, Qianyi Zhang, Yongguang Lu, Jianhao Jiao, Jagmohan Chauhan, Wen Wu, Jun Zhang, Dimitrios Kanoulas
abstract: The increasing deployment of mobile robots in dynamic outdoor environments necessitates robotic systems capable of maintaining reliability amidst fluctuating wireless connectivity. While the Robot Operating System (ROS) has established itself as the de facto standard for such networked robotics, its abstraction of communication as an opaque, besteffort utility creates a critical bottleneck: it fails to leverage physical layer (PHY) information, resulting in degraded performance and unreliable execution in fluctuating networks. To address this, this paper presents MobileROS, a wireless-native robot operating system that transforms wireless communication from an external service into a core system resource. Grounded in the Symbiotic Paradigm, MobileROS establishes a bidirectional exchange where network conditions inform robotic decisions and mission requirements guide network resource allocation. Based on service mesh principles and domain-driven design, our architecture implements a Hub-Engines-Cells (HEC) model. It features a central Hub for global optimization, three specialized engines (the Radio Information Engine, the Cross Domain Engine, and the Physical Adaptive Engine) for crosslayer intelligence, and distributed Cells as functional units. A key mechanism, Application-Driven Bidirectional Dynamic Slicing, allows robots to actively reconfigure network resources based on semantic urgency, transforming the robot from a passive observer into an active network controller. We systematically evaluate MobileROS across three cities (London, Hong Kong, and Shenzhen) in five scenarios: distributed visual SLAM, cross-domain LiDAR perception, V2X autonomous driving, hybrid multi-robot collaboration against WebRTC baselines, and partition recovery validating CAP-theorem-aware failsafe mechanisms. Results demonstrate that MobileROS maintains significantly more stable performance than standard ROS in mobile wireless deployments.We provide implementation details at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/MobileROS</uri>.

title: Multi parameter discrimination using multiple spectral troughs in a cascaded fiber sensor
authors: Riming Xu, Yanbo Li, Xingnan Chen, Jin Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Accurate monitoring of temperature, axial strain, and refractive index is essential for structural health monitoring, industrial process control, and environmental sensing. However, conventional optical fiber sensors often suffer from strong parameter cross-sensitivity and limited discrimination capability when multiple parameters must be measured simultaneously. In this work, a compact multi-parameter optical fiber sensing platform based on a cascaded single-mode fiber (SMF), multimode fiber (MMF), and long-period fiber grating (LPFG) structure is proposed and combined with a wavelength-based spectral demodulation strategy. Within this cascaded configuration, multiple characteristic spectral troughs originating from different physical mechanisms coexist in a single transmission spectrum, including multimode interference in the MMF section and resonance coupling induced by the LPFG. Instead of assigning individual sensing elements to different parameters, the proposed approach exploits the distinct and linearly independent responses of multiple spectral troughs to temperature, axial strain, and refractive index. Each trough is therefore represented by a sensitivity vector, forming a multi-trough sensitivity framework that enables robust multi-parameter discrimination through matrix-based spectral demodulation. Experimental results demonstrate highly linear and repeatable wavelength shifts of three representative troughs under variations of temperature, strain, and refractive index. The measured temperature sensitivities are −0.0733, −0.0833, and −0.0733 nm/°C, while the corresponding strain sensitivities reach −0.6706, −0.6916, and −0.8026 nm/mε, all with coefficients of determination exceeding 0.99. Under refractive index modulation, two troughs exhibit negligible responses below 0.0006 nm/%, whereas the third shows a pronounced sensitivity of −0.1426 nm/% with excellent linearity. By exploiting the differential sensitivity characteristics of multiple spectral troughs within a single cascaded fiber structure, effective discrimination of temperature, axial strain, and refractive index is achieved without additional sensing elements or complex optical paths. Long-term stability tests further confirm wavelength fluctuations within ±0.01 nm, demonstrating the excellent stability and repeatability of the proposed sensing platform.

title: Multi-channel Enhanced Synchro-Reassignment Transform for Robust Identification of Sub/Super-Synchronous Oscillations Using Synchronized Measurements
authors: Lixin Wang, Tianhong Niu, Zhenglong Sun, Han Gao, Shouqi Jiang, Guowei Cai, Tek Tjing Lie
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Synchrophasor-based sub-synchronous oscillations (SSOs) parameter identification is effective for monitoring SSOs, while its performance is significantly degraded by measurement noise, posing challenges to reliable parameter identification. This paper proposes a novel technique using multi-channel enhanced synchro-reassigning transform (MESRT) for robust and accurate SSO parameter identification. First, each measurement channel is transformed using the short-time Fourier transform (STFT) to construct a global multi-channel time-frequency (TF) representation. Then, an average modal energy-based strategy is developed to eliminate noise-induced spurious modes in the STFT spectrum. Subsequently, a three-step selection rule is applied to extract TF coefficients representing oscillation modes, from which the time-domain components are reconstructed. Finally, the Hilbert transform (HT) method is employed to identify the oscillation frequency and damping factor of each mode. The proposed MESRT method effectively improves the noise immunity and accuracy of conventional SRT by considering inter-channel correlations and leveraging modal energy to remove spurious modes. Case studies validate that the proposed method performs exceptionally well in terms of accuracy and noise robustness, demonstrating superior performance compared to existing methods.

title: Toward a Soft Robotic Gripper Integrating High Force Grasping and Dexterous Manipulation: The SOFRo Gripper_supp3-3693142.mp4
authors: Dongjun Shin
abstract: Developing a robotic hand that integrates high fingertip force, rapid response, and multi-degree-of-freedom (DoF) motion to achieve human hand-like capabilities remains a challenge in the field of robotic hands. This study presents the SOFRo Gripper, a soft, high-force robotic gripper designed to combine these capabilities within a compact pneumatic architecture. The finger module of the SOFRo gripper incorporates synergistically arranged chambers together with a multinode tendon routing strategy that distributes actuation forces, enabling both flexion and abduction/adduction motions while enhancing fingertip force. Furthermore, a Force-Enhanced Pleated (FEP) mechanism was applied to the chambers to increase fingertip force, resulting in a 32.41% improvement over conventional chamber designs. The proposed SOFRo gripper achieves a high fingertip force of 68.76 N and dexterous motion capabilities, enabling a maximum lifting force of 400.0 N and in-hand manipulation. To validate its versatility, extensive experiments were conducted across a wide range of tasks, including object grasping, high-force manipulation (e.g., lifting heavy objects, closing a valve), delicate handling (e.g., grasping tofu, inserting a light bulb), and high-speed manipulation (e.g., spinning a top, catching a ball). These results demonstrate the SOFRo Gripper’s ability to combine high force with versatile soft robotic manipulation.

title: SmarTracker: Leveraging LLMs to Automate User Behavior Tracking in Mobile Apps
authors: Shuohan Wu, Hao Zhou, Kaifa Zhao, Le Yu, Shudi Bao, Xiapu Luo
abstract:
The explosive growth of the mobile application market makes it a significant challenge for developers to increase their user retention. To deliver personalized experiences that attract and engage users, developers are placing greater emphasis on understanding user intentions and building detailed user profiles. A common practice is to insert tracking code during app development to log in-app user behaviors, such as button clicks and page visits, which are used in downstream software analytics tasks. However, current approaches for generating such tracking code face two key limitations: they either require substantial manual coding effort, or often fail to capture the full meaning behind user actions.

To address this, in this paper, we take the first step toward applying large language models (LLMs) for the automated generation of tracking code. We present SmarTracker, a tool that first automatically instruments Android apps to track finegrained UI-level user interactions. To capture the semantics of these interactions, SmarTracker analyzes Android app source code to extract rich contextual information. It then leverages an LLM to interpret user interaction contexts and generate tracking code that accurately captures the semantics of user behavior. We extensively evaluate SmarTracker on real-world Android apps. The result shows that it can reliably reconstruct user interactions with high accuracy, while preserving essential semantic details that reflect the underlying business logic of user behaviors. Runtime measurements further confirm that the system introduces minimal overhead. Overall, SmarTracker offers a practical and effective solution to integrating user behavior tracking into the mobile development workflow.

title: Distributed Retrospective Federated Learning for Microwave Passive Circuit Design
authors: Liang Qiao, Jiteng Ma, Shuping Dang, Mark Beach
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Electromagnetics, Antennas and Propagation
abstract: With the increasing difficulty of microwave circuit design for higher frequencies, there has been a surge of interest in artificial intelligence (AI)-aided electronic design due to its exceptional reasoning and problem-solving abilities. AI-aided electronic design is considered data-hungry because it relies on the quantity and quality of labeled data to train and optimize models. However, collecting a large amount of data is challenging for individual designers due to the high cost and computational burden. Furthermore, diverse and high-quality design data is distributed among multiple entities, and sharing raw data with high commercial value is unfavorable and could cause a breach of confidentiality. In this regard, federated learning (FL) can be introduced to tackle the dilemma between joint AI-aided electronic design and commercial confidentiality. However, unlike always-online environments, microwave software does not necessarily need to operate continuously and may have intermittent network connectivity. Additionally, various projects may have distinct specifications for circuit design. These factors pose challenges for implementing classical FL in microwave circuit design applications. The proposed RetroFL technique innovatively addresses these issues, tailoring FL specifically for microwave circuit design scenarios for the first time. By using this method, participants can train a generalized model asynchronously while safeguarding the secrecy of distributed data. Experimental results are provided to verify the effectiveness of the proposed retrospective FL method in modeling and designing microwave circuits. The proposed retrospective FL method allows the AI model to acquire knowledge from diverse sources, thereby enhancing the performance of AI-based circuit design algorithms.

title: IRASET 2026 Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)

title: Physics-Driven Electromagnetic Inverse Scattering With Limited-Aperture Measurements
authors: Kuiwen Xu, Yuxin Zhang, Rencheng Song, Shasha Hou, Min Tan, Haogang Wang, Yulong Zhou, Zhi Quan, Wenjun Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: Electromagnetic inverse scattering problems (ISPs) in engineering applications are typically constructed based on limited-aperture measurement data, which further exacerbates the inherent nonlinearity and ill-posedness. To alleviate these issues, this article proposes a novel inverse scattering network architecture, the limited aperture network (LA-Net), which can be flexibly applied to arbitrary apertures (90°–360°) without retraining. The proposed LA-Net integrates two core operators, the wavelet coefficient learning operator (WCLO) and the induced current reconstruction operator (ICRO). Specifically, WCLO is defined as a wavelet-compressed module that compresses the input deterministic current components dimension from 2N <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\times 2$</tex-math> </inline-formula>N to N <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> N. This operator significantly reduced computational burden. ICRO is an unrolling-network module to predict the final accurate induced current from a preliminary restored induced current by WCLO. Finally, a physics-informed loss function is used to constrain the model. The dataset contains scatterers illuminated by varying numbers of antennas, while the network could independently process data from a single incidence. This architecture enables the proposed LA-Net to handle arbitrarily limited apertures without requiring additional training. Under the joint constraints of WCLO and ICRO with multiphysics loss functions, the unrolling network is capable of reconstructing high-quality images of scatterers even with a small random number of antennas.

title: Crop Dynamic Root Depth Matched High-resolution Multi-layer Soil Moisture Dataset Generated by In-situ-Remote Sensing Data Fusion
authors: Xiang Zhang, Xin Liu, Aminjon Gulakhmadov, Jiefeng Wu, Xihui Gu, Won-Ho Nam, Wenying Du, Panda Rabindra Kumar, Veber Afonso Figueiredo Costa, Kganyago, Nengcheng Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Frequent agricultural droughts pose a serious threat to crop production. Soil moisture (SM), as a key monitoring indicator, is essential for developing effective agricultural drought indices. Most existing soil-moisture-based agricultural drought indices overlook variations in crops' water requirements across developmental stages and root-zone depths, instead focusing on a single soil moisture layer (surface or root zone). A primary challenge for field-scale agricultural drought monitoring is the lack of high-spatial-resolution, multi-layer soil moisture datasets. To address this gap, we propose a data-fusion framework that integrates remote-sensing products and pointwise in-situ observations to generate daily, 30 m resolution, multi-layer soil moisture across the root zone. The newly developed multi-layer soil moisture dataset demonstrated excellent performance, with an average Pearson correlation coefficient (PCC) of 0.989 and root mean square error (RMSE) of 0.034 m³ m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-3</sup>. We also tested spatio-temporal generalization and confirmed that the fusion approach robustly captures field-scale soil moisture when downscaling from 9 km to 30 m. Using this multi-layer dataset, we developed the Dynamic Root-Depth-Matched Agricultural Drought Index (DRDI), which dynamically adapts to crop root-depth distributions across phenological stages. Spatio-temporal analysis using DRDI revealed drought conditions across areas and seasons. Compared with existing indices, DRDI better reflected drought impacts on crop yield and provided more accurate field-scale agricultural drought assessments. Through data sensitivity analysis, DRDI demonstrated resilience to input perturbations. This work has important implications for Geo-AI–based generation of high-quality soil moisture data. More importantly, the proposed drought index offers new perspectives for the geoscience community and agricultural drought applications.

title: A Novel Cooperative Positioning Algorithm Based on High-Precision IMU Preintegration and Adaptive Sliding Observation Optimization
authors: Qingxin Wang, Ya Zhang, Shiwei Fan, Hong Zhu, Jianxiong Wei, Wei Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Environments with dense obstacles that cause multipath propagation of ranging signals present a critical challenge that need be urgently addressed for multiple agents cooperative system to achieve high-precision positioning. Aiming to address the problems of imprecise models in typical inertial measurement unit (IMU) preintegration-based graph optimization algorithms and the non-Gaussian distribution of ranging sensor noise in multiple agents cooperative system, this article proposes a novel cooperative positioning algorithm based on high-precision IMU preintegration and adaptive sliding observation optimization. First, a leader-follower cooperative positioning model is constructed, accounting for the spatial offset between micro-electro-mechanical system (MEMS) and ultra wide band (UWB). Then, a high-precision IMU preintegration model incorporating Coriolis force and centripetal force is derived. Finally, an adaptive sliding observation optimization model is designed to enhance robustness against ranging outliers. Multiple sets of simulations and multiple unmanned ground vehicles (UGVs) cooperative experiments have been conducted, validating the exceptional performance of the proposed algorithm. In multiple UGVs experiments conducted in obstacle-dense environments, the proposed algorithm achieved reductions in positioning error of 16.2% and 7.8% compared to algorithms based on rough IMU preintegration model and improved Earth-rotation-aware IMU preintegration model, respectively.

title: HF-DGF: Hybrid Feedback Guided Directed Grey-box Fuzzing
authors: Guangfa Lyu, Zhenzhong Cao, Xiaofei Ren, Fengyu Wang
abstract:
Directed Grey-box Fuzzing (DGF) is widely used for crash reproduction and patch testing because it can steer execution toward predefined target locations. However, existing DGF tools often rely on limited runtime feedback, which weakens both target convergence and target-state exploration.

This paper presents HF-DGF, a directed grey-box fuzzing framework with hybrid feedback-guided seed scheduling. HF-DGF integrates control-flow distance, value-flow influence score, and slice coverage. For precise distance guidance, we design a backward-stepping algorithm that computes basic-block-level distances on a virtual interprocedural control-flow graph (ICFG). For deeper target-state exploration, we introduce value-flow influence and its corresponding metric, the value-flow influence score. To control the runtime cost introduced by hybrid feedback, we further adopt a selective instrumentation strategy.

Evaluation on 41 real-world vulnerabilities (32 valid for comparative analysis) shows that HF-DGF consistently outperforms existing tools. On average, it reproduces crashes 5.05× faster than AFL, 50.56× faster than AFL++, 5.79× faster than AFLGo, 73.75× faster than WindRanger, 2.56× faster than DAFL, and 8.45× faster than Beacon. Notably, among cases where all fuzzers trigger the target crash, HF-DGF achieves the lowest code coverage, indicating stronger directionality. It also outperforms AFLGo, WindRanger, DAFL, and Beacon in static-analysis efficiency.

title: sUAV-JSL: A Novel Single UAV-Based Jamming Sources Localization Scheme in GNSS and Communication Denial Environment
authors: Ying Luo, Xiangshuai Kong, Chun Wu, Min Zeng, Jie Tian, Hong Jiang, Guanjun Xu, Zhenwen Ren, Li Zhang
abstract: In an electronic warfare environment where both global navigation satellite system (GNSS) and communication are denied, the ground-based jamming sources localization approaches suffer from limited mobility, whereas multi-UAV-based approaches become infeasible under communication jamming due to their reliance on data exchange. To address the above issues, this paper proposes a single UAV-based jamming sources localization (sUAV-JSL) scheme, which firstly employs an improved multiple signal classification (I-MUSIC) direction-finding module based on principal component analysis (PCA) to eliminate dependence on the number of prior jamming sources. Secondly, a single-UAV fixed-point iterative (SFPI) localization scheme is developed for dynamically updated observation geometry. Finally, a path planning strategy based on minimizing the Cramer-Rao lower bound (CRLB) trace is employed to improve localization efficiency. Simulation results show that the DF algorithm of the sUAV-JSL reduces the average DF error by 87<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> compared to the minimum variance distortionless response (MVDR) algorithm under a specific signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) scenario. The average root mean square error (RMSE) of the localization algorithm of the sUAV-JSL is 32<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> lower than that of the weighted least-squares (WLS) algorithm. Moreover, the sUAV-JSL achieves approximately 75<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> faster localization speed compared to the localization without path planning. In addition, the joint simulation results further demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed sUAV-JSL scheme.

title: IEEE Consumer Technology Society
authors: N/A

title: Prototype-Guided Unsupervised Semantic Segmentation with Explicit Concept Modeling
authors: Suxia Chen, Quanzhen Huang
abstract: USS aims to achieve dense semantic prediction without annotation, yet existing methods that directly cluster self-supervised features overlook the semantic gap between low-level representations and high-level segmentation targets. Prior prototype-based methods treat prototypes as post-hoc classifiers or static centers rather than an explicit semantic abstraction layer that actively participates in feature learning and decoding. To address this, we propose BS3 (Build a Semantic Space Stage by Stage), a prototype-based USS framework that bridges the semantic gap through learnable prototypes serving as a discrete semantic vocabulary. BS3 comprises three progressive stages: (1) a dynamic prototype discovery module learns prototypes via soft-assignment clustering with exponential moving average updates, regularized by diversity and spatial consistency losses; (2) a concept-conditioned decoder projects pixel features into prototype space through vector quantization and prototype-conditioned cross-attention to fuse semantic identity with spatial detail; (3) a prototype-centered contrastive learning paradigm aligns pixel representations using prototype assignments as grouping criteria via InfoNCE optimization. All supervision is self-generated without manual labels. Extensive experiments demonstrate state-of-the-art performance: 29.1% mIoU on COCO-Stuff (171 categories), 21.6% on Cityscapes (19 categories), and 72.7% on Potsdam-3 (3 categories); we also report preliminary zero-shot transfer evidence on ADE20K, BDD100K, and foggy urban scenes (see Section IV-G). The annotation-free nature of BS3 makes it applicable to consumer electronics such as smart-home scene understanding, drone-based terrain mapping, and mobile semantic parsing.

title: IEEE Consumer Technology Society Board of Governors
authors: N/A

title: Comprehensive Optimization of RPMFS Motors Considering Variable Numbers of Slots and Poles
authors: Đorđe M. Lekić, Slobodan N. Vukosavić
abstract: A new method is proposed for optimized design of rotor permanent magnet flux switching motors with high specific torque and low torque ripple. Slot and pole numbers are treated as independent optimization variables during the design process, alongside magnetic core dimensions to enable a fair comparison between machines having different slot/pole combinations and pole ratios, including Vernier machines. The method includes procedures for automated generation of the finite element model, winding distribution in slots and the end-winding arrangement for each machine based on its slot/pole combination. Since the level of the steel degradation due to punching and cutting differs significantly with slot and pole numbers, corresponding magnetic properties are adjusted for each machine. The proposed method ology also includes an algorithm for estimating and increasing the slot filling factor of all machines on each generation of the optimization procedure, providing an additional mechanism for increasing specific torque. As such, the proposed approach significantly reduces the amount of empirical input data, widens the optimization search domain and avoids suboptimal designs. Ac curacy of employed models and practical applicability of the developed method are experimentally verified on a 1.3 kW motor with an optimized slot/pole combination of 24 slots and 26 poles.

title: SGprAle: Self-Supervised Gaussian Process Regression for Average Localisation Error Estimation in Wireless Networks
authors: Abhilash Singh, Jaiprakash Nagar, J Amutha, Sandeep Sharma
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computing
abstract: This paper presents sGprAle, a self-supervised framework for predicting Average Localisation Error (ALE) in wireless sensor networks under limited labeled data conditions. The proposed approach integrates Gaussian Process Regression (GPR) with an autoencoder-based feature learning strategy to enhance robustness, generalisation, and predictive reliability. By learning task-agnostic latent representations from unlabeled data through a reconstruction objective, sGprAle enables the GPR model to operate in a compact and informative latent space using only a small labeled subset. Bayesian optimisation is employed to adapt kernel hyperparameters, ensuring calibrated uncertainty estimates and stable performance when labeled data are scarce. The framework is systematically evaluated across multiple labeled-data scenarios using ablation studies, uncertainty analysis, and statistical comparisons. Results demonstrate that the self-supervised latent representation substantially improves model robustness and label efficiency, allowing sGprAle to achieve competitive accuracy relative to existing methods while requiring significantly fewer labeled samples. These findings highlight the effectiveness of combining self-supervised representation learning with probabilistic regression for addressing the node localisation problem. The proposed model offers a reliable and energy efficient solution for ALE prediction, contributing to improved localisation performance and operational efficiency in wireless sensor networks.

title: Detecting SQLi Attacks Using a Compact Transformer-based Machine-Learning Framework
authors: Shoug Ibrahim Alsuhaibani, Hajer Mohammed Alsubhi, Ahmed Aljughaiman
abstract: Structured Query Language Injection (SQLi) remains one of the most damaging web-application vulnerabilities because it enables adversaries to manipulate backend databases, extract sensitive data, and potentially gain persistent control over critical services. In practice, SQLi payloads are frequently obfuscated (e.g., via comments, encoding, or keyword splitting), which reduces the effectiveness of traditional signature- and rule-based defenses and leads to missed attacks or excessive false alarms. This paper proposes a compact Transformer-based machine-learning framework that learns contextual semantics of SQL token sequences to discriminate benign from malicious inputs under such syntactic variation. The method applies SQL-aware preprocessing (query cleaning and normalization, tokenization that preserves structural keywords and operators, abstraction of literals into placeholder tokens, and sequence padding), followed by a lightweight transformer encoder and a binary classification head trained on labeled SQL queries. We evaluate the proposed model against strong baselines, including Support Vector Machines (SVMs) with TF–IDF features, eXtreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost), and a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) operating on token sequences. Across our experiments, The proposed model achieves approximately 99% accuracy on the evaluated dataset and F1-Score while maintaining robust detection under syntactic and formatting changes that commonly bypass feature- and rule-based systems. Ablation experiments further quantify the contribution of transformer components (e.g., positional encoding and attention configuration) to detection performance. The results indicate that a compact self-attention model can provide high detection quality with efficiency characteristics suitable for practical, high-throughput deployment scenarios.

title: Tracking multiple sources under angle-only measurements of multiple passive sonar sensors in cluttered 3D environments
authors: Jonghoek Kim
abstract: Considering 3D cluttered underwater environments, we handle tracking multiple sources under angle-only measurements of multiple passive sonar sensors. Here, angle-only measurements indicate that a passive sonar sensor can only measure the azimuth and elevation of a nearby source’s signal. For tracking multiple sources in cluttered 3D environments, we apply Gaussian Mixture Probability Hypothesis Density (GM-PHD) filter. In the GM-PHD filter, the Extended Kalman Filter (EKF) is applied for tackling non-linearity of angle-only measurements. Note that range uncertainty of a source is large for angle-only measurements, which can result in poor filter performance. Using angle-only measurements, source birth locations are unknown, since angle-only measurements do not provide source position information. Note that the angle-only measurements of any two passive sonar sensors can cross at a 3D point, and the crossing point yields the viable location of a source. If a crossing point of two angle-only sensors satisfies the maximum sensing range constraint, then the crossing point can build a source estimate in the proposed GM-PHD EKF. This paper considers the case where the clutter density of the 3D workspace isn’t known in advance. We address how to estimate the clutter density in an adaptive manner. To our knowledge, this paper is novel in tracking multiple 3D sources in cluttered workspace with unknown clutter density, under angle-only measurements of multiple sonar sensors.

title: Distributed Synchronisation of Heterogeneous Dynamical Networks With Nonlinear Diffusive Couplings
authors: Yongkang Su, Joaquin Carrasco, Iñaki Esnaola, Lanlan Su
abstract: This letter investigates the problem of input-output synchronisation in a class of heterogeneous dynamical networks with nonlinear diffusive couplings in the presence of disturbances on the coupling links. Focusing on networks whose agent dynamics satisfy suitable relative dissipativity properties, distributed sufficient conditions are established to guarantee bounded edge disagreement in the presence of external disturbances. Specifically, these conditions can be verified using only local information associated with adjacent agents and coupling links. To illustrate the proposed results, a heterogeneous network of Goodwin oscillators is studied, and a numerical example further demonstrates the advantages of the proposed approach under network topology changes.

title: Time-Varying Robust Sampled-Data High-Order Control Barrier Functions with Variable Sampling
authors: Hassan Bitar, Noel Maalouf
abstract: Safety-critical robots increasingly operate in dynamic environments where safety specifications evolve over time (e.g., moving obstacles) and control inputs are applied digitally via zero-order-hold at a fixed, often conservative update rate. These challenges are addressed within a unified robust sampled-data high-order control barrier function (HOCBF) framework. The sampled-data doubly robust HOCBF condition is extended to time-varying safe sets by incorporating barrier time derivatives and a tightening term that certifies inter-sample safety under explicit time variation. A reachability-certified variable holding-time policy is then introduced, selecting the next update interval online via a scalar feasibility test of the safety-filtered input, allowing longer holds away from constraint activation and shorter updates near constraint-active regions. Validation on a unicycle with a moving circular keep-out set and on a QBot digital twin shows that the time-varying treatment restores safety where a time-invariant baseline fails, while the variable holding-time policy reduces conservatism and unnecessary updates while maintaining forward invariance.

title: AutoCam: Hierarchical Path Planning for an Autonomous Auxiliary Camera in Surgical Robotics_supp2-3699217.mp4
authors: Alexandre Ley Banks Gadbois
abstract: Incorporating an autonomous auxiliary camera into robot-assisted minimally invasive surgery (RAMIS) enhances spatial awareness and eliminates manual viewpoint control. Existing path planning methods for auxiliary cameras track two-dimensional surgical features without simultaneously accounting for camera orientation, workspace constraints, and robot joint limits. This study introduces AutoCam: a system for automatic auxiliary camera placement to improve visualization in RAMIS. Implemented on the da Vinci Research Kit, AutoCam uses a priority-based, workspace-constrained controller that combines heuristic geometric placement with nonlinear optimization to ensure robust tracking. A user study (N=6) demonstrated that the system achieved 99.84% visibility of a salient feature, a pose error of 1.71 ± 1.54 degrees and 4.98 ± 5.00 mm, and computational efficiency (6.8 ± 12.8 ms loop time). An additional pilot study (N=6), in which novices completed a Fundamentals of Laparoscopic Surgery task, suggests that users can teleoperate as effectively from AutoCam’s viewpoint as from the endoscope’s, while still benefiting from AutoCam’s improved visual coverage. These results indicate that an auxiliary camera can be autonomously controlled using the da Vinci patient-side manipulators to track a salient feature, laying the groundwork for new multi-camera visualization methods in RAMIS. The open-source code for AutoCam is available at: RCL Autocam Public.git.

title: EDA-DM: Enhanced Distribution Alignment for Post-Training Quantization of Diffusion Models
authors: Xuewen Liu, Zhikai Li, Junrui Xiao, Mengjuan Chen, Jianquan Li, Qingyi Gu
abstract: Diffusion models have achieved great success in image generation tasks. However, the lengthy denoising process and complex neural networks hinder their low-latency applications in real-world scenarios. Quantization can effectively reduce model complexity, and post-training quantization (PTQ), which does not require fine-tuning, is highly promising for compressing and accelerating diffusion models. Unfortunately, we find that due to the highly dynamic activations, existing PTQ methods suffer from distribution mismatch issues at both calibration sample level and reconstruction output level, which makes the performance far from satisfactory. In this paper, we propose EDA-DM, a standardized PTQ method that efficiently addresses the above issues. Specifically, at the calibration sample level, we extract information from the density and diversity of latent space feature maps, which guides the selection of calibration samples to align with the overall sample distribution; and at the reconstruction output level, we theoretically analyze the reasons for previous reconstruction failures and, based on this insight, optimize block reconstruction using the Hessian loss of layers, aligning the outputs of quantized model and full-precision model at different network granularity. Extensive experiments demonstrate that EDA-DM significantly outperforms the existing PTQ methods across various models and datasets. Our method achieves a 1.83× speedup and 4× compression for the popular Stable-Diffusion on MS-COCO, with only a 0.05 loss in CLIP score. Code is available at http://github.com/BienLuky/EDA-DM.

title: An IoT-Enabled Real-Time Student Learning Environment for Continuous Monitoring and Personalized Feedback
authors: Amit Kumar Chandanan, Shweta Bansal, Alok Jain, Shuchi Bhadula, Lakshmi S.R, Yogesh Ramaswamy
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: A real-time student learning environment with an IoT is presented to increase continuous monitoring and individual academic feedback. The framework incorporates smart sensing Microchips, edge analytics and adaptive learning models to measure behavioral, cognitive and performance indicators with low latency rates. The sensor-based data streams are then gone through the lightweight machine learning algorithms that are executed in the edge to produce real-time insights into engagement, understanding, and task make headway. The recommendations delivered to the instruction are made according to the changing profile of the individual learner and this is efficiently done with the help of a dynamic feedback engine that helps in early identification of learning deficiencies and offering individualized therapy. The modular structure guarantees data interoperability, scalability, and data security, and allows easy integration with the current classrooms. The system would facilitate proactive learning support by taking advantage of multimodal data and constant observation and fuel educator decision-making using real-time intelligence. The system was found to have an accuracy of 96.4 on engagement detection and a low inference latency of 85 ms making it highly efficient in educational deployment in real-time.

title: PhotoChain: A Blockchain-based Framework for Secure and Verifiable Cross-Platform Photo Sharing
authors: Nanda Kumar V, Sachin V B, Sanju Jose J, Sarathiraja M
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: The rapid growth of digital photography and social networking platforms has significantly increased image sharing across diverse environments. However, existing mechanisms lack reliable guarantees for ownership preservation, privacy protection, and content authenticity, leading to unauthorized redistribution, tampering, and misuse. This paper presents PhotoChain, a blockchain-based framework designed to ensure secure and verifiable photo sharing. The proposed system utilizes a permissioned blockchain to record ownership, access policies, and transaction history in an immutable manner. Cryptographic hashing is employed to generate unique image fingerprints for tamper detection, while programmable logic enforces access control to restrict unauthorized usage. A hybrid storage model is adopted, where images are stored off-chain and metadata is maintained on-chain, improving scalability and storage efficiency. The framework also supports cross-platform verification, enabling authentication of images even after external sharing. Experimental results demonstrate significant improvements in transaction performance, access control effectiveness, and tamper detection accuracy compared to existing approaches. The proposed system provides a scalable, secure, and transparent solution for modern digital photo sharing environments.

title: Development of MID based fully integrated Radar Systems for the Automotive Industry
authors: Jabil Diri, Thomas Mager, Roman Dumitrescu, Bruno Mecke
publish: 2025 16th International MID Congress Mechatronic Integration Discourse (MID Congress)
abstract: The automotive industry one of the main drivers of the European economy is currently facing major challenges due to global competition. New models and innovations, such as autonomous driving, are needed to enhance competitiveness and secure the future of the automotive sector in Europe. Despite extensive research and financial investments, fully autonomous vehicles remain elusive for conventional use. Autonomous driving aims to significantly increase safety, improve sustainability, and enable more efficient resource utilization, yet it requires creative and innovative solutions.3D-Mechatronic Integrated Devices (MID) technology offers a promising solution to these challenges. The European research project MID4Automotive explores integrating radar systems directly into vehicle bumpers or other shaped plastic surfaces using a special MID lacquer. This innovation seeks to provide low-cost radar systems in multiple vehicle positions, leveraging radar’s established reputation for reliability and safety.

title: Two Types of Errors in the Assessment of Spatial Data Positional Accuracy
authors: Fujun Luo
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
