title: FPGA Implementation of Face Detection System Using MTCNN on ZCU104 Board
authors: Abinash Kumar Pala, Jatin Kumar Rauto, Sthitaprajna Sahoo, Shubham Ranjan Sahoo, Srikanta Mishra, Ashutosh Rout, Dinesh Kumar Dash
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: A crucial component of many computer vision applications is face detection. These consist of automated attendance systems, surveillance, and facial recognition [9], [10]. This paper introduces a Multi-task Cascaded Convolutional Neural Network (MTCNN) based real-time face detection framework on the Xilinx ZCU104 FPGA [1], [3]. The Wider Face dataset is used by the system to evaluate detection performance in various lighting conditions, occlusions, and poses [1]. The Vitis AI workflow is used to quantize, compile, and deploy the MTCNN model on the FPGA's Deep Learning Processing Unit (DPU) [3]. In comparison to conventional CPU-based systems, experimental results demonstrate that this hardware-based implementation offers high accuracy and significantly lowers power consumption [2], [4]. The results demonstrate that FPGA-based embedded AI systems offer a scalable, energy-efficient, and successful solution for real-time face detection tasks [3].

title: Biosensing in the Internet of Medical Things: A System Architecture and Design-Oriented Survey
authors: Mwaffaq Otoom, Mohammad A. Alzubaidi, Gheorghita Ghinea, Shayma Al-Tahat, Nesreen Otoum
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Diseases and healthcare concerns have become more prevalent in our society. As a result, more individuals are turning towards wearable technology as a way to keep track of their well-being. In addition, the introduction of the Internet of Medical Things will allow for continuous tracking of individuals’ health through a distributed network of devices.For engineers, the design of these networks must be done carefully in order to maximize their efficiency and effectiveness; however, engineers cannot focus solely on individual sensor technologies because they must consider all aspects of an IoMT ecosystem. This paper reviews biosensing systems used in the IoMT and provides an overview of how a wide variety of medical sensors can be integrated together for maximum effectiveness, illustrating how such systems can be organized into an entire ecosystem from the end-users’ perspective and how that broad Vision can be achieved through engineering design.This paper will help researchers and practitioners in the design and development of scalable, efficient and adaptable IoMT solutions. It will also help determine direction for future research into intelligent and sustainable biosensing systems.

title: Effect of nTiC addition on the performance improvement of pyramid solar still - A Renewable Energy Focus on Sustainability
authors: B. Deepanraj, L. Anantha Raman, Gaydaa AlZohbi, N. Senthilkumar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: A square pyramid solar still (PSS) with a single basin was fabricated using galvanized iron absorber coated matte black and insulated with thermocol for desalination of brackish salt water. Experiments were conducted in Chennai $\left(13.0843^{\circ} \mathrm{N}, 80.2705^{\circ} \mathrm{E}\right)$ using basin water depths (WD) of $1-5 \mathrm{~cm}$ dispersed with titanium carbide (TiC) nanoparticles with concentrations of $\mathbf{0 - 1} \mathbf{w t. \%}$. nTiC were dispersed evenly using magnetic stirrer and ultrasonication methods. Adding nTiC consistently enhanced thermal and desalination performance relative to the base case (without nTiC) by improving photothermal absorption and heat distribution. Across all trials, productivity increased from 0.186 to $0.812 \mathrm{~kg} / \mathrm{m}^{2}$ (4.37 folds higher), while water and glass temperatures increased up to $59.18^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and $57.36^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, respectively. Exergy output rose from 30.2 to $158.4 \mathrm{~kJ} / \mathrm{h}$ (5.25 folds higher). The best overall performance occurred at $0.5 \mathrm{wt}. \% \mathrm{nTiC}$ and 3 cm water depth, yielding 0.812 $\mathrm{kg} / \mathrm{m}^{2}$ productivity and $158.4 \mathrm{~kJ} / \mathrm{h}$ exergy, demonstrating an optimal balance between thermophysical gains and high-loading penalties such as agglomeration, viscosity rise, and optical shielding.

title: An Ensemble Machine Learning Approach for Imbalanced Brain Stroke Prediction
authors: R. M. Sakthivel Suriya, V.S. Adithyan, Ramkiran B, Pavithra M, Vijayabhaskar B
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Early detection of brain stroke can play an important role in reducing deaths and enhancing outcomes, but it is a challenging task owing to class imbalance in datasets, with the stroke-positive patients being minority samples (ratio of 19:1). This study evaluates and optimizes ensemble learning architectures by testing four machine learning algorithms: Random Forest, Support Vector Machine (SVM), XGBoost, and Neural Networks. The class imbalance issue is handled by employing hyperparameter tuning with GridSearchCV along with a unique algorithmic weighting technique that incorporates the use of both XGBoost and SVM in soft voting. Additionally, the SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) technique determined the important predictor variables, which included age and clinical factors, like hypertension and mean blood glucose values. Overall, this study has shown that ensemble methods applying gradient boosting and SVM algorithms provide better prediction results in assessing clinical stroke risk, with a final optimal accuracy rate of 95.04%.

title: PGT-GAN: Privacy-Gradient Tabular Generative Adversarial Network
authors: S. Moses Dian, R. Indhumathi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Publishing a dataset is essential for various research, data analysis, and the data mining process. The third parties can access this data to make informed decisions. Many of these released datasets contain highly sensitive information, including disease, diagnoses, treatment options, and symptoms. The publication of numerous sensitive information is not acceptable since it endangers people's privacy. The main focus of the proposed method is on ensuring that the released data is private and secure. Existing privacy-preserving data publication approaches for multiple sensitive attributes do not consider the fact that sensitive attribute values may have varying levels of sensitivity. To tackle this problem, we proposed a method, Privacy-Gradient Tabular Generative Adversarial Network (PGT-GAN). We developed ML-driven identification for Low-Risk Sensitive (LRS) and High-Risk Sensitive (HRS) security levels for different sensitive attribute values that had differing sensitivity requirements. Elliptic Curve encryption combined with sensitivity-adaptive Differential Privacy is used to encrypt and allocate an adaptive privacy budget to the extremely sensitive data. Dynamic k-anonymization is utilized during the publication of LRS data that respects privacy. An experimental evaluation is conducted against baseline methods, demonstrating that the proposed method achieves a better privacy-utility trade-off and significantly minimize the information loss of published data.

title: A Hierarchical Fusion Method Based on Dempster-Shafer Theory for Interoperability Assessment of Complex System
authors: Wen Yang, Jiajie Sun, Junhao Li, Zhekai Pang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: The interoperability assessment of complex system often involves substantial uncertainty and conflicting evidence, while conventional methods provide only limited descriptive information. This paper therefore develops a hierarchical fusion method based on Dempster-Shafer (DS) theory. Based on the characteristics of the multi-stage task processes, a hierarchical indicator system with six primary indicators and twenty-four secondary indicators is developed. Hierarchical fusion enables the method to generate the interoperability score, grade, pignistic probability (BetP) distribution, confidence, and conflict information. Simulation results show that the method distinguishes among different interoperability states, including high-level balance, stage transition, and local imbalance. Compared with the weighted average method, it is more sensitive to transitional characteristics and critical weak links. A correlation-aware conflict refinement mechanism is further introduced at the secondary-indicator level to alleviate conflict overestimation caused by highly correlated indicators. Monte Carlo perturbation experiments and $95 \%$ confidence intervals further demonstrate the robustness of the proposed method under input fluctuations. The proposed method provides a more interpretable and informative assessment of interoperability in complex systems.

title: Breathing Rate Estimation from Data Fusion of Multiple Elevated and Tilted FMCW Radars
authors: Mareike Wendelmuth, Alexander Yarovoy, Francesco Fioranelli
publish: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)
abstract: The problem of measuring vital signs with contactless radar monitoring in settings where wearables are not feasible, such as psychiatric clinics, is considered. Breathing rate estimation using three elevated and tilted FMCW radars placed at ceiling height is investigated in this paper. A dataset of 18 participants was collected, including activities with frequent posture changes. A processing pipeline to perform motion based segmentation, localization with MIMO beamforming, phase extraction, and short time window breathing rate estimation using autoregressive (AR) models is proposed. A simple fusion method to combine the individual radar estimates based on their correlation with an ideal respiration model is suggested. Robustness improvement across different body orientations and including active body movements due to multiple radar data fusion is experimentally demonstrated. A decrease of the mean absolute errors (MAE) of down to 0.88 respirations per minute (rpm) for the best-case participant, and an average of 3.33 rpm for all considered participants is achieved. The feasibility of reliable breathing rate monitoring of actively moving persons using elevated radars in realistic psychiatric care environments is shown.

title: SCHEMEFORGE-AI: An Intelligent Framework for Automated Scheme Analysis and Recommendation using Artificial Intelligence
authors: Niventh P, Vijayalakshmi N, Swathi G, Pavithran K, Prasanth B, Jeyaram G
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: SCHEMEFORGE-AI is a smart scheme analysis and recommendation system that is aimed at automatically identifying, classifying, and delivering welfare and service schemes to customers in a personalized manner based on the use of artificial intelligence. Most of the traditional scheme dissemination systems use manual rule based filtering and hard disk databases, which usually lead to lack of accessibility, lack of personalization and inefficient decision making. To overcome these shortcomings, the suggested SCHEMEFORGE-AI approach combines natural language processing to extract important attributes of unstructured scheme documents with machine learning models to classify schemes with a hybrid eligibility-matching system that takes learned patterns and rules together with rule-based validation. The scheme attributes are sampled with user profiles in a dynamic manner to produce rank and explainable recommendations. It is experimentally proved that SCHEMEFORGE-AI performs with a classification accuracy of 93.8% and recommendation accuracy of more than 91.7% at large-scale workload, which is significantly better than traditional rule-based systems. The accuracy of the recommendations is determined as the number of the correct matching schemes of the eligible schemes divided by the number of the recommended schemes.

title: Efficient Fine-Tuning Strategies for LLMs in Low-Resource Environments
authors: Satyanarayana Gudimetla, Gazal Sharma, Afsar Jahan, Deepa Mathew K, Anshu Malhotra, K. Adilakshmi
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: The adaptation of large language models (LLMs) to low-resource settings is still a significant challenge because of limited data and limited computational resources. The present paper suggests a new fine-tuning methodology using adaptive selection of subnetworks and synthetic curriculum augmentation to become able to learn effectively in the face of resource miniaturization. Rather than fine-tuning the entire parameter space, the method is dynamic and finds task-sensitive subnetworks at the token level and fine-tunes them selectively, much lowering memory and compute demands. To reduce the sparsity of data, a lightweight curriculum generator learns to synthesize successively more complex pseudo-samples using minimal supervision to inform stable and task-orientation adaptation. The method is tested on the WikiANN multilingual named entity recognition dataset, which inherently allows for experiments on low-resource on a variety of languages. Experimental results prove that the given strategy can attain a competitive performance with a significant reduction of training cost and data dependency in comparison with the traditional fine-tuning approaches. The results suggest the promise of structured sparsity with curriculum-constructive augmentation to be able to use LLM with a limited number of resources in a resource-constrained environment.

title: Mining Mathematical Misconceptions Using Deep Learning and Large Language Models
authors: Aya Lyousfi, Asmaa Mourhir, Naeem Nisar Sheikh
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Mathematical misconceptions represent one of the most persistent and consequential barriers to STEM education, systematically blocking student progression from elementary arithmetic to advanced calculus. Automated detection of these errors remains difficult because traditional classification approaches struggle with the extreme class imbalance inherent in misconception detection, where thousands of possible errors must be distinguished from limited training examples. This paper presents a retrieval-based system that combines dense neural search with Large Language Model (LLM) reasoning to match student errors to known misconceptions. A finetuned MathBERT bi-encoder retrieves the top- 25 semantically relevant misconceptions from 2,587 candidates, achieving 93% recall. Two LLM-based reranking strategies refine these results: ReAct-style reasoning with chain-of-thought prompting, and pairwise comparison with majority voting. The ReAct agent using DeepSeek-R1 improves mean average precision at rank 5 from 0.70 to 0.80. Unlike classification models that output opaque probability distributions, this approach retrieves humanreadable misconception descriptions, providing teachers with interpretable, verifiable explanations of student errors. Results demonstrate that retrieval-based architectures offer superior scalability for educational diagnosis tasks.

title: ThermoLine-Net: A Deep Learning–Based Algorithm for Overheating Detection in Transmission Lines
authors: Yizhao Wang
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: Overheating detection in transmission lines is essential for ensuring the safe and stable operation of power systems, as overheating may cause equipment damage and power outages. Traditional methods, including manual inspection and infrared imaging, often suffer from limited accuracy and poor adaptability to complex environments. To address these limitations, this paper proposes ThermoLine-Net, an improved YOLOv12-based framework for transmission line overheating detection. The model incorporates three key components: a Multi-Scale Heat Thermal Fusion (MSHTF) module to enhance cross-level feature interaction and improve small-target detection; an Environment-Enhanced Guided Attention (EEGA) mechanism to suppress environmental interference and highlight critical overheating features; and a Remote-Distance Micro-Heat Enhancer (RDMHE) to strengthen recognition of small, low-resolution overheating targets at long distances. Experimental results on a public power system dataset demonstrate that ThermoLine-Net achieves a mean Average Precision (mAP) of 96.5%, validating its effectiveness and robustness in complex transmission scenarios.

title: Production Atmosphere Requirements for the Use of Direct-Contact Prelithiation within Lithium-Ion Battery Production
authors: Felix Diller, Maximilian Hüesker, Maximilian Lechner, Lovis Wach, Sophie Grabmann, Rüdiger Daub
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Production Technologies and Systems for E-Mobility (EPTS)
abstract: Direct-contact prelithiation is a promising method to increase the energy density and the cycle life of lithium-ion batteries. In this process, the anode active material is brought into direct contact with lithium metal. The additional lithium compensates for the loss of active lithium due to the solid electrolyte interphase formation during cell formation and operation. The formed intermediate product, a combination of lithium metal with anode active material or already partially lithiated anode active material show increased reactivity to moisture, nitrogen, oxygen, carbon dioxide, and other air components. Though, the requirements on the production atmosphere for these intermediate products are not known, hindering the industrialization of direct-contact prelithiation. The knowledge of the production atmosphere requirements is crucial to ensure process stability and material integrity, allowing for the industrial implementation of innovative materials, production processes, and intermediate products. However, the influence of different production atmospheres on the stability and electrochemical performance of anodes in direct contact with lithium metal for direct-contact prelithiation has not been sufficiently investigated. In this study, silicon-based anodes were prelithiated by applying lithium foil onto the anode surface. A calender integrated in an argon-filled glove box was used to form this anode-lithium composite. The anode-lithium composites were stored under specific atmospheric conditions, including argon, nitrogen, and dry room environments with various dew points. Storage durations of 8hours, 24hours, and one week were evaluated. For comparison, pristine lithium foil was stored under the same conditions. The stored anode-lithium composites were analyzed using optical microscopy and electrochemically tested in both half and full cells. The results indicate that the storage of prelithiated anodes has a negative impact on their electrochemical performance in all tested atmosphere conditions and should be minimized. The anode-lithium composites show a faster change in color than the pure lithium foil. Negligible degradation was observed at dry room conditions with a dew point smaller than −47°C and an exposure time of up to 24hours.

title: Dual-Mode Adaptive Memory-Anchored Target State Estimation Based on Shooting Event Triggering under Dense False Measurements
authors: Luyun Xu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: To address the problem of severe contamination of radar tracking filters by dense projectile false measurements during anti-aircraft weapon firing, which leads to degraded estimation accuracy and even target loss, a dual-mode adaptive memory-anchored state estimation algorithm triggered by shooting events is proposed. Distinguished from conventional probabilistic data association and interacting multiple model methods, the proposed algorithm utilizes the system’s firing signal as a prior temporal trigger condition. During firing periods, it actively suspends measurement updates and switches to a memory-anchored mode based on the mean velocity derived from a dual-buffer sliding window, thereby circumventing false measurement interference at its source. Upon cessation of firing, a linear weighted smooth transition strategy is employed to achieve rapid target reacquisition. Theoretical analysis elucidates the error propagation characteristics of the memory-anchored extrapolation and the influence mechanism of the sliding window length on estimation accuracy. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm significantly enhances target state estimation accuracy and reacquisition capability after lock-loss in dense false measurement environments, providing a novel engineering solution for radar anti-projectile interference.

title: Experimental Evaluation of SMOTE for Rare Cyberattack Detection in IoT Environments
authors: Abdelaaziz Nassiri, Pr Azeddine Khyat, Pr Kamal El Guemmat, Mohamed Aazi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The increasing deployment of Internet of Things (IoT) systems in critical sectors has expanded the attack surface of connected environments and intensified the need for effective intrusion detection mechanisms. However, IoT security datasets are typically characterized by high heterogeneity, limited feature consistency across devices, and severe class imbalance, which degrades the detection of rare attack classes. In this paper, we experimentally investigate the impact of the Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE) on the detection of rare cyberattacks and proxy zero-day scenario in IoT environments. Our framework combines multiple sources of security relevant data. We evaluate the performance of machine learning models, including Random Forest and XGBoost, under both original and rebalanced data distributions, considering classification performance and inference efficiency. The results demonstrate that using multimodal learning and the right rebalancing methods greatly enhances detection reliability, particularly for rare,uncommon or new threats.

title: Towards Sustainable Textile Manufacturing: Implementing Intelligent Predictive Maintenance in the Indian Context
authors: Hirendra Singh, Bhim Singh, Ramendra Singh Niranjan, Gaurav Kumar Pandey
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: Indian textile industry currently facing problems like unexpected machine backdowns, wastage of energy, and a handsome amount of maintenance expenditure which led to poor productivity and sustainability. However, it has been observed in the literature that industry 4.0 technologies integrated with predictive systems come out with lucrative benefits. Current study reflects the effect of Intelligent Predictive Maintenance (IPdM) in textile manufacturing industry in India. Digital technologies of industry 4.0 like Industrial Internet of things (IIoT), Sensors, and machine learning (ML) models were used with traditional predictive maintenance that enable real-time monitoring, anomaly detection. The results indicate 68% increase in Mean Time Between Failures, decrease about 41% in Mean Time to Repair, machine availability improved from 82% to 93%, and maintenance costs decreases about 27%, while energy consumption reduced upto 11%. Outcomes of this study reflects the economic benefits and sustainability in textile manufacturing industry. Study provides empirical evidence on IPdM for enhancing productivity and performance in the Indian textile sector.

title: Optimal Financial Portfolio Management Using Deep Transfer Learning with Real-Time Financial Data in Cloud Computing Platform
authors: Shailesh Sayarmal Chaudhary
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Financial portfolio management is a critical task in the modern finance, which involves the skilful manipulation of large dynamic data sets in market settings in order to optimize returns and at the same time reduce risk. Conventional methodological frameworks are usually limited in their ability to capture sophisticated market dynamics as well as their ability to react to real time perturbations and hence constrain their forecast capabilities and effectiveness of decision making processes. The current research proposes a model that optimizes the utilization of deep transfer learning and real-time financial information, which is implemented on a cloud computing platform, to support intelligent management of portfolios. The salient financial features are extracted using DefenseNet and the convergence to the most informative subset of salient features is performed with the help of the enhanced bobcat optimization (EBO) algorithm. Then the finely-tuned features are added to a complex transfer learning model, which predicts the portfolio choices in the changing market environment; real-time U.S. market assets data are also exploited to support the presented framework. Practical experience proves that the methodology significantly enhances the predictive accuracy, augments portfolio returns, and is highly adaptable to market dynamics, which provide a sound and scalable solution to the modern financial decision-making in the domain of cloud-based systems.

title: Optimizing Neural Machine Translation: Enhancing Data and Decoding Efficiency
authors: Dhruv Mojila
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Neural Machine Translation (NMT) has revolutionized language translation by achieving state-of-the-art performance, but it faces significant efficiency challenges, particularly in data utilization and decoding speed. This paper addresses these challenges through two key contributions: improving data efficiency and optimizing decoding processes. For data efficiency, novel methods such as the copy mechanism for low-resource translations and non-parametric search-based guidance are introduced. Additionally, a universal NMT system is developed for low-resource languages with meta-learning capabilities for rapid adaptation to new languages. In decoding efficiency, innovative approaches like trainable greedy decoding and non-autoregressive translation are proposed, significantly reducing computational costs while maintaining translation quality. This work advances the field by presenting solutions that balance high translation quality with improved computational and data efficiency.

title: An Ethical Intent-Aware Electric Fence System
authors: S. Rathinamala, Banupriya V, Sivasian S, Vishal C. P
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Electric fences have been extensively employed in protecting agricultural land and restricted areas but, traditional methods work around-the-clock, ignoring whether intrusions are intentional or unintentional. Such a method can cause unwanted harm to humans and animals. In this regard, an intention-aware electric fence system is suggested, which will be able to detect any movement or intrusion around the fence and identify if the intrusion is from a human being or from animals. Unlike other approaches, this system will not send electric pulses to the fence right away, as soon as a person or animal comes within its vicinity. Instead, this system will alert the person of its presence with an auditory signal. The fence will become electric only after identifying the intention of the intrusion. In this way, a safety protocol will be introduced prior to activating the electrical charge on the fence, which is why the proposed approach is safe.

title: PaudhRog: Plant Leaf Disease Detection using Transfer Learning with InceptionResNetV2
authors: Sheenam Middha, Abhay Kumar
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Agricultural world is largely worried about reduced crop yields due to infestation by pests and diseases. The challenge that is facing the farmers is that they have had a hard time trying to detect and diagnose plant diseases especially at the early stages on the leaves before they appear in the fruits. To meet this requirement, the new product PaudhRog, relies on artificial intelligence (AI) to recognize the illness of plant leaves at an early age with special attention to crops like wheat, tomato, apple, and several tomato and other plants. The research will be able to surpass the admirable 99.5 percent accuracy rate using the potent InceptionResNetV2 model. To achieve this, such strategic measures as reducing the imbalances of datasets through oversampling and under sampling, integrating transfer learning with InceptionResNetV2, and systematic application of TensorFlow and Flask in Python have been used. It enhances its ability to identify the disease using high quality photos in the huge plant village collection that has a broad selection of plant leaves. Since the contribution of the meteorological conditions to the crop productivity is enormous, the weather widget on the website is not only usable but also provides the farmers with the necessary information about environmental conditions that are critical. PaudhRog aims to become an automated disease detector that seeks to be a proactive partner in safeguarding agricultural output and a major stakeholder in the global food security.

title: Ultrasonic experimental study of the water quality: Impact of salinity
authors: Abedlaaziz Ghafiri, Elhoussaine Ouacha, Hicham Banouni, Elhoussine Oukhai, Lahbib. Alahyane
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Ultrasonic technique is a powerful method for characterizing and non-destructively testing aqueous media. This technique is faster and less expensive than classical chemical methods, which require samples, reagents, and significant analysis time. This study experimentally investigates the impact of water salinity on the evolution of ultrasonic acoustic wave properties, namely ultrasound velocity and amplitude. To this end, we conducted six experiments with different samples using the monostatic configuration, in which the same transducer alternates between transmitter and receiver. The first sample, considered as a reference, is 200 g of distilled water. The subsequent samples were prepared by successively adding 10 g of commercial salt to the initial sample. The results demonstrate a direct relationship between the measured parameters and salt concentration. Significant variations in ultrasound velocity and amplitude of the backscattered signal were observed as the salt percentage increased. Ultrasound velocity increases while amplitude decreases. These results demonstrate that the proposed ultrasonic technique can efficiently control water quality under similar conditions.

title: Construction of Deep Learning-Based Dynamic Early Warning Model for Charging Resource Capacity
authors: Sihua Liu, Kai Kang, Min Li
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: With the rapid growth in the number of new energy vehicles, charging loads exhibit high randomness, strong volatility, and significant temporal coupling characteristics, posing severe challenges to the safe operation of distribution networks and the coordinated planning of charging infrastructure. Traditional methods for assessing charging resource capacity are often based on static or empirical models, lacking both timeliness and accuracy in early warning, and are no longer sufficient to meet the demands of refined regulation. To address these issues, this paper constructs a deep learning-based dynamic charging resource capacity early warning model. First, drawing upon multi-source data encompassing charging facility operations, user charging behaviour, and distribution network status, a dynamic charging resource capacity indicator system is established. This system unifies the modelling of temporal characteristics and spatial correlation features. Subsequently, a deep temporal learning model is introduced, integrating Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks with attention mechanisms. This enables joint prediction of charging load evolution and capacity risk trends, alongside the construction of a hierarchical early warning criterion. Practical case studies validate that the proposed model outperforms conventional methods in both prediction accuracy and warning precision, enabling effective early identification of charging resource capacity risks. This research provides a viable intelligent warning technology support for the operational control of electric vehicle charging facilities and the safety management of distribution grids.

title: Artificial Intelligence for Sustainable Agriculture and Improved Nutritional Outcomes
authors: Bhawnesh Kumar, Rajesh Kumar Verma, K. Adilakshmi, Mahalakshmi G, Swati Swati, Atul Singla
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: The issues of agricultural sustainability and human nutrition have continued to be disjointed, with yield-driven management or dietary change strategies applied on a population scale. This paper presents Adaptive Nutrient-Flux Agrointelligence (ANFAI), which is an integrated system that evolves itself to rearrange soil microbial nutrient pathways into human dietary outputs. ANFAI combines dynamic microbial-genome remodeling, plant metabolic steering and real-time community nutrient-deficit inferences to create an endless feedback loop of the whole soil-plant-human nexus. In the system, first, the seasonally responsive consortia of microorganisms are designed that become useful in the enhancement of the micro nutrient mobilization in soil. It then adjusts the crop metabolic activity with sensor-controlled adjustments of irrigation, light exposure, and nutrient micro dose that allow the control of desired increases in essential metabolites without genetic modification. Parallel, anonymized population nutrition information can also be used to inform a nutrient-gap model specifying that which local plant metabolites would most effectively enhance the adequacy of the local diet. A harmonizer uses reinforcing learning to coordinate such layers to maximize nutrient density, soil carbon permanence, water effectiveness and alleviation of micronutrient shortages. ANFAI enables ANFAI to connect real-time human health to changes in the ecosystem through the treatment of nutrients as dynamic fluxes, as opposed to static traits. Such approach offers a new paradigm to a sustainable agricultural intensification coupled with a certain direct enhancement in the nutritional quality on the community level. The suggested Adaptive Nutrient-Flux Agrointelligence approach aligns nutrient-flow forecasts with current soil, plant, and community nutrition signals with an overall accuracy of roughly 89%.

title: TamOCR: A Lightweight LLM-Assisted OCR Framework for Digitizing Tamil-Brahmi Inscriptions
authors: K Saranya, S R Nitthin Kumar, R Praveen Kumar, S Arun Nivesh
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: The digitisation of Tamil–Brahmi inscriptions is essential for safeguarding South India’s earliest written heritage, yet real-world OCR deployment is still challenged by weathered stone surfaces, broken or missing glyphs, inconsistent lighting, and highly variable carving styles. In response to survey findings that highlighted the need for modular and resilient OCR pipelines, this work introduces TamOCR, a lightweight, LLM-assisted OCR framework tailored for Tamil–Brahmi inscription digitisation. The framework combines a robust pre-processing stage for contrast enhancement and noise reduction, a segmentation module that isolates glyphs from complex stone backgrounds, and a recognition stage driven by compact vision models tuned for low-resource execution environments. To mitigate recognition ambiguities, TamOCR employs a lightweight language-model–based reconstruction layer that performs context-aware correction, symbol disambiguation, and transliteration refinement. We evaluate the framework on diverse inscription datasets with varying degradation levels and show substantial gains in character-level accuracy, word-level recognition stability, and robustness when compared with conventional OCR engines and heavier neural architectures. Owing to its modular design, the framework can be deployed on resource-constrained platforms, making it suitable for field archaeology, museum applications, and large-scale digital archiving. This work offers an early practical realisation of Tamil–Brahmi OCR using a lightweight LLM and provides a feasible pathway toward scaling the preservation of ancient scripts.

title: Structural Node Representation on Irregular Roles
authors: Wang Zhang, Wenjun Wang, Xuan Guo, Tianpeng Li, Pengfei Jiao, Lin Pan, Jian Wu, Xue Chen
abstract: Roles of nodes refer to the structural patterns of relations that reflect node identities or functions in the graph or network. Learning role-based node representations has been a hot topic in the last decade, as they are helpful in fully understanding the internal mechanisms of the network and improving performance on downstream tasks. In real-world networks, the dependencies between the roles are extremely complex, where the local structures of nodes can vary considerably even if they share the same role. However, existing methods ignore the varying dependencies and fail to discover roles having the irregular structure. To fully understand the above problems, we first propose an index based on a graph kernel to measure role irregularity, and analyze its influence on downstream tasks. Then for practically overcoming the shortcomings, we propose a novel framework based on a graph neural network and a variational auto-encoder with a cross-attention mechanism (GVCAM). It leverages the graph convolutional network to preserve the varying dependencies between nodes and the variational auto-encoder to capture the structural traits of nodes. We introduce the crossattention mechanism that fuses the two parts to adaptively learn the irregular roles. Extensive experiments on one synthetic and six real-world datasets show that GVCAM achieves competitive performance across multiple tasks compared with ten state-of-the-art baselines.

title: Influence of Grafting Modification on the Intrinsic Electronic Structure of Polypropylene Cable Insulation Materials: Insights from Single-Molecule Density Functional Theory
authors: Chenyu Gao, Wenyu Ye, Qingquan Duan, Shuai Hang, Xize Dai, Jian Hao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Grafting modification is an effective strategy to improve the electrical insulation performance of polypropylene (PP) for high-voltage cable applications; however, the underlying microscopic electronic mechanisms remain insufficiently understood. In this work, density functional theory (DFT) calculations are employed to systematically investigate the intrinsic electronic properties of pristine PP, styrene-grafted PP (PP-g-St), and AO3052- grafted PP (PP-g-AO) under external electric fields. The molecular conformation, electron density distribution, dipole moment, polarizability, hyperpolarizability, ionization energy, electron affinity, excitation characteristics, molecular orbital distribution, energy gap, and molecular surface electrostatic potential are comprehensively analyzed. The results indicate that grafting does not induce significant field-driven conformational changes but profoundly modifies the electronic structure of PP. Compared with pristine PP, grafted systems exhibit enhanced polarization capability, reduced excitation energy, increased electron affinity, and pronounced field-dependent electronic redistribution. In particular, PP-g-AO shows the strongest electron localization around oxygen-containing functional groups, the highest electron affinity, and the formation of deep and stable electron traps under high electric fields. Molecular orbital and electrostatic potential analyses further reveal that grafting introduces low-energy unoccupied states and highly heterogeneous surface electrostatic regions, which effectively capture injected electrons and suppress charge transport. These microscopic electronic features collectively explain the experimentally observed enhancement in breakdown strength of grafted PP. This study provides a fundamental molecular-level understanding of how grafting modification improves the insulation performance of polypropylene.

title: Compact Hybrid-Mode Diplexer With Nonresonant Nodes Based on MISL Technology
authors: Yu Zhan, Yi Wu, Ming Yin, Dongqi Gu, Ruipeng Liu, Weiqi Liu, Kaixue Ma, Kiat Seng Yeo
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This letter proposes a highly compact hybrid-mode diplexer based on the metal-integrated suspended line (MISL) technology. By embedding nonresonant nodes (NRNs) into suspended patch resonators, independently controllable transmission zeros (TZs) are generated to improve the interchannel isolation. A double-sided dual-mode metal-integrated waveguide (MIW) is employed as the common junction, which facilitates a seamless mode transition between the TE mode of the waveguide resonator and the TM mode of the patch resonator, synergizing the spatial compactness of suspended patch resonators with the high quality-factor of MIW resonators. Consequently, mode-conversion losses are effectively suppressed while preserving a miniaturized footprint. For validation, an X-band diplexer prototype was designed and fabricated. Experimental measurements reveal dual-channel operation at center frequencies of 10.36 and 12.28 GHz, with −15-dB reflection bandwidths of 9.31% and 8.91%, respectively. The realized minimum insertion losses of two channels are 0.85 and 0.77 dB, respectively, alongside an interchannel isolation exceeding 39 dB. This work provides an effective solution for the design of low-loss, high-isolation, and miniaturized diplexers.

title: SuperDP: Differential Privacy Refutation via Supermartingales
authors: Krishnendu Chatterjee, Ehsan Kafshdar Goharshady, Đorđe Žikelić

title: Towards a Physics-Informed Deep Learning Approach for 3D Phaseless Microscopy
authors: Nicola Cuzzocrea, Nadia Mammone, Cosimo Ieracitano, Yingying Qin, Krishna Agarwal, Martina T. Bevacqua
publish: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)
abstract: The inverse scattering problem consists in reconstructing the contrast of an unknown object from measurements of the scattered field it generates in response to a given illumination. The solution of such a problem is relevant in various applications, for instance, microscopy, biomedical microwave imaging and subsurface imaging. This paper proposes a new method based on a physics-informed neural network for the solution of the inverse scattering problem underlying 3D phaseless microscopy. The network takes as input the solution obtained by means of the Born Approximation. Furthermore, a loss function is adopted that integrates the physics of the scattering phenomenon. The method is preliminarily tested against simulated data using the MNIST dataset, which contains both handwritten numerals and letters and is a standard benchmark for machine learningbased approaches.

title: An Intelligent NLP-Based Framework for Automated Depression Detection in Social Media Networks
authors: Swati Jain Kansal, Nanhay Singh
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: The fast development of social media has provided some immense possibility of mass mental health surveillance by language analysis. The current paper describes an Intelligent NLP-Based Framework of Automated Depression Detection in Social Media Networks, which goes beyond the traditional text classification and includes agentic explainability, hybrid semantic railway, and adaptive inference pipelines. In contrast to current methods that use only static deep learning classifiers, the given framework combines contextual BERT embeddings with optional sub-word semantic vectors and a simple graph-based interaction model to discover similarities between the posts of the users in terms of relations. All predictions are supported by recalling semantically equivalent historical sharing, similarity scores, and allow clear and clinically significant explanations. The framework enables both scalable real time deployment in its full-graph reasoning and fast standalone reasoning, which is also scalable and can be used in real-time loads. Moreover, dynamic processing of new posts can be done without retraining, which provides robustness to dynamic online discourse. Making the representation learning, relational modeling, and explainable decision support a one-pipeline solution, the framework will offer a viable, explainable, and production-ready remedy to early screen depression in large-scale social media environments.

title: Advanced Artificial Intelligence-Based Adaptive Approaches for Personalized Education: A Literature Review and Research Perspectives
authors: Hasna El Bacha, Asmae Ouhmida, Nezha Benmoussa, Manal Ouakil, Abdelhadi Raihani, Bouchaib Cherradi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: A single teaching method cannot fit all students’ needs, as the increase of digital learning spaces has been lightly proved. According to this, tailored education is recognized as a promising paradigm to enhance learning effectiveness by customizing education paths and content for each learner based on his or her own individual profile. Therefore, AI has been extensively investigated as a technology that can be applied to the development of Adaptive Learning (AL) systems that are capable of handling the personalization process on a large scale. In this paper, we examine the AI-enabled AL systems in the context of personalized learning, and we present an evaluation of them. It covers existing techniques, such as machine learning and high-level AI techniques. It analyses the pros and cons of how transparent the two are as systems, how pedagogically relevant they are, and how effective they are in terms of personalization. This reflection opens up avenues for researching the design of more transparent, robust, and learnercentered adaptive learning systems and reveals substantial underexplored research areas. This will help the researchers and practitioners to learn about the trends and the gaps existent in personalized education.

title: Intelligent Deep Learning Systems for Aquatic Weed Detection: Models, Adaptation Challenges, and Future Directions
authors: Savita Sharma, Sunanda, Sakshi Arora
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: The unregulated growth of aquatic weeds is a serious threat to freshwater ecosystems because of the loss of biodiversity, decreased water quality, and disruption of socio-economic activities. Conventional monitoring techniques that rely on manual surveys are labor-intensive, limited in spatial coverage, and impractical for larger or more remote water bodies. Although there is promising modern work using deep learning (DL) and remote sensing for weed detection in agricultural systems, research in aquatic environments remains limited. In this review, we discuss DL models, Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), Vision Transformers (ViTs), U-Nets, and generative models and remote sensing approaches including Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV)-based multispectral imaging and satellite data, with a focus on their transition from use on land to in aquatic systems. The review will also discuss challenges that are unique to aquatic weed detection such as spectral noise caused by the water surface, seasonal variability, and lack of annotated datasets. A regionally specific case study on Surinsar–Mansar Lake in Jammu and Kashmir is discussed in terms of these challenges. Finally, we present a research roadmap including incentivizing the development of open aquatic weed datasets, combining multimodal sensing with DL models, and using explainable AI in decision-making for ecological management. Overall, the review aims to synthesize the use of terrestrial ecosystem weed monitoring and application of it in aquatic ecosystems.

title: Latent-Heat Cloaking: Adaptive Thermal Invisibility via Phase-Change Metamaterials
authors: Ahlam Alaoui, Mohammed Lahsaini, Younes Achaoui
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Thermal metamaterials enable precise manipulation of heat flux through mechanisms inaccessible to conventional materials. Among their most compelling functionalities, thermal cloaks redirect heat around a protected region while preserving the surrounding temperature field, rendering the core thermally invisible. Conventional cloak designs, however, rely on static material constituents. They remain intrinsically passive under time-varying boundary conditions, leaving the protected region unable to buffer thermal excursions autonomously. This work presents the first investigation of thermodynamic phase-change materials (PCMs) as functional constituents of a transformationthermotics cloak. It constitutes a proof of concept for a new class of adaptive thermal metamaterials. PCMs replace passive polymer layers within a concentric multilayer architecture. They absorb and release substantial latent heat across a narrow melting interval, giving the cloak a thermal shielding and storage capacity by stabilizing its core during transient excitations, without any external actuation. Transient finiteelement simulations are performed under sinusoidal boundary temperatures ranging from 10 to $40{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, across excitation periods from 30 to 240 min. The PCM-enhanced cloak reduces the core oscillation gain to $\mathbf{1. 6 \%}$, compared to $\mathbf{3 5. 1 \%}$ for the unstructured reference case. Controllable time lags reaching 57 min are achieved depending on the PCM selected. External field restoration is preserved throughout, confirming that thermal invisibility and adaptive core protection coexist within a single passive architecture.

title: PSEF-Net: A Spiral-based Attention Edge Fusion Network for Parkinsons’ Disease
authors: Yixin Lin, Haoming Ye, Cheng Yang, Xiaolong Chai
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: Early Parkinson’s disease is often accompanied by a decline in fine motor control, manifesting as increased line fluctuations and unstable trajectory control during writing and drawing. To address the challenge of simultaneously capturing overall morphological information and local anomaly details in the classification of Parkinson’s spiral images, this paper proposes PSEF-Net (Parkinson’s Spiral Attention and Edge Fusion Network). This method takes the original image and edge map as input, extracts features through the ResNet50 main branch and the Canny edge branch respectively, and introduces CBAM (Convolutional Block Attention Module) after the high-level features of the main branch to enhance the response of key regions. Detection is then completed through feature fusion and a classification head. Five-fold cross-validation results on the HandPD dataset show that the model achieves average accuracy, precision, recall, F1, and AUC of $90.17\%, 89.92\%, 0.902, 0.897$, and 0.941, respectively. Compared with the ResNet50 baseline model, the proposed method improves accuracy by 7.74 percentage points, providing a low-cost, non-invasive, and repeatable preliminary risk assessment for early screening of Parkinson’s disease.

title: Research on Consistency Detection of Airborne Software Requirements Based on Large Language Models
authors: Jing Qi, Zhinan Wu, Jinghua Sun, Meiqiang Shen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multi-scale Artificial Intelligence (MAI)
abstract: The quality and consistency of airborne software requirements are paramount for aviation safety and airworthiness certification. However, traditional manual reviews struggle to effectively perform global checks across multiple requirements—particularly in identifying terminology inconsistencies, logical anomalies, and functional redundancies scattered throughout large-scale requirement sets. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a novel requirement review framework that integrates the semantic understanding capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) with the structured reasoning capabilities of Knowledge Graphs (KGs). First, through requirement structure extraction, unstructured requirements are parsed into a graph-structured representation, leveraging variable associations to capture explicit dependencies. Subsequently, a graph-augmented consistency detection module is designed to identify anomalies and redundancies across non-adjacent requirements via topological analysis. This approach transforms the discrete requirement review process into a global, relation-aware analysis, significantly enhancing the depth of defect detection and optimizing the verification efficiency of airborne software requirements. Experimental results on a DO-178C-compliant dataset demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves a Recall of 95.0%, significantly outperforming standalone LLM and retrieval-based baselines, particularly in detecting complex logical inconsistencies.

title: Robust Radiometric Normalization for Hyperspectral Time-Series Analysis via Superpixel-Constrained Pseudo-Invariant Feature Regions Extraction
authors: Junyu Chen, Geng Zhang, Xiangpeng Feng, Haiwei Li, Yali Wu, Silei Lian, Siyuan Li
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: Radiometric inconsistencies across multi-temporal hyperspectral images severely compromise the reliability of time-series analysis. To address this issue, this paper proposes a robust radiometric normalization algorithm based on superpixel-constrained extraction of object-based pseudo-invariant feature regions. The algorithm first converts multi-temporal data into surface reflectance space to reduce major environmental disturbances. A superpixel segmentation strategy is then introduced to extend pseudo-invariant feature extraction from the pixel level to the object level, improving robustness against geometric misregistration and noise. Furthermore, a joint constraint mechanism integrating statistical, spatial, and spectral information is incorporated into the IR-MAD algorithm to enhance the reliability of pseudo-invariant region selection. Finally, a histogram-matching-based radiometric mapping model is constructed to achieve consistent normalization of multi-temporal reflectance data. Experimental results on ZY-1E hyperspectral imagery demonstrate that the proposed algorithm significantly improves radiometric consistency, with the mean relative absolute error controlled within 4.2 %, thereby enabling more robust change detection and quantitative analysis in hyperspectral time-series applications.

title: Comparative Evaluation of MPPT Strategies for a PMSG Wind Energy System
authors: Fatima Mokhliss, El Ayachi Chater, Najma Laaroussi, Larbi Chrifi-Alaoui, Laurent Delahoche
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Wind is a natural source of energy; however, it is unpredictable owing to its variability and randomness. However, it is possible to extract the maximum energy from a wind energy conversion system (WECS) if the Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) algorithms are selected and utilized correctly. Three techniques were selected for the investigation: the traditional technique (P&O), PSO metaheuristic, and Fuzzy Logic (FLC). To facilitate a fair comparison, the aforementioned tests were applied to the same Permanent Magnet Synchronous Generator (PMSG) with a rated power of 8.5 kW, delivering 7.8 kW of mechanical power owing to losses. It is asserted that Matlab/Simulink is the optimal software for verifying the results. The performance of each algorithm was compared under realistic conditions at various wind speeds.

title: Pneumatic Measurement System Design Based on Resource-constrained Controller
authors: Bo Li, Junguo Li, Anton Louise De Ocampo
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: With the rapid development of industrial-scale automation, the requirement for accurate, reliable pneumatic measurement systems is increasingly paramount. Existing systems have historically been criticized for their bulkiness, slowness, and inaccuracy. This paper presents an innovative high-performance pneumatic measurement system entirely built around a resourceconstrained microcontroller. Using a hardware-software codesign approach, the STM32F746 MCU is employed as the core processor, and a layered software architecture is implemented, including Kalman filtering and PID control algorithms. A detailed discussion of component selection, the circuit schematic, the overall architecture, and the algorithms is provided. Results from static calibration, dynamic response, and environmental adaptation show that the measurement accuracy is $\pm 0.5 \%$, the rise time is 12 ms, and robustness is maintained through temperature variations. Overall, this method further validates the technology and offers an additional route for pneumatic measurement systems in industry.

title: Comparative analysis of global desalination projects powered by renewable energies: technical, economic and environmental assessment
authors: Mohammed Azgar, El Ayachi Chater, Najma Laaroussi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The global freshwater shortage caused by the increasing demand for freshwater due to the growing world population and the effects of climate change require seawater desalination to be used as an alternative source of freshwater. However, traditional desalination methods generate significant amounts of greenhouse gas emissions, with estimates suggesting around 80 million tons of CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> annually. This study provides a comparison of 19 seawater and brackish water desalination projects using renewable energy as the power source. The projects include a number of different membrane-based processes (Electro-Dialysis (ED), Forward Osmosis (FO), and Reverse Osmosis (RO)), as well as thermal based processes (Mechanical Vapor Compression (MVC) and Multi-Stage Flash Distillation (MSF)). The focus of the comparison was on the technical aspects of each project (Specific Energy Consumption (SEC)), the integration strategies employed in each project (Grid Connection vs. Battery/Hydrogen Storage vs. Product Water Storage), and the financial viability of each project (Levelized Cost of Water (LCOW)). The results demonstrate that green desalination can be financially viable when compared to traditional methods, with optimal SECs of less than 3.0 kilowatt-hours per cubic meter and delivered water costs of 0.359 dollars per cubic meter provided that some form of subsidy or support mechanism is available to offset the higher upfront capital expenditures. The study also demonstrated that successful implementation of green desalination will depend on developing effective integration strategies (i.e., prioritizing product water storage over energy storage) as well as establishing policy frameworks that support and reduce the initial investment cost associated with green desalination.

title: Geo-Contextual Information Extraction and Summarization System using NLP and Large Language Models
authors: B. Krishnakumar, Rajesh Kumar G, Malathi Eswaran, Vikkram R, S Sadesh, T. Kumaresan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: The internet has no shortage of information about famous cities and landmarks. But ask about a small town in rural Tamil Nadu or a village in rural Bangladesh, and chances are that you will be directed to a wikipedia article or a third party unverified website that buries the interesting bits therein under an avalanche of census statistics, electoral history and administrative classifications. GCIES (Geo-Contextual Information Extraction and Summarization) was designed to correct exactly that. Given nothing more than a location name, GCIES automatically finds, filters and summarizes what that place is known and famous for. The system concurrently fetches the data on Wikipedia and OneFiveNine village database, processes the input through a custom Geographic Scoring Algorithm to select the appropriate result, filters out the noise with a SpaCy Named Entity Recognition filter, reducing the number of tokens used by more than 80 percent before it ever reaches Llama 3.3 70B, which generates 6 to 7 brief, culturally relevant insights with the help of the Groq API. Previously queried locations are served directly from an Upstash Redis cache within less than 100 milliseconds and entirely bypasses the pipeline. The entire system is a full-stack web application: React (Vite) on the frontend, Python FastAPI on the backend, deployed on Render. The architecture has been documented, along with design choices of each phase, and experimental results validating the system's token efficiency, disambiguation accuracy, and latency.

title: Lightweight Deep Learning Architectures for Edge Intelligence: Budget-Aware Compression–Architecture Co-Design
authors: Mohammad Al Khaldy, Ziadoon Otaiwi, Ahmad Al-Qerem
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Edge intelligence moves learning and inference closer to sensors and users, reducing round-trip latency and limiting raw data transfer. The same shift exposes a mismatch: modern deep networks are accurate but often exceed the memory, compute throughput, and energy budgets of IoT nodes, mobile devices, and embedded accelerators. Lightweight architectures and compression methods are widely available, yet they are frequently applied in sequence-first select a mobile-friendly backbone, then compress until the model fits. This workflow can waste accuracy because architecture choices constrain which compression operators remain stable, and compression changes operator shapes that determine runtime speed and peak activation memory. This paper proposes a budget-aware co-design framework that couples architecture scaling (width, depth, and input resolution) with structured pruning, quantization, and knowledge distillation in a single constrained optimization procedure. A device-aware cost model estimates weight storage, peak activation memory, and latency using a simplified compute-memory balance bound, and a deployment verification loop is described to filter candidates. Using a transparent, illustrative memory-bound analysis across representative edge profiles, we show how activation constraints can dominate in SRAM-limited settings and why early-layer structured pruning can complement INT8 inference by reducing both arithmetic and activation footprints. The paper concludes with design guidelines that connect system-level constraints to concrete architecture and compression choices.

title: XAI for Automated Prediction Model for Cyber Incident Response and Security Support in Cloud
authors: Chandra Shekar Reddy Avula
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: The rising number and sophistication of cyber incidents in the cloud environments necessitates intelligent, explainable, and automated response mechanisms to assist in making timely and dependable protection decisions. The classic incident response systems have been noted to have low flexibility, lacks visibility, and slow forensic investigation mechanisms that make them less effective in countering new threats. The paper will find a solution to these issues by providing an explainable artificial intelligence (XAI)-based automated prediction model on cyber incident response and support of security in cloud infrastructures. A domain attention (DAtt) mechanism with critical incident features, a modified electric fish optimization (MEFO) algorithm to select optimal features and policy tuning, and a deep Q-network (DQN) to be trained and analyze the patterns of complex cyber incidents to make adaptive decisions. Reinforcement learning-based post-incident investigation was supported by generating a controlled malware forensics dataset of 26 paired infected and uninfected memory dumps based on 13 isolated windows virtual machines analysed with the Volatility framework. The experimental outcomes demonstrate the high accuracy of the proposed XAI-enabled model, the high response time, and the enhanced interpretability, the effectiveness of the integration of XAI with DQN in providing reliable cloud-based cyber incident responses.

title: A Comprehensive Review of Wireless Communication Protocols for Battery Management Systems in Electric Vehicles
authors: Challa Krishna Rao, Sarat Kumar Sahoo, Gandam Deepika Rani, Ilupothi Jahnavi, Konchada Lahari, Bora Manikantha, Boddepalli Manmadha Kumar, B. Srinivasarao, V V Appalanaidu Menda
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: Recent studies identify BLE and ZigBee as promising candidates for low power, short range multi node architectures, while Wi-Fi and UWB can deliver higher through put and lower latency with careful electromagnetic and reliability optimization. NFC based and hybrid approaches are also gaining interest for secure data readout, second life battery applications, and maintenance without continuous wireless connectivity. Beyond telemetry, next generation WBMS platforms are integrating cloud connectivity, digital twins, and machine learning algorithms to enable predictive diagnostics, remaining useful life estimation, and over the air (OTA) parameter optimization. These capabilities improve state of charge (SOC) and state of health (SOH) estimation accuracy while enabling fleet level monitoring and continuous, software defined performance enhancement throughout the battery lifecycle. However, the deployment of wireless BMS introduces new design challenges concerning cyber security, functional safety, synchronization, and coexistence with other in vehicle wireless systems, along with the need for standardized, automotive qualified communication protocols and certification frameworks. This paper surveys the state-of-the-art developments in wireless communication for EV BMS, encompassing industrial implementations, experimental demonstrators, and research proposals up to 2025. It provides a comparative analysis of BLE, ZigBee, Wi-Fi, UWB, LoRa, and NFC technologies in terms of performance, scalability, cost, and safety implications. On this basis, the study identifies application oriented technology choices and highlights promising hybrid and cloud integrated architectures that are shaping the future evolution of BMS in electric vehicles.

title: Comparison Study of Tianmu-1 Radio Occultation Data with ERA5 and MERRA-2 Reanalysis Datasets
authors: Xia Sheng, Zewen Ming, Guowen Ma
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: The radio occultation data from the domestic Tianmu-1 constellation provide a high-quality data source for global meteorological observation. This study uses June 2024 Tianmu-1 radio occultation temperature profiles as the main dataset. ERA5 and MERRA-2 reanalysis data are adopted as reference benchmarks. We build a standardized spatiotemporal matching and quality control framework over the conterminous United States <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(25^{\circ} \mathrm{N}-49^{\circ} \mathrm{N}, 67^{\circ} \mathrm{W}-124^{\circ} \mathrm{W}\right)$</tex>. We then compare the datasets from three perspectives: overall statistics, vertical stratification, and regional spatial distribution. We also analyze detection accuracy variations and error sources at different altitude layers. The results show that the correlation coefficient between Tianmu-1 temperature data and the two reanalysis datasets reaches 0.989, and the mean biases relative to ERA5 and MERRA-2 are −1.35 K and −1.42 K, respectively. The detection accuracy in the middle and upper atmosphere (above 500 hPa) is excellent. The absolute value of bias is mostly less than 1 K. A significant systematic negative bias (−4.28 K to −5.44 K) appears in the lower troposphere (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$700-850 \text{hPa}$</tex>). This bias is caused by the inherent physical mechanism of radio occultation detection and water vapor. This study confirms that Tianmu-1 radio occultation data have good overall applicability. They can provide reliable domestic data support for meteorological observation over the conterminous United States. This work also serves as a technical reference for error correction and operational application of GNSS radio occultation data.

title: SSA without Dominance for Higher-Order Programs
authors: Roland Leißa, Johannes Max Griebler
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Programming Languages
abstract: None

title: Interpretable Diabetes Risk Classification using Optimized Deep Learning and Baseline Machine Learning Models
authors: Sujith Jayaprakash, Annette Nayana Nellyet, Chitra Ganesh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: Diabetes prediction is one of the important health-related applications in the deep learning (DL) and machine learning (ML) community. However, most of the studies on diabetes prediction have focused on achieving high accuracy and ignored the effect of the demographic constraints and class imbalance in the datasets. Although they achieve good results, the results are not validated. In this paper, we propose a transparent prediction model that integrates the optimal deep learning model with the traditional machine learning approaches. We applied median imputation and Min-Max normalization on the dataset and applied the SMOTE-based oversampling technique only on the training folds to avoid data leaking. We performed 5-fold cross-validation experiments on logistic regression, feedforward neural networks, and an autoencoder-augmented classifier and presented the accuracy and F1-score along with the threshold-dependent ROC–AUC and precision–recall curves. The logistic regression model is used as a baseline while the modified neural network achieved an accuracy of 85% for discrimination. The SHAP analysis provided that the glucose, BMI, and insulin are the most important features which is in line with the clinical symptoms of diabetes. The main limitation of this study is that the results are only validated in a gender-specific dataset and there is no validation of the generalizability of the findings. The advantages of the proposed model are that the prediction accuracy and interpretability can be achieved simultaneously which addresses the long-standing problem of diabetes prediction.

title: Characterizing and Accelerating Spacecraft Onboard Workloads on RISC-V Platform
authors: Boul Chandra Garai, Sumit Kumar Mandal, K. R. Yogesh Prasad, R Govindarajan
abstract: This paper studies 28 production spacecraft workloads from ISRO (Indian Space Research Organisation) missions to find out which computations actually limit performance on resource-constrained space processors. Spacecraft onboard processors face strict power and thermal constraints while performing critical real-time calculations for navigation, control, and data processing. Using a Basic Computational Element (BCE) breakdown, we find that software trigonometric calls (up to 36% of execution time) and small matrix operations (13%–41%) are the main bottlenecks across navigation, attitude control, guidance, and remote-sensing workloads. To check whether these findings translate into useful hardware, we build two domainspecific accelerators—a CORDIC (COordinate Rotation DIgital Computer) trigonometric unit and a 3 × 3 systolic matrix accelerator—integrated into a RISC-V processor through custom instruction set extensions (RVTrig, RVMatrix) and evaluated using cycle-approximate gem5 simulation. Across 18 representative workloads, the combined accelerators give a 3.13× geometric mean speedup and 37.2% energy reduction, reaching up to 9.29× on the most accelerator-friendly workload. Section VI introduces a timestep-based roofline model that tracks algorithmic progress rather than raw operations, so that an accelerated system no longer looks slower under the standard FLOPs-based view.

title: Configuring Circular Supply Networks: Digital Technology Embedded Pathways
authors: Shenghao Xie, Atif Sarwar, Yu Gong, Eric Ka Ho Leung
abstract: Circular Supply Networks (CSNs) are essential organizational forms for advancing the transition to a circular economy and can be supported by digital technologies. However, limited understanding exists of how digital technologies become embedded in CSN configurations. This study addresses this gap by examining the structuring logics and governance dynamics of digital technology-enabled CSNs. The study employs a rigorous multiple-case study design, drawing on 30 interviews and extensive archival data analyzed through a systematic coding approach. Applying Actor–Network Theory (ANT), this research analyzes five blockchain applications across different industries to investigate how digital technologies become embedded in CSN configurations. The findings reveal four translation processes underlying CSN configuration: anchoring actor tensions, actor-centric alignment, technology-embedded governance, and the emergence of configured CSNs. These processes give rise to two distinct configuration pathways, enhancement and structuration. By integrating ANT, the study advances understanding of how digital technologies configure CSNs by unpacking the sociotechnical dynamics through which human and non-human actors co-shape CSNs. The insights highlight the importance of adopting a network-oriented perspective when implementing circular initiatives and designing governance mechanisms that align with underlying value creation.

title: Trap-Based Modeling and Mechanisms of Non-Monotonic Hot-Carrier Degradation in Cryogenic CMOS Devices
authors: Junru Qu, Xinze Li, Dong Liu, Ran Tao, Yuanxi Liao, Yuchen Gu, Di Yang, Qing Tian, Ying Sun, Yongyu Wu, Dawei Gao, Bing Chen, Yong Ding, Genquan Han, Ran Cheng
publish: IEEE Journal of the Electron Devices Society
abstract: This work experimentally characterized and investigated the temperature dependence of hot carrier injection (HCI) at low temperature (LT) for 130 nm foundry-level CMOS with no resolvable transient self-heating-effect (SHE) induced current droop under the tested conditions. Cryogenic virtual source transistor model together with trap models at LT are developed and employed to extract the related carrier and trap parameters. For both n- and pFETs, the temperature dependence of HCI-induced degradation (HCD) exhibits a non-monotonic aggravation from 300 K to 8 K. For nFETs, the HCD is mainly caused by interface traps, while for pFETs, it is additionally controlled by the electron trapping to existing oxide traps, which causes the opposite shift of Vth when the stress time is short and the temperature is low. Based on the extracted trap parameters, both types of traps strongly correlate to the mean free path λ. Therefore, ΔVth shows the maximum value at 45 K, where λ reaches the maximum due to the reduced phonon scattering and the enhanced ionized impurity scattering. The temperature, voltage and time dependence of the HCD can be precisely calculated by the trap-based model developed in this work. Careful design and process optimizations are necessary to improve the LT HCI reliability of Si CMOS for various LT applications.

title: Fractional-Order Numerical Analysis of Supercontinuum Generation in Photonic Crystal Fibers
authors: Andrés González García, Gerardo González García, J Juan Rosales
abstract: In this work, a dimensionally consistent fractional correction of the generalized nonlinear Schrödinger equation is proposed for the numerical analysis of supercontinuum generation in photonic crystal fibers. Unlike a direct substitution of the classical dispersion operator with a fractional derivative, the proposed model introduces a fractional-order perturbation in the dispersion relation in the frequency domain. This correction is controlled by the fractional parameter α, scaled by a coefficient with physically consistent units, and constructed in such a way that it vanishes exactly when α = 1.0. Therefore, the model recovers the classical generalized nonlinear Schrödinger equation (GNLSE) without residual linear terms. The model includes higher-order dispersion, Kerr nonlinearity, delayed Raman response, optical self-steepening, linear losses, and is solved using an adaptive propagation scheme based on the Split-Step Fourier (SSF) method combined with a fourth-order Runge–Kutta (RK4) treatment for the nonlinear operator. The corrected formulation was validated analytically and numerically. In particular, the classical limit was verified by comparing the fractional solver with α = 1.0 against a standard GNLSE solver. This comparison yielded a relative field error of 0.0 and a maximum fractional correction value of 0.0 km−1. Numerical simulations were performed for α = 0.5,0.8,1.0,1.2 and 1.5, using the same physical and numerical parameters in all cases. The results show that the fractional parameter modifies the spectral-temporal redistribution of the optical pulse, rather than acting as a monotonic amplifier of spectral broadening. Under the simulated conditions, the largest bandwidth, defined at the −20 dB level was obtained for α = 1.2, with an approximate value of 1052.9 nm. However, the RMS spectral width decreased from 242.56 nm for α = 0.5 to 219.13 nm for α = 1.5, while the RMS temporal width decreased from 2.31 ps to 1.96 ps in the same α range. These results indicate that the proposed fractional correction acts as an effective dispersive redistribution parameter for controlling supercontinuum dynamics in photonic crystal fibers.
