Convergence analyses were also conducted with respect to the number of FFT points, the initial propagation step, and the temporal window. The adopted mesh, N=215, along with an initial propagation step ΔZ0=5×10−10 km, provided stable metrics for RMS spectral width and RMS temporal width, and output energy. The bandwidth at - 20 dB showed greater sensitivity to weak components in the spectral tails and is therefore reported as a complementary threshold-based metric. Overall, the proposed formulation provides a more rigorous fractional framework for studying controlled deviations from the classical dynamics of supercontinuum generation, while maintaining consistency with standard GNLSE.

title: Dynamic Voltage Balancing of Series-Connected SiC MOSFETs via Combined dv/dt Feedback and Adaptive drive delay control
authors: Zhangyong Chen, Yuanxin Li, Yunfeng Wu, Lei Ren, Zhijie Luo, Fan Feng
abstract: Silicon carbide (SiC) MOSFETs are ideal for high-voltage power converters, but their limited voltage rating necessitates series connection to meet multi-kilovolt system requirements. Dynamic voltage imbalance during switching transients, caused by mismatches in parasitic parameters and gate driver delays, poses a critical challenge for series-connected SiC MOSFETs. Passive balancing techniques suffer from energy dissipation and efficiency loss, while existing active methods often have slow response or complex implementation. To address these limitations, this article proposes a novel active gate drive (AGD) scheme combining dv/dt feedback control and adaptive drive delay control for dynamic voltage balancing of series-connected SiC MOSFETs. The method employs voltage-controlled current sources/sinks to regulate gate current during switching transients, supplemented by Field-Programmable Gate Array (FPGA)-controlled delay adjustment based on detected voltage slew rates, enabling rapid convergence to balanced states. Experimental verification on double-pulse test circuits demonstrates that the proposed AGD reduces voltage imbalance to within 5% for both dual-device and four-device series configurations, confirming its effectiveness and feasibility for high-speed applications.

title: Compressed-CSI Feedback with Near Real-time Domain Adaptation_supp1-3698862.pdf
authors: Hyun Jong Yang
abstract: This paper proposes a lightweight, pretraining-free framework for compressed channel state information (CSI) feedback under time-varying channel conditions. While existing deep learning-based methods suffer from performance degradation under environmental changes and require costly on-device retraining, our method enables a user equipment (UE) to locally rebuild a sparse linear codebook from fresh CSI samples gathered over a short post-shift interval. The updated codebook is then conveyed to the base station (BS) in a one-shot update with low signaling overhead. Extensive evaluations show that the proposed method substantially reduces post-shift CSI mismatch and recovers spectral efficiency under representative domain shifts. In several representative operating points, the adapted scheme operates close to the corresponding perfect-CSI benchmark, while the absolute throughput recovery becomes much larger in mismatch-sensitive transmission modes. The results further show that the practical throughput impact of CSI mismatch is strongly operating-mode dependent, which motivates system-level evaluation beyond reconstruction error alone.

title: Joint Precoding and Surface Morphing Design for Flexible Intelligent Metasurfaces-Aided Cell-Free Massive MIMO Systems
authors: Dongqi Wang, Enyu Shi, Jiayi Zhang, Jiancheng An, Derrick Wing Kwan Ng, Bo Ai
abstract: This correspondence investigates a flexible intelligent metasurfaces (FIMs)-aided cell-free (CF) massive multiple-input multiple-output (mMIMO) architecture and proposes an optimization framework for joint precoding and FIMs surface-shaping optimization. Specifically, a sum-rate maximization problem is formulated, subject to transmit power and FIMs morphing range constraints. To address the problem's inherent non-convexity, we employ an alternating optimization (AO) approach to decouple the joint optimization of precoding and surface morphing. Under the considered simulation setup, FIMs-enabled geometric reconfiguration achieves up to a 47% sum-rate improvement over rigid-array baselines, demonstrating the performance potential of geometry-adaptive FIMs designs. Moreover, the performance gain becomes more pronounced as the number of users increases, highlighting the strong potential of FIMs to support dense user connectivity in CF mMIMO networks.

title: SuperCarver: Texture-Consistent 3D Geometry Super-Resolution for High-Fidelity Surface Detail Generation
authors: Qijian Zhang, Xiaozheng Jian, Xuan Zhang, Wenping Wang, Junhui Hou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Conventional production workflow of high-precision mesh assets necessitates a cumbersome and laborious process of manual sculpting by specialized 3D artists/modelers. The recent years have witnessed remarkable advances in AI-empowered 3D content creation for generating plausible structures and intricate appearances from images or text prompts. However, synthesizing realistic surface details still poses great challenges, and enhancing the geometry fidelity of existing lower-quality 3D meshes (instead of image/text-to-3D generation) remains an open problem. In this paper, we introduce SuperCarver, a 3D geometry super-resolution pipeline for supplementing texture-consistent surface details onto a given coarse mesh. We start by rendering the original textured mesh into the image domain from multiple viewpoints. To achieve detail boosting, we construct a deterministic prior-guided normal diffusion model, which is fine-tuned on a carefully curated dataset of paired detail-lacking and detail-rich normal map renderings. To update mesh surfaces from potentially imperfect normal map predictions, we design a noise-resistant inverse rendering scheme through deformable distance field. Experiments demonstrate that our SuperCarver is capable of generating realistic and expressive surface details depicted by the actual texture appearance, making it a powerful tool to both upgrade historical low-quality 3D assets and reduce the workload of sculpting high-poly meshes.

title: Deep-Learning Based Hardware-Efficient Load Identification for Power Amplifiers
authors: Yi Zhu, Fred Van Rijs, Leo C. N. de Vreede, John Gajadharsing, Chang Gao
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Electromagnetics, Antennas and Propagation
abstract: Power amplifier (PA) performance in cellular base stations decreases significantly under antenna impedance variations. Conventional protection techniques, which use circulators or impedance-sensing circuitry, typically increase power consumption and system complexity, with limited scalability in massive-MIMO systems. This paper presents a hardware-efficient approach that estimates load impedance directly from measured PA I/Q data using deep learning, thereby eliminating the use of additional sensing hardware. Grounded in the coupling between load impedance and large-signal transistor dynamics, the method is motivated by the coupling between load impedance and large-signal transistor behavior. Load identification is cast as an inverse electromagnetic problem, where a convolutional neural network learns the nonlinear mapping from performance features of gain, efficiency, and spectral characteristics to the reflection coefficient Γ. Validation on a 100-W Doherty PA through simulation and experimental measurements yielded a normalized mean-square error (NMSE) of -35.4 dB (mean |ΔΓ| ≈ 0.002). Behavioral modeling comparison demonstrates that Γ-aware models maintain robust accuracy under mismatch, improving NMSE by 23.8 dB over conventional 50 Ω-only approaches. This work demonstrates how deep learning techniques can solve complex electromagnetic inverse problems for adaptive RF transmitter architectures, enabling hardware-efficient implementations with compact form factor. A trained inference model and representative test-data examples will be made available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/lab-emi/OpenDPD upon publication</uri>.

title: Doppler Shift Analysis in LEO Satellite Systems: A Ground-Station Perspective
authors: Shuyuan Lu, Guanjun Xu
abstract: Low Earth orbit (LEO) satellite communications are increasingly important for achieving low-latency global coverage. However, severe Doppler effects caused by high-speed movement and high-frequency operation pose significant challenges to system performance. We employ stochastic geometry to develop a statistical model for Doppler frequency shifts in satellite-terrestrial communication systems based on the binomial cluster process. For a ground station (GS), we establish a GS-centric orbital visibility model parameterized by the GS location and observation constraints. Additionally, we derive the cumulative distribution function expressions for the primary and secondary satellite-terrestrial links, as well as for the maximum Doppler shift within satellite clusters. Simulation results corroborate the analysis and reveal the impact of satellite clustering, orbital characteristics, and GS observation conditions on Doppler effects. Comparisons with real data further confirm that the proposed spatial model closely matches the Doppler distribution induced by realistic satellite geometries.

title: Untethering EIT: An Integrated Wireless Tactile Interface for Human&#x2013;Machine Interaction_supp8-3688715.mp4
authors: Shili Liang
abstract: Large-area tactile sensing is a core requirement for intelligent robots. However, traditional high-density sensor arrays face challenges due to complex wiring and high integration difficulty. These issues limit their industrial application. This article proposes a stable and flexible tactile sensing system based on electrical impedance tomography (EIT). At the hardware level, we developed a highly integrated and miniaturized data acquisition (DAQ) system. The developed DAQ system weighs 34 g, complementing a conformable flexible sensor with a thickness of 700 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu$</tex-math> </inline-formula>m. This design improves portability and achieves real-time dynamic mapping at 11.1 FPS. At the algorithmic level, we adopted a U-Net architecture to solve the nonlinear reconstruction challenges of EIT. Within a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$58\times 58\,\,\text{mm}^{2}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> sensing area, the model achieves a two-point spatial resolution limit of 7 mm and a mean multipoint localization error of 1.33 mm under multitouch scenarios. Furthermore, we utilized a CNN-BiLSTM model to decode complex spatiotemporal signals, achieving 96% accuracy for dynamic handwriting recognition and 85% in cross-user tests with ten participants, thereby demonstrating strong generalization capability. Finally, we deployed the system on an industrial robotic arm and a bionic hand. The system demonstrates high-fidelity contact sensing. It can also precisely identify small objects of 5 mm. The proposed EIT system provides a lightweight and scalable solution for industrial tactile perception and intelligent interaction.

title: Congruent kinesthetic and tactile haptic rendering accelerates the learning of highly dynamic virtual sensorimotor tasks_supp1-3698607.pdf
authors: L. ALEXANDRE RATSCHAT

title: Integrating Neuroscientific Knowledge Into Adaptive Hypergraph Learning for Brain Disorder Diagnosis
authors: Mengshen He, Jin Liu, Hulin Kuang, Xu Tian, Hailin Yue, Junjian Li, Xinyu Li, Bin Guo, Jianxin Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Brain disorders are associated with impairments in cognitive and social functioning, placing a substantial burden on families, healthcare systems, and communities. However, accurate diagnosis remains challenging due to complex higher order interactions among brain regions. Existing graph-based methods are largely limited to pairwise connectivity. In addition, these methods often fail to fully exploit well-established neuroscientific prior knowledge, resulting in limited biological interpretability and suboptimal diagnostic performance. Therefore, we propose a prior knowledge-guided adaptive hypergraph learning (PK-AHGL) framework that represents individual-level functional connectivity networks as hypergraphs to capture higher order multiregion interactions while incorporating neuroscientific prior knowledge for brain disorder diagnosis. PK-AHGL consists of three key modules: 1) an adaptive hypergraph convolution module. Unlike traditional hypergraph neural networks that use static hyperedge weights, this module adaptively learns the weights of different hyperedges; 2) a sparse affinity Laplacian module. Key brain regions are extracted from disorder related functional brain networks and used as prior knowledge. Based on these regions, we compute a hyperedge similarity matrix that encourages similar hyperedges to have similar weights; and 3) a proportional margin ranking module. This module further utilizes prior knowledge by guiding hyperedges containing a higher proportion of key brain regions to obtain larger weights. Experiments on autism brain imaging data exchange (ABIDE), Strategic Research Program for the Promotion of Brain Science (SRPBS)-schizophrenia (SCZ), SRPBS-major depressive disorder (MDD), and Alzheimer’s disease neuroimaging initiative (ADNI) show that PK-AHGL achieves accuracies of 75.78%, 82.66%, 74.89%, and 77.22%, respectively, outperforming multiple state-of-the-art methods. These results suggest that PK-AHGL provides an effective auxiliary tool for brain disorder diagnosis and may support community-oriented mental health services.

title: Adap-STWT: Real-Time Traffic Flow Prediction Based on Multi-Scale Graph Adaptive Fusion and Spatio-Temporal Wavelet Transformer
authors: Shijie Cai, Jie Hu, Jia Chen, Wencai Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Accurate traffic prediction is crucial for managing and making decisions in urban traffic systems. Recent studies have shown outstanding performance in traffic prediction by using methods that employ graph adjacency matrices predefined by heuristic rules or learn graph structures through trainable parameters. These studies tend to focus on interactions between nodes based on macroscopic traffic node attributes, overlooking the profound impact of individual behaviors and decisions of microscopic traffic participants on the transportation system. This valuable microscopic information has not been utilized to guide graph structure learning. In this paper, we proposed a spatio-temporal model with the capability of multi-scale adaptive graph structure learning, named Adap-STWT. Initially, an optimal graph structure learning module is constructed for dynamic traffic systems, encompassing multiple scales from microscopic traffic participants to macroscopic node attributes. Subsequently, in conjunction with this optimal graph structure, an optimized spatio-temporal prediction module is designed to facilitate multi-step traffic flow prediction. Ultimately, iterative optimization of the two modules is achieved through an alternating training approach. Notably, owing to the limited availability of large-scale public traffic datasets containing trajectory information, evaluations are conducted on two relatively small public datasets. The results underscore the potential of graph structure learning based on microscopic trajectories for traffic flow prediction. The source code is publicly available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/caisj11/Adap-STWT.git</uri>

title: Density-Aware 4-D Trajectory Planning for Urban Air Traffic With Different QoS Levels
authors: Christian Vitale, Charalambos Menelaou, Panayiotis Kolios, Stelios Timotheou, Christos G. Panayiotou, Georgios Ellinas
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: In the coming years, unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) are expected to find applications in various new mobility concepts. However, current practical trajectory planning solutions for these vehicles in densely populated environments either inadequately address safety concerns, under-utilize available airspace, or rely on overly optimistic assumptions. To address these challenges, we introduce a novel Unmanned Traffic Management System (UTMS), which first partitions the airspace into cubic regions of equal size and employs a reservation mechanism to plan inter-cube paths from source to destination. In this way the proposed UTMS reservation mechanism can accommodate different quality-of-service (QoS) levels and admit UAVs within cubes up to a (tunable) maximum allowed density. Secondly, within each cube, an intra-cube planner robustly determines the trajectory of each UAV, ensuring a guaranteed safety level under realistic system state uncertainty. Extensive simulation results demonstrate that the proposed UTMS effectively manages the trade-off among the tunable UAV density and safety, thereby mitigating the design limitations observed in current state-of-the-art approaches.

title: Tuning Linearity of IGZO Synaptic Transistor with Low-Power by Oxygen Vacancy Engineering for Spiking Neural Networks
authors: Juyoung Yun, Taewon Seo, Yoonyoung Chung
abstract: We propose an N<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> plasma treatment for controlling oxygen vacancies to enhance the linearity of indium-gallium-zinc oxide (IGZO) synaptic transistors. Oxygen vacancies are intentionally generated via Ar bombardment during IGZO sputtering and subsequently passivated by N<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> plasma. This process suppresses persistent photoconductivity, which is a primary limitation in photonic synapses for fast recovery. Our approach yields robust synaptic behaviors, including long-term potentiation and spike-time-dependent plasticity. Crucially, the weight update nonlinearity factor is reduced from 1.55 to 0.41, enabling accurate artificial neural network learning.

title: Prediction-Driven Receding Horizon Scheduling for Joint Multi-Satellite Collaborative Computing and Tracking of Highly Maneuverable Space Targets
authors: Xinyang Wang, Di Zhou, Min Sheng, Zhanwei Wang, Jiandong Li
abstract: Multi-satellite collaborative sensing and computation serves as a key technology for ensuring continuous tracking of highly maneuverable space targets. However, how to utilize limited observation information to achieve precise prediction of maneuver trajectories and thereby guide scheduling decisions in real-time to ensure tracking continuity under resource constraints remains a core challenge faced. To tackle this, we propose a Prediction-Driven Receding Horizon Scheduling framework. This framework leverages real-time trajectory prediction to drive the dynamic evolution of the task Directed Acyclic Graph structure. Building on this, a Task Collaboration and Handover-Aware Genetic Algorithm is designed, achieving joint optimization of collaborative computing decisions and satellite handover selections. The proposed scheme ensures superior tracking stability for highly maneuverable targets while reducing the perception-to-decision latency, thereby enabling future large-scale Low Earth Orbit satellites constellations to achieve more effective continuous monitoring and rapid response to sudden space threats.

title: PGA-Stereo: Robust Stereo Matching with Monocular Semantic Priors and Evidence-Guided Cost Modulation
authors: Di Jia, Fan Zhang, Chen Zhao, Yuqi Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: The core challenges limiting the practical applicability of stereo matching are the inherent matching ambiguity in ill-posed regions, such as reflective and texture-less surfaces, and the limited robustness under abnormal unilateral input conditions. While recent methods leverage semantic priors from monocular Vision Foundation Models (VFMs), their inherent deficiency in geometric constraints, coupled with the fragility of a unimodal architecture, fails to fundamentally address these challenges. This paper presents PGA-Stereo, a novel unified framework for stereo matching that synergizes monocular and binocular depth estimation within a single architecture. In PGA-Stereo, a Prior-Guided Feature Modulator (PGFM) leverages monocular semantic priors as conditional guidance to enhance the discriminability of binocular geometric features at the feature fusion stage. To further enhance the adaptability of cost aggregation, the Adaptive Evidence-Guided Modulation Module (AEGM) introduces an evidence-aware mechanism, dynamically modulating the initial cost volume in a scene-adaptive manner, addressing ill-posed regions with different information biases. Moreover, to counteract the potential loss of information in critical regions during iterative optimization, a Dynamic Fusion Optimization Module (DFOM) adaptively integrates multi-source evidence within a ConvGRU-based iterative update scheme, enabling efficient disparity refinement. Extensive experiments demonstrate that PGA-Stereo is highly effective for ill-posed scenarios and exhibits superior generalization capabilities in real-world settings. At the time of submission, PGA-Stereo was recognized as the leading method among all published approaches on the KITTI 2012 Reflective benchmark. PGA-Stereo establishes a new state-of-the-art (SOTA) on the KITTI 2015 benchmark, achieving a 2.25% D1-fg error rate and outperforming IGEV-Stereo by a 15.73% relative reduction in foreground outliers.

title: Salt Typhoon and the Limits of “Quantum Security”: A Technique-Level Audit_supp1-3688536.csv
authors: Juljan Krause

title: Parallel Secure Pattern Matching with Differential Privacy and Consistency Checking_supp1-3698857.pdf
authors: Jian Xu
abstract: Secure Pattern Matching (SPM) aims to identify all occurrences of a target pattern within a text while preserving data confidentiality and has vital applications in bioinformatics, digital forensics, and cloud-based healthcare. However, existing SPM schemes often suffer from limited scalability on large scale datasets and provide insufficient correctness assurances under outsourced cloud settings. To address these limitations, we propose PSPM, a parallel SPM framework built upon secure multi-party computation (MPC), supporting both single- and multi-pattern queries with comprehensive wildcard functionality. The proposed scheme integrates differentially private Read and Write primitives to obfuscate memory access patterns and enable secure, oblivious data operations. To enhance efficiency, the input text is divided into overlapping sliding windows, each processed in parallel under SIMD-style execution. Each window performs bidirectional scanning to fully leverage parallelism and maximize throughput. For single-pattern queries, local matching is achieved through a border-array–based algorithm, while multi-pattern matching employs an MPC-adapted Aho–Corasick automaton. We design a lightweight cross-consistency checking mechanism that validates outputs via wildcard-augmented variants, thereby enabling detection of inconsistency-inducing single-path computation faults under the standard non-colluding semi-honest setting. Formal security proofs and extensive experimental evaluations on large genomic datasets demonstrate that our framework outperforms prior SPM protocols by up to 1.73× in single-pattern tasks and 10.56× in multi-pattern tasks.

title: Design of gyroidal structures for heat exchanger applications, manufactured with binder jetting using copper
authors: Daniel Mayer, Sandipan Sen, Jörg Fischer-Bühner, Daniel Beckers, Alexander Kauffmann, Stefan Dietrich, Frederik Zanger
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Production Technologies and Systems for E-Mobility (EPTS)
abstract: The thermal management of modern vehicle systems requires the use of high-performance heat exchangers that are also designed to be compact to guarantee the best possible integration into the overall system architecture. In this context, additive manufacturing opens up the possibility of using more complex structures to generate larger exchange surfaces and a 3D fluid flow using gyroid structures. Binder jetting is a process that has great potential for these applications, as its fast build rates and scalability make it suitable to produce medium quantities and easy processing of Cu. However, to enable its broad transfer of this technology to industry and to ensure the applicability of the process, it is necessary to better understand the process in terms of its production limits regarding thin-walled structures.In this work, the design limits of gyroid structures were evaluated with respect to their suitability for depowdering using various test geometries. An evaluation method was presented that makes it possible to relate the depowdering of samples to the respective slices based on CT-images. It was found that the gyroid design parameters have a decisive influence on the depowdering of the manufactured components. It was shown that the depth effect of compressed air during the depowdering step is limited and becomes more difficult with scaled component dimensions. In addition, the depth effect decreases with increasing wall thickness and decreasing unit cell size. However, stretching the unit cell of the gyroid structures along the depowdering direction has proven to be positive.

title: TRACIDUS: AI based Psychological Cybercrime Mapper
authors: Arpith Joshua Rao P, Aakash Suresh, Joel R. B, J Thangakumar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: There is an increasing rate of cybercrime in the current digital world, there is a rise in the use of psychological manipulation approaches in integration with traditional technical methods, making users more vulnerable and out dating traditional security measures in place. This prompts the need to implement a cognitive component to battle cybercrime and better understand the lesser explored factors; the impact of attacker psychology in cyberspace. The use of cognitively engineered threats such as phishing, impersonation, fraud and social engineering takes advantage of certain biases such as trust, authority, urgency and fear to navigate and influence the victim's behavior, to mitigate technical challenges. To oppose these newly founded threats, this paper introduces a new approach TRACIDUS, an AI based psychological cybercrime mapper that excels in detecting, interpreting and visualizing the psychological angle of these attacks and thus applies a human awareness focused approach to reduce human error and educate. This framework makes use of Natural Language Processing (NLP), semantic analysis and weighed cognitive persuasion vectors to identify underlying patterns that implies to deceive users in digital communications. Experimental evaluations on typical text-based cyberattack scenarios show that the framework effectively detects cues of psychological manipulation with high clarity and dependable performance. This approach allows TRACIDUS to be scalable and provide a viable solution for cyber defense that accounts for the psychological approach, aid in further development of human focused cybersecurity systems.

title: Enhancing LMS Privacy and Performance through Computational Intelligence for Secure and Efficient Moodle Administration
authors: Ahmad Shtayyat, Amjad Gawanmeh
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Moodle has found extensive application in higher education, although there has been a growing pressure on both its administration and security, as well as on performance that can survive bursty academic loads. The paper describes a computational intelligence (CI) architecture, which consolidates. (i) multi-source telemetry web attack and misuse detection, (ii) privacy-preserving operational decision learning, and (iii) predictive performance management risk-conscious autoscaling and configuration guardrails. The framework is an amalgamation of signature-based controls with admonitored HTTP attack classification, log-sequence anomaly identification with pre-trained language models, and forecast-driven autoscaling policy informed by meta reinforcement learning. To address the privacy limitations, it fosters on-premise analytics, federated training across institutional silos, and differentiated privacy of aggregate indicators delivered to administrators. Predictive CI-driven scaling improves the 99th-percentile response time of 0.681 s (0.050 s) with 8 hours of Moodle-like workload simulation, eradicates SLA violations (0.81 to 0 percent), and decreases the compute cost (64.00 to 57.35 server-hours). The proposed supervised layer has F1 = 0.817 and AUC = 0.994 on a synthetic dataset of HTTP requests, against a baseline of F1 = 0.358 using the rule only. The findings help to support incremental implementation of CI elements having auditable policies and low operation load.

title: Adopting artificial intelligence applications in legal education- studying the case of faculty of law at University of Petra
authors: Ali Mohamed Aldabbas, Mohammad Saeed Abdallah ALsheyab, Ma’en Abdel-Rahim Juwaihan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: This study addresses the most important areas of use of artificial intelligence applications among students of the Faculty of Law at Petra University with the aim of enhancing legal education and exploring the sources of concern and challenges faced by students when using applications based on artificial intelligence in legal education, and the role of the faculty in guiding students towards integrating artificial intelligence applications into legal education with the aim of facilitating effective integration of artificial intelligence into curricula and preparing for the future legal scene. The study reveals an increase in awareness and reliance of students of the Faculty of Law at Petra University on applications based on artificial intelligence in various areas of legal learning. However, there are still difficulties and challenges in the use of artificial intelligence tools among students, such as insufficient training for students, weak university technological infrastructure, linguistic challenges especially the scarcity of Arabic legal applications based on artificial intelligence, and ethical concerns such as reliability, bias, and "hallucination" in artificial intelligence outputs. To cope with these issues, the study confirms the need for institutional investment in advanced digital infrastructure, training programs, and improved AI-focused curricula.

title: Machine Learning Operated Approaches for Advancing Efficiency of $\text{FASnI}_{3}$ Solar Cell
authors: Raushan Kumar, Alok Priyadarshi, Asisa Kumar Panigrahy, Alisha Priya
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: The <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{FASnI}_{3} \text{PV}$</tex> cells have gained considerable interest due to their high efficiency, flexibility, and low-cost fabrication. Their direct bandgap and strong optical absorption <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\sim 10^{5} \text{cm}^{-1}\right)$</tex> make them highly suitable for photovoltaic applications. In this study, AFORS-HET simulations are employed to systematically analyze CeZnO as the buffer layer and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Cr}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}$</tex> as the hole transport layer (HTL) to optimize the performance of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{FASnI}_{3}$</tex>-based PV cells. The proposed device structure <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Al} / \text{ITO} / \text{ZnO} / \text{CeZnO} / \text{FASnI}_{3} / \text{Au}$</tex> is designed to minimize recombination losses. Furthermore, by optimizing the FASnI3 layer thickness, doping concentration, and device temperature, an excellent performance is achieved with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{V}_{\mathbf{o c}}= 1278 \text{mV}, \mathrm{J}_{\text{sc}}=23.45 ~\text{mA} / \text{cm}^{2}, \text{FF}=79.70 \%$</tex>, and overall efficiency <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=23.26 \%$</tex> by simulation and ad predicted efficiency 24.12 % by using machine learning concept, which bests that of conventional solar cell devices.

title: Multi-Modal Lightweight Deep Learning Framework for Robust In-Field Plant/Crop Disease Detection with Explainability (XAI)
authors: Garima Srivastava, Pratishtha Saxena, Bhupendra Kumar, Tanu, Monu Gupta, Ghanshyam Yadav
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: Timely and precise identification of Plant/Crop diseases continues to be a significant problem in the agricultural sector, especially in developing countries. While there have been improvements in Deep Learning including convolutional neural networks (CNNs), the performance of many DL models still deteriorate in the-field/on-site due to the limited diversity of datasets, the lack of Explainability (Proper XAI Framework) and computational resource restrictions. In this paper, we present a multi-modal Deep Learning Framework for in-field Plant/Crop Disease Detection that is lightweight. Using a customized MobileNetV3 model with integrated environmental metadata (e.g. temperature, humidity, Soil Condition). To solve the problem of fine-grained subtype classification for Plant/Crop Disease images with scarce resources, this paper proposes a multi-stage information fusion based lightweight model enhanced by an attention mechanism model. Built on MobileNetV3, this paper introduces a multi-stage fusion method to extract the "theme" contained-rich image information in Plant/Crop Disease images. Furthermore, during the first stage of feature extraction, we present the Selective Kernel (SK) Attention Mechanism, and in the last two steps, we embed the Squeeze-Excitation Coordinate Attention (SCA) Mechanism to obtain deeper semantic and low-level feature extraction. The final feature representations used for sub-type classification are selected from the map weights derived at each stage. Moreover, we integrate Grad-CAM-based visual explanations to enhance model comprehensibility and agriculturalist trust. In addition, we incorporate Grad-CAM-based visual explanations to make the model more interpretable to farmers. It enables identifying relevant regions of an input image that contribute to the CNN’s classification decision. Extensive experiments across three agro-climatic zones and multiple Plants/crops types (Wheat, Potato, Tomato, Rice, Maize etc.) demonstrate its ultra-modern performance, with over 96% average accuracy in varied field conditions and real-time inference capabilities (<200ms). Our Proposed MobileNetV3 model (Selective Kernel (SKA) + Squeeze-Excitation Coordinate Attention (SCA) Mechanism) achieves high classification accuracy while remaining deployable on Smart Phones/Edge Devices. We assessed and contrasted their performances on the basis of various performance evaluation metrics including accuracy, precision, recall, f1-score and confusion matrix. This work represents a significant step toward Practical, Accessible and Explainable AI Solution in Smart & Precision agriculture.

title: First Experimental Demonstration of Metasurface-Enhanced Detection of Small Scatterers Using an Open-Ended Coaxial Probe
authors: Eleonora Razzicchia, Milad Mokhtari, Rotem Gal-Katzir, Yunxiao Zhang, Yarden Mazor, Emily Porter
publish: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)
abstract: Metasurfaces (MTSs) can improve electromagnetic coupling at material interfaces and have strong potential for enhancing microwave dielectric sensing. This work presents the first experimental demonstration of an MTS matching layer used with an open-ended coaxial probe (OECP) to improve detection of small embedded scatterers. A PEC-grid MTS, designed as an impedance-matching layer using a transmission-line analytical model, was fabricated and tested on custom samples consisting of a homogeneous background medium and a medium containing a small inclusion. Reflection measurements from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.5-20 \text{GHz}$</tex> show that the MTS improves field penetration and greatly increases the variations observed in the measurements between the two samples, making the embedded scatterer much more detectable. These preliminary proof-of-concept results indicate that MTS-based matching layers can enhance OECP sensitivity and provide a low-cost, practical solution for improving dielectric characterization in materials and biological tissues.

title: A Frequency-Enhanced Multi-Scale Decomposition Framework with Hierarchical Channel Aggregation-Redistribution for Ultra-Short-Term Wind Power Forecasting
authors: Yan Chen, Jin Su, Xiaochun Hu, Rongbin Liu, Yuhan Yang, Rongxing Jiang
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: Ultra-short-term Wind Power Forecasting (WPF) is critical for real-time power grid scheduling and renewable energy accommodation. However, existing methods still face two core challenges: first, multiple periodic patterns in wind power sequences are mutually coupled; second, the cross-variable channel dependencies exhibit heterogeneous structures. To address these issues, we propose FreHAC-Mixer, a novel deep learning framework that integrates adaptive frequency-domain decomposition with component-specific hierarchical channel interaction. Extensive experiments on two real-world wind power datasets show that FreHAC-Mixer outperforms eight state-of-the-art baseline models, reducing MAE and MSE by up to 6.7% and 3.9% for ultra-short-term forecasting.

title: Comparative study of textual feature extraction methods and machine learning models for the classification of industrial accidents
authors: Kawtar Benderouach, Idriss Bennis, Khalifa Mansouri
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Workplace accidents in the energy sector are a significant issue in terms of industrial safety, given their human and financial implications. Automated analysis of accident reports could be very useful for understanding and preventing these accidents. In this study, machine learning models are compared using these two accident types’ binary classifications according to their text descriptions. This study evaluates several techniques that may be used to extract textual features, such as Bag of Words (BOW), TF-IDF, Word2Vec, BERT, and TF-IDF + BERT. Several classification models used in this research are: logistic regression, SVM, decision tree, random forest, and XGBoost. To optimize the model’s accuracy, grid search optimization is done, while to deal with class imbalance, SMOTE technique is employed. The experimental results reveal an important conclusion: despite the recognized effectiveness of contextual encodings such as BERT, the best performance in terms of accuracy, precision, recall and F1 score is achieved by using the traditional BOW representation combined with SMOTE and GridSearch. This demonstrates that simpler representations combined with appropriate optimization strategies can outperform more complex language models for analyzing the severity of workplace accidents and managing industrial risks.

title: Study and Analysis of Approaches to Optimizing Coordination Between Processes in Industry 4.0
authors: Manal Tahri, Bachir Benhala, Mohamed El Khaili
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Industry 4.0 reflects a paradigm change driven by the extensive deployment of Cyber Physical Systems (CPS). The fourth revolution brings about changes in paradigms. As the world has become globalized, companies find themselves in a volatile business environment that requires coordination of processes as not a choice, but necessity. Our paper addresses the inherent complexity of managing dispersed processes, which constitutes a major challenge. The synchronization between production, logistics, and maintenance (PLM) often suffers from informational silos. In a mostly multi-site environment, the lack of seamless coordination leads to prolonged downtime and a degradation of service rates. Our research provides syntheses of proposed models capable of synchronizing these functions in real-time to minimize overall costs under resource constraints.

title: Crossmodal-Bart: a Unified Vision-Language Model for Video Summarization
authors: Gautham S N, Satish K, Akshai A, Santhi S, Kalaiselvi S
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: The rapid growth of online videos, crafting short and meaningful summaries is very important for easy understanding, quick viewing, and for saving storage space. Most methods developed so far have their focus either only on visual features or generate summaries directly from captions, yielding simplistic or low-context outputs. This work proposes CrossModal-BART, a video summarization framework where video visual understanding and text generation are combined into one seamless workflow. It uses BLIP to extract visual features from a collection of selected video frames, which are then converted into a few short captions. The resulting captions are ordered to keep the same order as in the video. Finally, BART takes this sequence of captions as input and creates a clear and readable summary. The proposed framework truly understands not only what is present in the video but also the context and meaning of the video. Experimental results revealed that CrossModal-BART generates more accurate, less repetitive and easier-tounderstand summaries than the traditional approaches. Comprehensive experiments on the benchmark dataset demonstrates that CrossModal-BART achieves significant improvements over state-of-the-art baselines: 61% ROUGE-1 score (vs. 52% for caption-only methods), 86% BERTScore (vs. 71%), and superior human evaluation ratings for coherence and informativeness. Applications of this system can be extended to educational videos, surveillance footages, media content, and a lot of other real-world applications.

title: Real-Time Unified Spam Detection System for Email and SMS
authors: Badal Singh, Rishab Mishra, Krishnakant Nagina, Syed Rameez Ali, Rajesh Tiwari, Nikhat Raza Khan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: A machine learning framework for identifying spam in emails and SMS is presented in the study. TF-IDF and Bag-of-Words embeddings are used after preprocessing techniques like tokenization, lemmatization, and stop-word removal. An 80–20 split was used to train ten classifiers, including Naïve Bayes variants, and they were assessed using ROC-AUC, accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and Z-score. With 99.20% accuracy and 0.98 ROC-AUC, ensemble models demonstrated strong reliability and outperformed other models. According to linguistic analysis, spam messages usually contain more words and characters and have intricate structures. The system demonstrated great potential for cybersecurity applications and was implemented via a graphical user interface (GUI) for voice-based and real-time spam detection

title: Multi-Objective Bayesian Optimization of XGBoost for Imbalanced Molecular Classification Using RDKit Descriptors
authors: Ahmad Al Athamneh, Mahmoud E. Farfoura, Yazan S Batarseh, Saad Alnassan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Lack of balance between the molecular classification poses a serious obstacle in cheminformatics especially where it is imperative to detect minority-classes. This paper will present a machine learning architecture that combines an XGBoost gradient soaring alongside multi-objective bayesian optimization to boost predictive capability in the case of class imbalance. The joint optimization between macro-F1 and ROC-AUC was done using Optuna tree-structured Parzen Estimator using 196 RDKit molecular descriptors based on the 477 training samples (class ratio 3.04:1) and SMOTE was utilized in a leak-free stratified cross-validation procedure. The chosen Pareto-optimal setting scored 0.842, 0.770, 0.750, 0.888, 0.733 on an independent test set of 120 compounds with accuracy, macro-F1, balanced accuracy, ROC-AUC and PR-AUC respectively. The confusion matrix analysis shows that it possesses high majority-class detection and moderate sensitivity to minority-classes. The analysis of the importance of features identifies nitrogen functional groups in aromatic features as important discriminators. These findings show that imbalance-aware resampling with multi-objective optimization offers a sound and repeatable framework of predicting DIA activities.

title: Development of a Non-invasive Blood Glucose Wristwatch System Based on PPG Signal
authors: Yutong Liu, Guangyu Zhang, Hongyu Zhu, Jian Liu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: Continuous and non-invasive blood glucose monitoring is essential for the effective management of diabetes and the prevention of chronic complications. This paper presents a wearable wristwatch system for non-invasive blood glucose monitoring based on multi-wavelength photoplethysmography (PPG) signals. The hardware architecture integrates a multiwavelength optical acquisition module $(540 \mathrm{~nm} / 660 \mathrm{~nm} / 940 \mathrm{~nm})$, a contact pressure sensing module to ensure signal fidelity, and a low-power microcontroller unit. A hierarchical network architecture was implemented, comprising a data acquisition layer, an edge access layer via a smartphone application, and a cloud computing layer for sophisticated signal analysis. To ensure high prediction accuracy, a robust preprocessing pipeline was developed, utilizing a refined valley-detection algorithm for cycle segmentation and Principal Component Analysis (PCA) to extract 11 key morphological features. The glucose regression model was established using Support Vector Regression (SVR) to map the nonlinear relationship between PPG features and glycemic levels. In vivo experiments demonstrate that the model effectively tracks blood glucose trends. This study provides a promising technical framework for mobile, non-invasive, and continuous glycemic monitoring.

title: Performance Enhancement of Classic and Differential P & O MPPT Used in PV System
authors: Jayanta Kumar Sahu, Dipika Debasmita Panda, Chittaranjan Swain, Sampad Kumar Pradhan, Mrutyunjay Senapati, Jyoti Prasad Patra
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: The response power of the PV system is affected by temperature, load impedance, and PV cell irradiation. Under certain climatic conditions, the system must be able to function close to its maximum power point in order to excerpt the maximum power from a PV cell. The response voltage or current of the PV system is adjusted for a specific load, irradiation level, and cell temperature using Maximum Power Point Tracking. But the load does not receive all of the real electricity generated by the PV array. As a result, it's crucial for obtaining the most electricity from the PV array. The key goal of this work is to examine MPPT techniques used in standalone PV and analyze Classic and Differential Perturb. The results will be compared based on accuracy or stability and Tracking efficiency. This approach, called the maximum power point methodology, is a simple and affordable way to monitor the solar PV array's maximum power as the weather changes. It offers a cost-effective maximum power point strategy that works with all PV systems. The system operated more effectively when monitoring the PV panel's maximum power.

title: An AI-Driven Decision Support Framework for Change Order Management in Construction Projects: A Systematic Bibliometric Review
authors: Zaid Momani, Hanasha Ismail, Eeydzah Aminudin, Che Khairil Izam Che Ibrahim, Siti Hamidah Abdull Rahman, Hamidah Mohd Saman
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Change orders—formal modifications to construction contracts that alter scope, cost, or schedule—represent persistent sources of delay, cost escalation, and dispute risk. This paper presents a bibliometric and thematic review examining how Artificial Intelligence (AI) is being integrated into decision support for change order management. Analysis of 122 Scopus-indexed publications (2019–2026) reveals that while 53% of research focuses on predictive analytics, only 7% addresses structured decision support, and governance integration—critical because change orders are legally binding contract modifications requiring formal authorisation—is notably absent from the literature. Building on this evidence, we propose a four-layer conceptual framework integrating established predictive capabilities with novel contributions: a Decision Translation Layer converting predictions into actionable recommendations, and a Governance Integration Layer ensuring contractual compliance through approval routing, stakeholder coordination, and audit mechanisms. The governance layer addresses a fundamental gap: AI recommendations cannot execute without mechanisms ensuring proper authorisation and documentation. This framework bridges the research-practice disconnect, advancing explainable, governance-aligned AI support for change order management in construction.

title: An Accurate Method for 3D Model Classification using the Explainable Boosting Classifier
authors: Fatima Rafii Zakani, Mohcine Bouksim, Khadija Arhid, Taoufiq Gadi, Mohamed Aboulfatah
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Accurate and efficient 3D model classification is still a major challenge in computer vision and other fields. This paper presents a new method which is expected to achieve high classification performance. The proposed approach is organised in two major phases. First, we extract a discriminative feature vector by computing three powerful geometric measures, i.e., Shape Diameter Function (SDF), dihedral angles, and the Shape Index, and fuse them into a unified representation for each 3D object. Second, this composite feature vector is used to train an Explainable Boosting Classifier (EBC), a powerful ensemble learning model, to perform the classification task. The effectiveness of the proposed method was thoroughly tested on the Princeton Benchmark dataset. Experimental results show that our proposed method can get a better classification accuracy than several leading state-of-the-art methods.

title: A Reward-Driven Blockchain-Based Crowdsourcing Framework for Ecological Data Collection
authors: Yara Kouttane, Raifa Akkaoui
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Morocco faces critical gaps in ecological data collection necessary for sustainable development and environmental management. At present, limited ecological data exists in silos managed by various national and regional governmental agencies, which hinders environmental data democracy and regular ecological monitoring. Therefore, in order to facilitate reliable and transparent ecological data acquisition by citizens, nongovernmental organizations, and academic institutions, we propose a reward-driven blockchain-based crowdsourcing platform, namely EcoChain, that leverages smart contracts and the InterPlanetary File System for semi-automated decision-making and fault-tolerance. However, as the platform encompasses various entities that might not necessarily be trusted, we design a reward-driven voting mechanism that enables selected validators to verify the reliability of each ecological data submitted to the system before being accepted for inclusion. Moreover, we propose a vector-commitment scheme based on Merkle trees to guarantee the anonymity of the randomly assigned validators during the validation phase for each ecological submission, while ensuring on-chain automated verification during the voting phase. Furthermore, in order to evaluate the performance of the system and analyse its security features, we implement a prototype utilizing an Ethereum test network.

title: A Wideband Parasitic Patch Antenna in a Rigid-Flex Thin Substrate for on-Body Communication
authors: Alessandro Ficarra, Emanuele Cardillo
publish: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)
abstract: This paper presents a patch antenna implemented on a multilayer rigid-flex substrate for communication in wearable applications. The antenna can be connected to the rest of the circuit through a flexible transmission line, which is able to conform to the curvatures of the human body or wearable devices such as smartwatches, rings, heart-rate bands, and wristbands. The <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$57-64 \text{GHz}$</tex> band is an unlicensed spectrum, enabling high-speed short-range on-body communication for wearable devices. The proposed antenna exhibits a wideband of 12 GHz and a flat gain of 6 dBi, thereby reducing the bit error rate and improving signal integrity.

title: Fault Diagnosis in Analog Circuits Using Quadratic Discriminant Analysis Classifier
authors: Shaktikanta Jena, Karmila Soren, Tapas Kumar Patra
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: Analog circuitry continues to play a crucial role in modern electronic systems; however, its long-standing reliability has been increasingly challenged by soft and parametric defects that degrade operational integrity. This study presents a machine learning-based diagnostic architecture designed to detect and classify faults in analog integrated circuits (AICs). The proposed method exploits frequency-domain features extracted from the real and imaginary components of the output voltage and supply current responses, which offer strong discriminative capability. These features are systematically generated using frequency-domain Monte Carlo simulations. Several classification techniques, including Support Vector Machine (SVM), k-Nearest Neighbors (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$k$</tex>-NN), Random Forest (RF), and Quadratic Discriminant Analysis (QDA), were evaluated using standard performance metrics such as precision, recall, and overall accuracy. Experimental results demonstrate that QDA delivers superior performance, achieving an average classification accuracy of 99.8%, and outperforming conventional neuro-fuzzy and Artificial Neural Network (ANN) approaches. Experimental validation was conducted using three benchmark circuits: a Sallen-Key band-pass filter, a four-op-amp high-pass filter, and a leapfrog filter. All circuits were modeled in SPICE, with component variations of up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 50 \%$</tex> introduced to emulate realistic parametric deviations.

title: Decoding the “Class Attraction” Effect in Satellite Imagery: A Quantitative Explainable AI Approach
authors: Jbrane Abrouch, Redouan Korchiyne, Rabiaa Cheikh Maoulainine, Rachid Dahmani, Mustapha Tahiri Alaoui
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: While deep learning architectures have achieved remarkable success in Land Use and Land Cover (LULC) classification from high-resolution satellite imagery, their inherent “black-box” nature limits their reliability in critical Earth observation applications. Recent literature has identified the “class attraction” effect-a phenomenon where models consistently misclassify spectrally overlapping classes, such as Agriculture and Barren Land. However, traditional evaluation metrics, such as the mean Intersection over Union (mIoU), fail to elucidate the spatial reasoning behind these decision failures. In this paper, we introduce a quantitative Explainable AI (XAI) framework to decode this phenomenon. Using Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM), we extract the spatial attention of four distinct Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architectures: U-Net, SegNet, PSPNet, and DeepLabV3+, evaluated on the DeepGlobe dataset. To transcend subjective visual inspection, we propose two novel spatial explainability metrics: Attention-Intersection over Union (A-IoU) and the Energy Ratio. Our quantitative results demonstrate that standard encoderdecoder models like U-Net exhibit severe attention scattering, capturing only 45.0% of their activation energy within the target class boundaries (A-IoU of 0.25). In contrast, DeepLabV3+ demonstrates highly focused spatial reasoning, directing 82.0% of its energy to the correct topological features (A-IoU of 0.68). These findings prove mathematically that multi-scale contextual aggregation, such as Atrous Spatial Pyramid Pooling (ASPP), is essential not only for accuracy but for mitigating the class attraction effect by anchoring the model’s focus on global geometric consistency rather than deceptive local spectral intensities.

title: Spatially Coherent Extension of MQDM for Hourly Future Climate Time Series
authors: B.Mounica, Pasham Rithwika Reddy, Mohammad Rokhayya Reshma, Kondapuram Shivaprasad Reddy, Surkanti Sandeep Kumar Reddy
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Hourly climate data is important in power system planning and renewable energy applications. Monthly Quantile Delta Mapping (MQDM) is generally used to convert daily climate data into hourly values while keeping the historical trends. Even though it performs well, applying it separately for different locations can sometimes affect the spatial relationship between regions. In this work, an attempt is made to improve MQDM by including spatial coherence using high-resolution historical data. The correction is first carried out at each grid point, and later a reordering step is applied to maintain the connection between locations. For this, the Schaake Shuffle method is used so that both statistical and spatial characteristics are not lost. From the results, it is observed that regional consistency improves after applying spatial processing, while the local accuracy remains almost unchanged. The final hourly data obtained can be useful for power system studies and climate-related analysis at a larger scale.

title: Artificial Intelligence to Detect Bulging of Earth Retaining Structures in Transport Systems
authors: Md Zahidul Islam, Chin Jian Leo, Ju Jia Zou, Samanthika Liyanapathirana, Pan Hu, Bo Xiao, Stanley Yuen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multi-scale Artificial Intelligence (MAI)
abstract: Abnormal bulging in earth-retaining structures (ERS) of road and rail transport systems potentially poses safety risks and requires a timely, accurate assessment to prevent structural failures. Traditional inspection methods are often labor-intensive, subjective, and difficult to scale. This study investigates the feasibility of using artificial intelligence (AI) techniques to automate the detection of bulging in ERS, an area of research that remains relatively underexplored. The latest version of the object detection technique YOLOv11s has been improved to identify bulging in visual images captured using a vehicle-mounted data acquisition system. Preliminary results demonstrate high accuracy in detecting anomalous bulging in ERS. The findings highlight the potential use of AI-assisted inspection systems to support efficient, scalable, and consistent structural health monitoring of ERS.

title: AI Inbound Sales Agent
authors: Kamalesh P, Tharun R, Harish Raj A, K Kowsalya
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: The given paper features a scalable AI inbound sales agent that is meant to automate the process of lead engagement, qualification, and follow-up based on the conversational intelligence and workflow automation. Conventional sales systems are characterized by poor scalability, processing time, and inconsistent handling of leads because of manual process. The suggested system will combine a multi-layered design with a React-based dashboard, n8n workflow orchestration, LinkedIn-based data enrichment, and AI-assisted voice interaction via Retell API, in order to solve these issues. The system uses real-time intent detection, sentiment analysis, and use of transcript in making decisions through the application of natural language processing. High-volume scenarios tested experimentally showed substantial gains with a 10 times higher call capacity, 95 percent cost-of-operation reduction, and a more than 40 percent rise in the conversion rates. Reliability of the system is statistically supported, with 99.5% uptime and minimal errors, at less than 1.3. Relative benchmarking with traditional and current AI-based systems emphasizes a high level of efficiency and consistency. The proposed solution will offer a highly reliable, scalable and enterprise solution to automated sales processes as well as guarantee compliance, security, and flexibility throughout various channels of communication.

title: Self-diagnosis Method for Sensor Array Faults in Intelligent Drilling Rigs
authors: Xin Xu, Jinqiu Hu, Shengli Chu, Kun Tian, Yiyue Chen, Jingzhi Cui
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: This paper proposes a lightweight self-diagnosis method for drilling-rig sensor arrays based on the isolation forest algorithm. The approach combines SPC preliminary screening with multi-feature anomaly scoring to detect soft faults such as data drift and freezing. A Drillbench simulation with ten pressure sensors verifies that the method accurately identifies linear-drift faults and distinguishes sensor anomalies from mud-density-induced pressure changes. The results demonstrate improved sensitivity and robustness compared with traditional SPC.

title: A Multi-Channel Vibrating-Wire Collector with CNN-Based Diagnosis and Conditional Recovery
authors: Haoxuan Li, Ying Peng, Sihan Wang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: Vibrating-wire sensors encode the measured quantity as resonance frequency, which is suitable for long-term monitoring but can lead field readers to accept countable yet unreliable waveforms under weak excitation, cable attenuation, contact degradation, electromagnetic interference, or noise. This paper presents a shared four-channel collector that combines adaptive sweep excitation, delayed pickup counting, state-aware diagnosis, and conditional short-time Fourier transform (STFT) recovery. A compact one-dimensional convolutional neural network (1DCNN) classifies each pickup window into six states and maps them to Accept, Repair, or Reject actions before the frequency is reported. Experiments show that repeated four-channel scans meet the 8 s target, the maximum raw frequency deviation from a commercial reference reader is 0.6 Hz, the CNN test accuracy is 96.89%, and decision replay reduces invalid acceptance to 0.5%. In the retained weak-signal case, the recovered frequency is 1378.2 Hz, close to the nominal 1378.4 Hz component.

title: Serverless News Aggregation Through Semantic Event Clustering and Multi-Feed Recommendation
authors: Deepika Paliwal, Pulkit Goyal, Tanishka Goel, Chaitali Bhowmik
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: Digital news consumption faces critical challenges including scattered sources, redundant content, misleading headlines, and sophisticated anti-scraping barriers. This paper introduces HierInfo, a news aggregation platform that addresses these issues through intelligent processing and personalized delivery. The system separates frontend interactions from a Python-based asynchronous Natural Language Processing (NLP) pipeline that generates vector embeddings, validates headline-content alignment, identifies unique events via Hierarchical Density Based Spatial Clustering of Applications with Noise (HDBSCAN) algorithm, and enriches articles through summarization and classification. Content is delivered through four specialized feeds—Trending, Critical, Personalized Daily Digest, and Explore—serving distinct user needs. Experimental evaluations demonstrate perfect deduplication accuracy (F1 = 1.0) with 13× speedup over Term Frequency–Inverse Document Frequency (TF-IDF) baselines and superior semantic search relevance (NDCG@10 = 0.95). The complete pipeline processes approximately 30 articles per hour on consumer hardware, demonstrating that serverless architectures can effectively balance semantic quality with computational efficiency for scalable news aggregation.

title: A Machine Learning-Based Calibration Method for a Wind-Speed Measurement Unit in Power-Grid Meteorological Devices
authors: Liming Wang, Qibin Zhou, Tengfei Li, Feng Zeng, Li Kang, Lei Zhao, Zhibo Ta
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG)
abstract: The wind-speed measurement unit of power-grid meteorological devices plays an important role in power system operation monitoring and extreme-weather risk assessment. However, due to structural flow disturbance and varying operating conditions, the raw wind-speed measurements often exhibit systematic bias, making high-accuracy and deployment-ready calibration modeling necessary. This paper proposes a wind-speed calibration method based on a "physics baseline and residual learning". A power-law model is first adopted as the physics baseline to anchor the overall trend. Then, nonlinear bias is compensated via residual learning, where the Reynolds number is introduced as a representative physical feature to characterize operating-condition differences. An ensemble strategy is further employed to improve prediction stability. Comparative experiments are conducted using wind-tunnel calibration data. Under five repeated random 8:2 train/test splits, the proposed method achieves a test-set "RMSE of 0.1220 m/s (std 0.0115 m/s)" and "MAE of 0.0761 m/s (std 0.0021 m/s)", significantly outperforming the physics-only power-law baseline. Moreover, the empirical CDF of absolute error shows "P90 = 0.1459 m/s" and "P95 = 0.1868 m/s", confirming stable accuracy for the majority of samples. Additional analyses under different wind-speed ranges and repeated random splits further verify the robustness of the proposed method.The results demonstrate that the proposed approach effectively compensates nonlinear bias while preserving physically consistent trends, providing a practical solution for engineering deployment of wind-speed calibration in power-grid meteorological devices.

title: Decoy-Enhanced Framework for Threat Protection in Intellectual Property
authors: B. M Brinda, Hemashree D, Kavipriya V, Kiruthiga D, Roshini R
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: These days, research data, algorithms, and private design papers are examples of intellectual property, which is regarded as one of the most significant digital assets for businesses. However, it has been noted that once an attacker gains access, conventional security measures like encryption and access control are ineffective, particularly for insider attacks. Additionally, to find sensitive information, today's attackers employ a variety of automated document analysis techniques, such as topic modelling and clustering. This study suggests a security framework called DEFT-IP, which is based on the application of anomaly detection in conjunction with cyber deception techniques, to address such problems. To find unusual activity in the document repository, the system analyses user access behaviour using a Variational Autoencoder (VAE). The technology will instantly launch decoy papers that mimic real intellectual property with purposefully changed content as soon as aberrant behaviour is identified. By confusing the semantic structure while preserving the papers' realism, these changes will deceive the analysis system. As a result, the likelihood of intellectual property theft will be decreased since the attackers will be tricked into obtaining false information. The suggested approach successfully detects abnormal behaviour while decreasing the automated document mining system's accuracy, according to experimental data. A proactive defence mechanism for intellectual property protection is provided by the DEFT-IP system.

title: BiPAM: Bidirectional Parallel Attention-Mamba for Efficient Anomaly Detection in Industrial Control Systems
authors: Mohammad Alhadidi, Mouhammd Alkasassbeh
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Industrial Control Systems (ICS) face escalating cyber threats, creating urgent demand for efficient anomaly detection capable of handling high-dimensional multivariate time series with severe class imbalance. We propose BiPAM (Bidirectional Parallel Attention-Mamba), a lightweight framework that fuses Selective State Space Models with self-attention through adaptive gating. The architecture employs parallel pathways: bidirectional Mamba blocks with learnable forget gates capture long-range temporal dependencies in both directions, while multi-head self-attention models local patterns and inter-sensor relationships. A learned gating mechanism adaptively combines both pathways based on input characteristics, and a dual-pathway decoder balances point-wise and sequential reconstruction. For anomaly identification, we combine association discrepancy scoring with reconstruction error to detect both reconstruction-based and temporal association anomalies. Evaluated on the 127-sensor WADI water distribution benchmark, BiPAM achieves 96.73% F1-score with 95.45% precision and 98.03% recall, improving over TFCL by 4.29% and outperforming nine baseline methods across autoencoder, Transformer, and frequency-domain paradigms. With only 289K parameters, 0.019ms per-sample latency, and 53,223 samples/sec throughput, BiPAM enables real-time edge deployment in resource-constrained industrial environments.

title: Toward a Zero Trust Security Framework for Smart Learning Environments: Integrating Intrusion Detection Systems to Mitigate Adversarial Machine Learning Threats in AI-Driven Education
authors: Abdissamad Ayoubi, Loubna Laaouina, Adil Jeghal, Hamid Tairi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Smart learning environments represent a major step toward making education more efficient and flexible. Through the transformative impact of artificial intelligence and big data, these environments have proven their ability to personalize the learning experience, enhance engagement, customize content and feedback, provide tailored recommendations, build learning paths, automated the assessment process, feedback generation, and improve decision-making in real time. These opportunities make it an indispensable choice in the field of education. On the other hand, the use of artificial intelligence in this environment has raised many security challenges that require constant attention to enhance cybersecurity and ensure an educational environment that is not only innovative but also secured. The security landscape in smart learning environments has become more complex due to their intricate architecture, which combines embedded artificial intelligence algorithms, cloud computing technologies, the Internet of Things, and various digital learning applications. This integration and complexity have led to an increase in data volume, while the scope of threats has expanded with the emergence of malicious cyberattacks targeting AI models through data manipulation or the exploitation of vulnerabilities. Furthermore, the proliferation of users and devices, along with the integration of educational systems with external services, further complicates the security landscape, broadens the scope of attacks, and increases the likelihood of security risks. This necessitates the adoption of integrated security strategies capable of adapting to the evolving nature of digital threats. This article explores the threats facing smart learning environments, focusing on those targeting machine learning and the best practices that should be adopted to protect smart learning environments from sophisticated cyber threats.

title: Large-Signal Output Tracking and Stabilization of a DC–DC Boost Using Improved Takagi–Sugeno Modeling and Gain-Scheduled LQR Control
authors: Othmane Maakoul, Abdellah Boulal
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This paper addresses the problem of large-signal output tracking and stabilization of DC-DC boost converters using a Takagi-Sugeno (TS) fuzzy control framework. While core techniques such as TS modeling, Parallel Distributed Compensation (PDC), Linear Quadratic Regulator (LQR), and Linear Matrix Inequalities (LMIs) are well established in the literature, their effective integration for large-signal tracking with reduced computational complexity remains a relevant challenge. To this end, an improved control strategy is proposed based on a reduced-rule TS fuzzy model, which preserves accuracy while lowering implementation complexity. An augmented-state formulation with integral action is introduced to ensure zero steady-state error. Furthermore, a gain-scheduled LQR controller is developed within the PDC framework, where controller gains are adapted online according to fuzzy membership functions.The stability of the closed-loop system is guaranteed using LMI-based conditions. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed integrated approach improves tracking performance and robustness under large-signal variations compared to conventional methods.

title: AI-Driven Image Quality Enhancement for Low-Dose CT
authors: Sekar K, Dheepa T, Bavishya K, Kavitha T, Kavya Sudha S, Malaya Dutta Borah
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: The low-dose computed tomography (LDCT) is known to reduce the risk of radiation, but also it is associated with a lot of noise that may affect the quality of diagnoses. This study discusses a deep learning based denoising method that is based on the Denoising Convolutional neural network (DnCNN), which is trained to learn and eliminate residual noise instead of the reconstruction of the complete image. This residual-learning approach is simpler and less expensive to compute than encoder-decoder-based architecture, including U-Net, which is appealing to clinical deployment. The Kaggle dataset-2020 is used in the experiments, with soft-kernel LDCT scans being matched with the corresponding normal-dose references. Following preprocessing stages such as intensity normalization, patch extraction and dataset partition, the network is trained to produce a noise component and remove it as well as the input images. Quantitative analysis using PSNR and SSIM demonstrates that DnCNN significantly decreases noises and retains fine anatomical details, which is a strong and effective choice in denoising LDCT in most medical imaging practices.

title: From Smart Green Factories to Smart Sustainable Cities: A Best Worst Method Based Decision-Making Framework for Green IoT in Industry 4.0
authors: Hayat El Hailouch, Zouhair Marmoucha, Hasna Nhaila, Mohamed El Khaili, Aziz Soulhi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: The objective of this article is to focus on ecological and sustainable cities, as well as the interactions between them, based on recent research literature related to this topic. We will also highlight the role of green industry in the successful creation of sustainable and ecological cities. Indeed, industrial parks have direct and indirect impacts on the cities in which they are located, and therefore using eco-parks has become essential for so-called green and sustainable cities. Therefore, to have an Industry 4.0 that consumes less energy and has a more acceptable carbon footprint, it is important to adopt innovative technologies such as Green IoT and Green AI. In this work, we will focus on the five principles of Green IoT for energy reduction as well as the key strategies to reduce energy consumption in IoT networks. Finally, we will describe the multi-criteria selection methods used to choose the most energyefficient IoT technology, including a simulation as an example using the AHP (Analytic Hierarchy Process) method, which prioritizes criteria such as energy consumption, cost, and lifespan. We will also discuss the TOPSIS (Technique for Order Preference by Similarity to Ideal Solution) method, which ranks solutions according to their proximity to an ideal green solution, as well as the BWM (Best Worst Method), which identifies the best and worst criteria and assigns weights to choose the most eco-efficient solution.

title: From Industry 5.0 to Maintenance 5.0: A Roadmap for the Evolution of Maintenance Practices and the Operator 5.0 Competency Framework
authors: Soukaina Sadiki, Driss Amegouz, Said Boutahari
