abstract: The proliferation of renewable energy in microgrids imposes severe multi-time-scale power fluctuations on hybrid energy storage systems (HESS), demanding dispatch strategies that jointly optimise short-term economy and long-term battery health. Existing approaches, however, suffer from two critical limitations: (1) conventional day-ahead schedulers treat the battery as an ideal lossless device, systematically neglecting the irreversible capacity degradation driven by cycling depth and electrochemical stress; (2) physics-based aging models are inherently non-convex and time-coupled, making them intractable for direct embedding in mixed-integer linear programmes without principled linearisation. To overcome these limitations, we propose DL-HESS (Dual-dimensional Lifetime-aware scheduling for Hybrid Energy Storage Systems), a day-ahead optimisation framework for HESS microgrids. Our contributions are twofold: (1) a dual-dimensional battery lifetime model that combines a linearised electrochemical soft cost with a dynamic depth-of-discharge-weighted equivalent full-cycle hard constraint, enabling simultaneous economy and durability optimisation within a MILP; (2) a three-step analytical linearisation procedure that reduces the semi-empirical aging rate to a convex linear function of battery current, with a certified relative error below 2% across the practical operating C-rate range. Experiments on a representative 20MWh industrial-park microgrid demonstrate that the proposed framework significantly extends battery service life while maintaining full operational availability, outperforming four competitive baseline strategies across economy, degradation, and reliability metrics.

title: Event-Trace Cohesion Metrics for Live Co-Authoring Streams
authors: Pratik Dhameliya
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Collaborative writing platforms generate extensive telemetry data, yet real-time analysis of co-authoring behavior remains challenging. This paper presents an event-trace cohesion framework that transforms high-frequency edit logs into interpretable collaboration metrics through incremental stream processing. The system parses raw editor logs into ElementaryOperations, aggregates them into author-level Operations, and maintains dynamic document structure through Paragraph and SuperParagraph tracking. A multi-stage pipeline computes real-time metrics including participation entropy, synchronous editing ratios, operation-type distributions, and semantic similarity trends using lightweight sentence embeddings. Evaluated on 45 collaborative writing sessions from university students (320,000+ ElementaryOperations), the architecture processes 1,200+ events/second with sub-100ms latency, enabling live dashboard visualization without full-history reprocessing. Key findings demonstrate that semantic similarity slopes correlate with expert-rated collaboration quality (r =0.38), participation entropy shows moderate correlation (r =0.42) with smooth collaboration, and window-based metrics capture transient patterns invisible to full-history analysis. The framework’s parameterized thresholds and incremental design support heterogeneous deployment scenarios across educational technology and collaborative systems. This work contributes an implementation-ready analytics pipeline that converts document telemetry into actionable insights for real-time monitoring, comparative analysis, and temporal trend discovery in co-authoring environments.

title: Vision-Driven Architecture for Translating, Segmenting and Interpreting Radar-based Satellite Imagery
authors: Naveen Karthik R, Varun Kumar G S, Ilamurgan G
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Synthetic aperture radar, or SAR, images allow us to visualize the Earth even when there are clouds or without sufficient lighting. However, its effectiveness is often marred by speckle noise and difficulties in interpreting backscatters. This paper proposes a three-stage deep learning process to automatically transform SAR images into a format that is easily understandable by non-experts. The process involves transforming SAR images into RGB images using a conditional GAN, then applying a semantic segmentation technique to identify specific geographic features, and lastly using an attention-based encoder-decoder model to generate a concise description of a scene. The key is to combine these three techniques into a single process, with each technique acting on the data individually. The process runs on a single GPU device and produces three final products.

title: Identifying the Optimal Machine Learning Framework for Landslide Susceptibility in Chongqing: A Multi-Model Comparative Study
authors: Mingming Han
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: The spatial heterogeneity and complex environmental driving mechanisms of landslide hazards in Chongqing remain unclear. This paper quantitatively investigates the spatial distribution patterns and influencing factors using landslide observation data recorded between 2000 and 2020 and multi-source environmental factors. Landslide susceptibility mapping reveals that the hazard points exhibit a significant “striplike” clustering pattern, primarily concentrated in the valleys of the Yangtze River and its tributaries within the core section of the Three Gorges Reservoir Area. Among the four evaluated machine learning models, CatBoost was selected as the optimal model (test set <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{AUC}=0.796$</tex>). While other models such as Random Forest <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{AUC} =0.792)$</tex>, LightGBM <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{AUC} =0.798)$</tex> and XGBoost <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{AUC} =0.793)$</tex> showed comparable predictive accuracy, CatBoost demonstrated superior generalization ability with the lowest risk of overfitting. To overcome the “black box” limitation of machine learning, the Shapley Additive Explanations (SHAP) method was employed for the in-depth decoding of the model. The results indicate that elevation (DEM) and slope are the dominant driving factors controlling landslide distribution, with hazard points predominantly located in low-altitude valley areas (below 500 m) and slope zones exceeding 10°. Furthermore, dependency analysis of SHAP values reveals a significant non-linear response: the interbedded sandstone and mudstone in the stratigraphic lithology contribute strongly and positively to landslide occurrence, while high vegetation coverage (NDVI) exhibits a clear inhibitory effect. This study provides a new interpretable framework for regional geological hazard prediction and serves as a scientific reference for formulating differentiated disaster prevention and mitigation strategies in Chongqing.

title: Multiband, Low Profile Cross Slotted Antenna for Body Centric Wireless Communication
authors: DK Kavitha, N. Durga Parameswara rao, Jeevana Kotte, Gera Anupama, U Manjula, M Chiranjeevi
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: The presented paper describes a compact low-profile cross-slotted wearable button antenna with penta-band performance. This antenna is based on a low-cost FR-4 substrate which has a size of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$35 \times 35 \times 1.6 \text{mm} 3$</tex> and uses an inset-fed rectangular patch with a central cross shaped slot, four corner slots and partial ground plane to enable easy wide impedance bandwidth and stable radiation pattern. The optimized design has resonances at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.88,3.58,5.66,7.5$</tex> and 9.3 GHz, which is useful in several wireless standards, 5 G sub-6 GHz (n77/n78/n46), Wifi 5/6E, WiMAX, S-band and X-band radar. The antenna shows a maximum gain ranging from 2.77 dB to 8.07 dB. and almost bidirectional radiation pattern, ensuring reliable on-body communication performance. On-body analysis using a multilayer human tissue model demonstrates minimal detuning and stable performance with low electromagnetic coupling. The values of Specific Absorption Rate (SAR) obtained are less than of international safety limits, which proves the capability of antenna in safe wearability. The proposed design offers an efficient, compact and broadband. Wireless communication solution based on modern body-centric systems.

title: Prediction of the Evolution of the Groundwater Flow Field After the Karst Groundwater System in the Dome Structure Area Is Blocked
authors: Jinghui Wu, Tongzhe Liu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: Jinling Mining Area is located in Zibo City, Shandong Province, and it is an important iron ore resource base in Shandong Province. Its geological structure is controlled by regional dome structures. To effectively address the frequent water gushing problems during the mining production process, the curtain water-stop wall technology was adopted in engineering practice to seal the karst aquifer. However, the groundwater system dominated by the dome structure has significant structural water-controlling characteristics. After sealing the karst water, it will trigger a re-adjustment of the regional underground flow field, which may affect the long-term stability and effectiveness of the water prevention and control projects. Therefore, this study constructed a three-dimensional numerical model of groundwater in the mining area based on the FEFLOW software. The model was verified through parameter identification and multi-source observation data. Then, using the validated model, the simulation and prediction of the underground flow field response characteristics under different hydrological years under the curtain interception condition were carried out. The simulation results show that in the continuous wet year scenario, the groundwater level in the upstream area of the curtain wall significantly rises; the water level in the downstream area remains generally stable; under the continuous normal year condition, the water level in most sections shows a slow upward trend; while in the continuous dry year scenario, the water level throughout the area generally decreases, and the decline in the downstream area of the curtain wall is more significant. The above flow field evolution patterns can provide quantitative basis for the scientific design, risk assessment, and dynamic regulation of the water prevention and control projects in the mining area.

title: Based on the 3D Point Smart Recognizion of Buildings Structural Facades and Quantity its Damages*
authors: Jiayu Zhang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: The traditional building structure surface damage detection technology has the problems of low efficiency, low precision and great dependence on artificial experience. In this paper, an intelligent damage identification and evaluation method based on 3D point cloud is proposed to solve the current technical problems. The high-precision point cloud data of the target object is obtained by 3D laser scanning, and the optimized RandLA-Net neural network is used for preprocessing and dimensionality reduction, which greatly improves the data processing speed. Furthermore, a deep learning model based on dynamic graph convolution network and attention mechanism is established to analyze the common damage types such as cracks, spalling and corrosion from the aspects of curvature characteristics and normal information. Considering the differences between the actual structure and the ideal geometric model, a multi-dimensional damage quantitative evaluation index system is formed to achieve the purpose of accurate grading. From the experimental results, it can be seen that the proposed method is better than the traditional detection method in terms of positioning accuracy and measurement accuracy. The results provide important support for the innovation and development of building safety monitoring technology, and have broad prospects.

title: Enhanced Alpha-Beta Pruning and Evaluation Function Optimization for Pac-Man AI Agents
authors: Yuvraj Deshmukh, Shweta Malwe, Prachi Mehta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: This paper presents an enhanced Alpha-Beta Pac-Man agent made for the Berkeley Pac-Man project. The agent employs an improved evaluation function, reactive behavioral rules that enable Pac-Man escape danger or quickly chase scared ghosts, and a memory system that stops Pac-Man from getting stuck going back and forth. Testing was done on two maze layouts, medium and tricky, and compared it with the normal Alpha-Beta agent. The results showed that the agent played more smoothly, kept scores steady, and used far fewer node expansions. Overall, it cut node expansions by about 34% while still keeping good scores. This shows that better decision-making and pruning can greatly improve Pac-Man in fast and changing mazes.

title: Smart Healthcare: Real-Time Patient Monitoring and Emergency Response Systems for Smart Cities
authors: Pooja Gupta, Sonal Wadhwa, Sangeeta Chaudhary, Ashima Arya, Abhinav Juneja, Niranjan Devanathan R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: The research paper explores the effects of the adoption of Internet of Things (IoT) on healthcare service effectiveness within the NCR Delhi area concentrating on four major determinants, which are system reliability, perceived ease of use, data security and privacy, and real-time responsiveness. The structured questionnaire was used to collect data, and the data was analyzed by the Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM). It was shown that the measurement model had good reliability, convergent and discriminant validity, and could be used to analyse the structure. Findings show that data security & privacy are the factors that have the greatest impact on effectiveness of healthcare services, then perceived ease of use, system reliability, and real-time responsiveness. The results indicate that the confidence of patients in having safe data management, as well as convenient and trustworthy platforms are the key to successful IoT-mediated healthcare provision. Although real-time responsiveness will also have a positive effect, its effect is not as significant in this situation. The study can be helpful to healthcare practitioners, technology developers, and policymakers who need to improve the concept of patient-centered care by using efficient, secure, and responsive IoT solutions.

title: Deep-Tissue Hydration Assessment Strategies: A Comparison of the RF-Based Solutions
authors: Alessandra Di Florio Di Renzo, Simone Trovarello, Oumaima Afif, Simona Di Meo, Diego Masotti, Marco Pasian, Marco Tartagni, Alessandra Costanzo
publish: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)
abstract: Accurate assessment of muscle hydration is essential in clinical practice, athletic performance, and rehabilitation, as changes in intramuscular water content directly affect physiological function and performance. Bioelectrical Impedance Analysis (BIA) is widely used for hydration evaluation, but its capability to characterize deep muscle hydration is limited by poor spatial selectivity and strong sensitivity to superficial tissues. Microwavebased electromagnetic (EM) sensing has recently emerged as a promising alternative, exploiting the correlation between tissue dielectric properties and water content to enable localized interrogation. However, the multilayer structure of biological tissues complicates spectral interpretation, as superficial layers significantly influence the measured response. This paper overviews current non-invasive hydration assessment approaches, highlighting the limitations of conventional and predictive BIA techniques and the potential of microwave-based sensing combined with multivariate predictive modeling to achieve accurate and localized intramuscular hydration monitoring.

title: Toward a Soft Robotic Gripper Integrating High Force Grasping and Dexterous Manipulation: The SOFRo Gripper_supp5-3693142.mp4
authors: Dongjun Shin
abstract: Developing a robotic hand that integrates high fingertip force, rapid response, and multi-degree-of-freedom (DoF) motion to achieve human hand-like capabilities remains a challenge in the field of robotic hands. This study presents the SOFRo Gripper, a soft, high-force robotic gripper designed to combine these capabilities within a compact pneumatic architecture. The finger module of the SOFRo gripper incorporates synergistically arranged chambers together with a multinode tendon routing strategy that distributes actuation forces, enabling both flexion and abduction/adduction motions while enhancing fingertip force. Furthermore, a Force-Enhanced Pleated (FEP) mechanism was applied to the chambers to increase fingertip force, resulting in a 32.41% improvement over conventional chamber designs. The proposed SOFRo gripper achieves a high fingertip force of 68.76 N and dexterous motion capabilities, enabling a maximum lifting force of 400.0 N and in-hand manipulation. To validate its versatility, extensive experiments were conducted across a wide range of tasks, including object grasping, high-force manipulation (e.g., lifting heavy objects, closing a valve), delicate handling (e.g., grasping tofu, inserting a light bulb), and high-speed manipulation (e.g., spinning a top, catching a ball). These results demonstrate the SOFRo Gripper’s ability to combine high force with versatile soft robotic manipulation.

title: Compressing for Compression with the XOR Cache
authors: Zhewen Pan, Joshua San Miguel
abstract: Modern processors dedicate a large share of their resources to caching, especially to the last-level cache (LLC). We observe that today’s memory hierarchies contain untapped compression potential in the redundancy created by private caching and inclusion. To exploit it, we introduce XOR Cache, a new LLC architecture based on XOR compression. Instead of storing cache lines as-is, XOR Cache stores the bitwise XOR of line pairs, halving the number of stored lines through a form of inter-line compression. When paired with existing compression schemes, XOR Cache also boosts their intra-line compression ratios: XORing similar lines lowers the entropy of the data before it is compressed. Our evaluation shows that XOR Cache reduces LLC area by 1.93× and power by 1.92×, with only a 2.06% performance overhead compared to a larger uncompressed cache.

title: LLM-Driven Collaborative Agents for Wireless Network Management
authors: Yongchao Zhang, Jia Hu, Geyong Min
abstract: The management of next-generation wireless networks faces unprecedented difficulties driven by complex heterogeneous architectures, exponential device growth, higher frequency bands vulnerable to blockages, dynamic traffic fluctuations, and ever-evolving service requirements. While Large Language Model (LLM)-driven agentic AI has emerged as a transformative paradigm for autonomous network management, effective collaboration among distributed LLM agents remains a fundamental challenge. Joint training or fine-tuning of multiple LLMs is computationally prohibitive, while message-passing-based coordination incurs substantial communication overhead and privacy concerns. To address these issues, this article presents a decentralized collaboration framework that enables distributed LLM agents to coordinate effectively via reward sharing, where agents share portions of their local rewards with neighbors to encourage cooperative policies that align with system-wide performance goals. Each agent employs deep reinforcement learning to dynamically optimize sharing weights based on local network observations and historical interaction outcomes, without requiring extensive inter-agent communication. Experimental evaluation on distributed edge computing scenarios demonstrates that the proposed framework achieves stable learning and facilitates effective multi-agent collaboration in reducing system costs.

title: Comprehensive Deep Learning Framework for Indoor Localization with BLE RSSI in Dynamic Environments
authors: Yeonjeong Gong, Kiwon Jung, Yeeun Kwon, Taekyung Sung, Minseuk Kim
abstract: The Received Signal Strength Indicator (RSSI) of low-cost and low-power Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE), which offers high infrastructure accessibility through the ubiquitous support of mobile devices, has been widely investigated in the field of indoor localization. Although the RSSI fingerprinting-based localization is a promising alternative in Global Positioning System (GPS)-denied environments, its performance in dynamic environments is degraded by signal loss and multipath fading. To address these limitations and enhance localization accuracy, this paper presents a comprehensive localization framework that integrates data interpolation with the Multiple Imputation by Chained Equations (MICE) method, deep learning feature extension using a special Classification (CLS) token-based Transformer fed with Differential Received Signal Strength (DRSS) features, and outlier mitigation via novel filtering. Experimental results on a dynamic tunnel driving dataset demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a mean localization error of 2.376 ± 0.194 m, outperforming the 1-d Kalman + Random Forest (RF) baseline (3.206 ± 0.173 m), corresponding to an improvement of approximately 25% under the same evaluation protocol, reducing the error by 0.83 m. These results highlight the effectiveness of the proposed framework for robust BLE-based localization in highly dynamic, GPS-denied environments, particularly in vehicular scenarios.
